Cultural Heritage Online (Agency for Cultural Affairs) * [http://www.city.setagaya.lg.jp/kurashi/107/159/778/h26/d00135671.html Experience Ancient Life at Noge Ōtsuka Kofun Festival] - Setagaya City website {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Kofun in Tokyo]] [[Category:History of Setagaya]] [[Category:Scallop-shaped kofun]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Tokyo]] [[Category:Tokyo Designated Historic Sites]] [[category:original pages]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Noge Otsuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Noge Otsuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Noge Otsuka Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Noge Otsuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Noge Otsuka Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Noge Otsuka Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Noge Otsuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:attempting to force refresh]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Noge Otsuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Noge Otsuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Noge Otsuka Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Noge Otsuka Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Noge Otsuka Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[ja:野毛大塚古墳]]

{{Kofun in Japan | name=Noge Ōtsuka Kofun | Alias=Higashi Ōtsuka, Kaminoge Kofun, etc. | image=[[File:Noge Otsuka Kofun -01.jpg|260px]]
Noge Ōtsuka Kofun (April 10, 2010) | location=Noge 1-chome, {{ill|Setagaya, Tokyo|ja|世田谷区}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}} | Position={{coord|35|36|18.1|N|139|38|35.1|E|}} | Group={{ill|Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|ja|荏原台古墳群}} | Shape=Scallop-shaped kofun | ConstructionEra=Early 5th century | BurialFacility=Main chambers 1–3: split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffins in clay coffins
Chamber 4: box-shaped stone coffin | Dimensions=82 m in length; posterior circular mound diameter 67 m; height 11 m | BuriedPerson=Unknown | ExcavatedArtifacts=Short armor, helmet, gorgets, shoulder guards, iron swords (19), iron arrowheads (25+), iron knives, iron sickles, {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡}}, bronze armlets, 19 stone replicas, over 30 combs, over 2,000 beads, and a quiver or arrow case | HistoricSite={{ill|Tokyo Designated Historic Site|ja|東京都指定史跡}}: "Noge Ōtsuka Kofun" | TangibleCulturalProperties=Excavated artifacts (Important Cultural Property of Japan) | SpecialNotes= | Map2={{location map|Japan Wards of Tokyo|label=Noge
Ōtsuka Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=36|lat_sec=18.1|lon_deg=139|lon_min=38|lon_sec=35.1|mark=帆立貝形古墳-南西.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Tokyo}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|36|18.1|N|139|38|35.1|E}}}} }} '''Noge Ōtsuka Kofun''' (野毛大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Setagaya, Tokyo|ja|世田谷区}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}}. It is scallop-shaped and designated as a {{ill|Tokyo Designated Historic Site|ja|東京都指定史跡}}. Artifacts excavated from the tumulus are designated as Important Cultural Property of Japan. == location == Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is situated in {{ill|Tamagawa Nogemachi Park|ja|玉川野毛町公園}}, within the {{ill|Musashino Plateau|ja|武蔵野台地}} at an elevation of approximately 30–33 meters.{{Cite book|和書|author=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「城南の遺跡」-世田谷周辺発掘調査最新報告- |publisher=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1993|pages=2-3}} Known since the late Edo period, it was historically referred to as "Higashi Ōtsuka." It has also been called "Kaminoge Kofun" and "Todoroki Golf Links Kofun." The tumulus is part of the {{ill|Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|ja|荏原台古墳群}} and represents the region's ancient burial practices. == Overview == At 82 meters in length, with a diameter of 67 meters and a height of 11 meters, Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is one of Japan's largest scallop-shaped kofun (hotategai-shiki kofun). An excavation conducted in 1897 (Meiji 30) revealed a stone coffin, and the artifacts were transferred to the Imperial Museum (now {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}). The mound is equipped with a stone covering (fukishi) and haniwa, and a horseshoe-shaped moat surrounds it.{{Cite book|和書|author1=世田谷区教育委員会|authorlink1=世田谷区教育委員会|author2=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「世田谷の三万年」-最近の発掘調査の成果から- |publisher=世田谷区教育委員会・世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1988|page=16}} == Burial Chambers == Four burial chambers are located in the posterior circular mound. The first chamber's split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin measures 8.2 meters in length and contains numerous grave goods, including: * Weapons and armor: short armor, helmets, gorgets, shoulder guards, 19 iron swords, and 25+ arrowheads * Mirrors and ornaments: {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡}}, bronze armlets, over 2,000 beads, and over 30 combs * Stone replicas: including axes and ceremonial tools Artifacts from the box-shaped stone coffin include magatama, tubular beads, iron weapons, armor fragments, and a wide variety of stone replicas, such as bowls, plates, and knives made from steatite. == Cultural Significance == The kofun is recognized as an archaeological benchmark for understanding 5th-century burial practices. Detailed findings were compiled by {{ill|Morikazu Gotō|ja|後藤守一}} in 1936. == Designations == * Important Cultural Property of Japan (Excavated artifacts): The artifacts are preserved at the {{ill|Setagaya Local History Museum|ja|世田谷区立郷土資料館}} and the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. * Tokyo Designated Historic Site: Designated as a Historic Site on February 6, 1975.{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.syougai.metro.tokyo.jp/iseki0/iseki/list/ruins/13112/112ruins.htm |title=世田谷区-遺跡一覧 東京都遺跡地図情報インターネット提供サービス |publisher=[[東京都教育委員会]] |accessdate=2016-07-03}} == Related Information == * The landscape stamp of {{ill|Setagaya Noge Post Office|ja|世田谷野毛郵便局}} features Noge Ōtsuka Kofun.[https://www.post.japanpost.jp/kitte_hagaki/stamp/fuke/detail.php?id=468 Landscape Stamp: Setagaya Noge Post Office] (Japan Post). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Noge-Otsuka Kofun}} * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/00011732 Excavated Artifacts of Noge Ōtsuka Kofun (Setagaya City Ownership)] - Cultural Heritage Online (Agency for Cultural Affairs) * [http://www.city.setagaya.lg.jp/kurashi/107/159/778/h26/d00135671.html Experience Ancient Life at Noge Ōtsuka Kofun Festival] - Setagaya City website {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Kofun in Tokyo]] [[Category:History of Setagaya]] [[Category:Scallop-shaped kofun]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Tokyo]] [[Category:Tokyo Designated Historic Sites]] [[category:original pages]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Noge Otsuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Noge Otsuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Noge Otsuka Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Noge Otsuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:original pages]][[category:Noge Otsuka Kofun]][[category:original pages]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Noge Otsuka Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Noge Otsuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:attempting to force refresh]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Noge Otsuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Noge Otsuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Noge Otsuka Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Noge Otsuka Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Noge Otsuka Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[ja:野毛大塚古墳]]

Cultural Heritage Online (Agency for Cultural Affairs) * [http://www.city.setagaya.lg.jp/kurashi/107/159/778/h26/d00135671.html Experience Ancient Life at Noge Ōtsuka Kofun Festival] - Setagaya City website {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Kofun in Tokyo]] [[Category:History of Setagaya]] [[Category:Scallop-shaped kofun]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Tokyo]] [[Category:Tokyo Designated Historic Sites]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Noge Otsuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Noge Otsuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Noge Otsuka Kofun]][[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Noge Otsuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Noge Otsuka Kofun]][[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Noge Otsuka Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Noge Otsuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:attempting to force refresh]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Noge Otsuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Noge Otsuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Noge Otsuka Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Noge Otsuka Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Noge Otsuka Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[ja:野毛大塚古墳]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name=Noge Ōtsuka Kofun | Alias=Higashi Ōtsuka, Kaminoge Kofun, etc. | image=[[File:Noge Otsuka Kofun -01.jpg|260px]]
Noge Ōtsuka Kofun (April 10, 2010) | location=Noge 1-chome, {{ill|Setagaya, Tokyo|ja|世田谷区}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}} | Position={{coord|35|36|18.1|N|139|38|35.1|E|}} | Group={{ill|Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|ja|荏原台古墳群}} | Shape=Scallop-shaped kofun | ConstructionEra=Early 5th century | BurialFacility=Main chambers 1–3: split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffins in clay coffins
Chamber 4: box-shaped stone coffin | Dimensions=82 m in length; posterior circular mound diameter 67 m; height 11 m | BuriedPerson=Unknown | ExcavatedArtifacts=Short armor, helmet, gorgets, shoulder guards, iron swords (19), iron arrowheads (25+), iron knives, iron sickles, {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡}}, bronze armlets, 19 stone replicas, over 30 combs, over 2,000 beads, and a quiver or arrow case | HistoricSite={{ill|Tokyo Designated Historic Site|ja|東京都指定史跡}}: "Noge Ōtsuka Kofun" | TangibleCulturalProperties=Excavated artifacts (Important Cultural Property of Japan) | SpecialNotes= | Map2={{location map|Japan Wards of Tokyo|label=Noge
Ōtsuka Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=36|lat_sec=18.1|lon_deg=139|lon_min=38|lon_sec=35.1|mark=帆立貝形古墳-南西.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Tokyo}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|36|18.1|N|139|38|35.1|E}}}} }} '''Noge Ōtsuka Kofun''' (野毛大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Setagaya, Tokyo|ja|世田谷区}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}}. It is scallop-shaped and designated as a {{ill|Tokyo Designated Historic Site|ja|東京都指定史跡}}. Artifacts excavated from the tumulus are designated as Important Cultural Property of Japan. == location == Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is situated in {{ill|Tamagawa Nogemachi Park|ja|玉川野毛町公園}}, within the {{ill|Musashino Plateau|ja|武蔵野台地}} at an elevation of approximately 30–33 meters.{{Cite book|和書|author=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「城南の遺跡」-世田谷周辺発掘調査最新報告- |publisher=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1993|pages=2-3}} Known since the late Edo period, it was historically referred to as "Higashi Ōtsuka." It has also been called "Kaminoge Kofun" and "Todoroki Golf Links Kofun." The tumulus is part of the {{ill|Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|ja|荏原台古墳群}} and represents the region's ancient burial practices. == Overview == At 82 meters in length, with a diameter of 67 meters and a height of 11 meters, Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is one of Japan's largest scallop-shaped kofun (hotategai-shiki kofun). An excavation conducted in 1897 (Meiji 30) revealed a stone coffin, and the artifacts were transferred to the Imperial Museum (now {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}). The mound is equipped with a stone covering (fukishi) and haniwa, and a horseshoe-shaped moat surrounds it.{{Cite book|和書|author1=世田谷区教育委員会|authorlink1=世田谷区教育委員会|author2=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「世田谷の三万年」-最近の発掘調査の成果から- |publisher=世田谷区教育委員会・世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1988|page=16}} == Burial Chambers == Four burial chambers are located in the posterior circular mound. The first chamber's split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin measures 8.2 meters in length and contains numerous grave goods, including: * Weapons and armor: short armor, helmets, gorgets, shoulder guards, 19 iron swords, and 25+ arrowheads * Mirrors and ornaments: {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡}}, bronze armlets, over 2,000 beads, and over 30 combs * Stone replicas: including axes and ceremonial tools Artifacts from the box-shaped stone coffin include magatama, tubular beads, iron weapons, armor fragments, and a wide variety of stone replicas, such as bowls, plates, and knives made from steatite. == Cultural Significance == The kofun is recognized as an archaeological benchmark for understanding 5th-century burial practices. Detailed findings were compiled by {{ill|Morikazu Gotō|ja|後藤守一}} in 1936. == Designations == * Important Cultural Property of Japan (Excavated artifacts): The artifacts are preserved at the {{ill|Setagaya Local History Museum|ja|世田谷区立郷土資料館}} and the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. * Tokyo Designated Historic Site: Designated as a Historic Site on February 6, 1975.{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.syougai.metro.tokyo.jp/iseki0/iseki/list/ruins/13112/112ruins.htm |title=世田谷区-遺跡一覧 東京都遺跡地図情報インターネット提供サービス |publisher=[[東京都教育委員会]] |accessdate=2016-07-03}} == Related Information == * The landscape stamp of {{ill|Setagaya Noge Post Office|ja|世田谷野毛郵便局}} features Noge Ōtsuka Kofun.[https://www.post.japanpost.jp/kitte_hagaki/stamp/fuke/detail.php?id=468 Landscape Stamp: Setagaya Noge Post Office] (Japan Post). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Noge-Otsuka Kofun}} * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/00011732 Excavated Artifacts of Noge Ōtsuka Kofun (Setagaya City Ownership)] - Cultural Heritage Online (Agency for Cultural Affairs) * [http://www.city.setagaya.lg.jp/kurashi/107/159/778/h26/d00135671.html Experience Ancient Life at Noge Ōtsuka Kofun Festival] - Setagaya City website {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Kofun in Tokyo]] [[Category:History of Setagaya]] [[Category:Scallop-shaped kofun]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Tokyo]] [[Category:Tokyo Designated Historic Sites]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Noge Otsuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Noge Otsuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Noge Otsuka Kofun]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Noge Otsuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Noge Otsuka Kofun]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Noge Otsuka Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Noge Otsuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:attempting to force refresh]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Noge Otsuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Noge Otsuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Noge Otsuka Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Noge Otsuka Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Noge Otsuka Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[ja:野毛大塚古墳]]

Cultural Heritage Online (Agency for Cultural Affairs) * [http://www.city.setagaya.lg.jp/kurashi/107/159/778/h26/d00135671.html Experience Ancient Life at Noge Ōtsuka Kofun Festival] - Setagaya City website {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Kofun in Tokyo]] [[Category:History of Setagaya]] [[Category:Scallop-shaped kofun]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Tokyo]] [[Category:Tokyo Designated Historic Sites]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Noge Otsuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Noge Otsuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Noge Otsuka Kofun]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Noge Otsuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Noge Otsuka Kofun]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Noge Otsuka Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Noge Otsuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:attempting to force refresh]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Noge Otsuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Noge Otsuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Noge Otsuka Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Noge Otsuka Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[Category:kk]][[Category:Noge Otsuka Kofun]][[Category:kk]] [[ja:野毛大塚古墳]]

{{Infobox ancient site | name=Noge Ōtsuka Kofun | Alias=Higashi Ōtsuka, Kaminoge Kofun, etc. | image=[[File:Noge Otsuka Kofun -01.jpg|260px]]
Noge Ōtsuka Kofun (April 10, 2010) | location=Noge 1-chome, {{ill|Setagaya, Tokyo|ja|世田谷区}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}} | Position={{coord|35|36|18.1|N|139|38|35.1|E|}} | Group={{ill|Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|ja|荏原台古墳群}} | Shape=Scallop-shaped kofun | ConstructionEra=Early 5th century | BurialFacility=Main chambers 1–3: split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffins in clay coffins
Chamber 4: box-shaped stone coffin | Dimensions=82 m in length; posterior circular mound diameter 67 m; height 11 m | BuriedPerson=Unknown | ExcavatedArtifacts=Short armor, helmet, gorgets, shoulder guards, iron swords (19), iron arrowheads (25+), iron knives, iron sickles, {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡}}, bronze armlets, 19 stone replicas, over 30 combs, over 2,000 beads, and a quiver or arrow case | HistoricSite={{ill|Tokyo Designated Historic Site|ja|東京都指定史跡}}: "Noge Ōtsuka Kofun" | TangibleCulturalProperties=Excavated artifacts (Important Cultural Property of Japan) | SpecialNotes= | Map2={{location map|Japan Wards of Tokyo|label=Noge
Ōtsuka Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=36|lat_sec=18.1|lon_deg=139|lon_min=38|lon_sec=35.1|mark=帆立貝形古墳-南西.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Tokyo}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|36|18.1|N|139|38|35.1|E}}}} }} '''Noge Ōtsuka Kofun''' (野毛大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Setagaya, Tokyo|ja|世田谷区}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}}. It is scallop-shaped and designated as a {{ill|Tokyo Designated Historic Site|ja|東京都指定史跡}}. Artifacts excavated from the tumulus are designated as Important Cultural Property of Japan. == location == Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is situated in {{ill|Tamagawa Nogemachi Park|ja|玉川野毛町公園}}, within the {{ill|Musashino Plateau|ja|武蔵野台地}} at an elevation of approximately 30–33 meters.{{Cite book|和書|author=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「城南の遺跡」-世田谷周辺発掘調査最新報告- |publisher=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1993|pages=2-3}} Known since the late Edo period, it was historically referred to as "Higashi Ōtsuka." It has also been called "Kaminoge Kofun" and "Todoroki Golf Links Kofun." The tumulus is part of the {{ill|Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|ja|荏原台古墳群}} and represents the region's ancient burial practices. == Overview == At 82 meters in length, with a diameter of 67 meters and a height of 11 meters, Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is one of Japan's largest scallop-shaped kofun (hotategai-shiki kofun). An excavation conducted in 1897 (Meiji 30) revealed a stone coffin, and the artifacts were transferred to the Imperial Museum (now {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}). The mound is equipped with a stone covering (fukishi) and haniwa, and a horseshoe-shaped moat surrounds it.{{Cite book|和書|author1=世田谷区教育委員会|authorlink1=世田谷区教育委員会|author2=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「世田谷の三万年」-最近の発掘調査の成果から- |publisher=世田谷区教育委員会・世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1988|page=16}} == Burial Chambers == Four burial chambers are located in the posterior circular mound. The first chamber's split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin measures 8.2 meters in length and contains numerous grave goods, including: * Weapons and armor: short armor, helmets, gorgets, shoulder guards, 19 iron swords, and 25+ arrowheads * Mirrors and ornaments: {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡}}, bronze armlets, over 2,000 beads, and over 30 combs * Stone replicas: including axes and ceremonial tools Artifacts from the box-shaped stone coffin include magatama, tubular beads, iron weapons, armor fragments, and a wide variety of stone replicas, such as bowls, plates, and knives made from steatite. == Cultural Significance == The kofun is recognized as an archaeological benchmark for understanding 5th-century burial practices. Detailed findings were compiled by {{ill|Morikazu Gotō|ja|後藤守一}} in 1936. == Designations == * Important Cultural Property of Japan (Excavated artifacts): The artifacts are preserved at the {{ill|Setagaya Local History Museum|ja|世田谷区立郷土資料館}} and the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. * Tokyo Designated Historic Site: Designated as a Historic Site on February 6, 1975.{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.syougai.metro.tokyo.jp/iseki0/iseki/list/ruins/13112/112ruins.htm |title=世田谷区-遺跡一覧 東京都遺跡地図情報インターネット提供サービス |publisher=[[東京都教育委員会]] |accessdate=2016-07-03}} == Related Information == * The landscape stamp of {{ill|Setagaya Noge Post Office|ja|世田谷野毛郵便局}} features Noge Ōtsuka Kofun.[https://www.post.japanpost.jp/kitte_hagaki/stamp/fuke/detail.php?id=468 Landscape Stamp: Setagaya Noge Post Office] (Japan Post). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Noge-Otsuka Kofun}} * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/00011732 Excavated Artifacts of Noge Ōtsuka Kofun (Setagaya City Ownership)] - Cultural Heritage Online (Agency for Cultural Affairs) * [http://www.city.setagaya.lg.jp/kurashi/107/159/778/h26/d00135671.html Experience Ancient Life at Noge Ōtsuka Kofun Festival] - Setagaya City website {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Kofun in Tokyo]] [[Category:History of Setagaya]] [[Category:Scallop-shaped kofun]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Tokyo]] [[Category:Tokyo Designated Historic Sites]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Noge Otsuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Noge Otsuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Noge Otsuka Kofun]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Noge Otsuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Noge Otsuka Kofun]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Noge Otsuka Kofun]][[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Noge Otsuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:attempting to force refresh]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Noge Otsuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Noge Otsuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:Noge Otsuka Kofun]][[Category:Noge Otsuka Kofun]][[Category:Noge Otsuka Kofun]][[ja:野毛大塚古墳]]

Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Tomatsuri Ōtsuka Kofun|ja|戸祭大塚古墳}} - Located in {{il..."

revision.text:
* Prefecture. * {{ill|Noge Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:野毛大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Setagaya|en}}[[ja:世田谷区]], Tokyo. * {{ill|Unoki Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:鵜木大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Ōta, Tokyo|en}}[[ja:大田区雪谷大塚町]], Tokyo. * {{ill|Miyazaki Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:宮崎大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Miyamae-ku, Kawasaki|en}}[[ja:川崎市宮前区宮崎]], Kanagawa Prefecture. * {{ill|Hirui Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:昼飯大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Ōgaki|en}}[[ja:大垣市]], Gifu Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:宗慶大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Motosu|en}}[[ja:本巣市]], Gifu Prefecture. * {{ill|Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:志段味大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|en}}[[ja:名古屋市守山区]], Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Toyota Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:豊田大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Toyota|en}}[[ja:豊田市]], Aichi Prefecture. Excavated {{ill|sue ware|en}}[[ja:須恵器]] designated as an important cultural property. * {{ill|Terado Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:寺戸大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Mukō|en}}[[ja:向日市]], Kyoto Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun (Toyonaka)|en}}[[ja:桜塚古墳群 (豊中市)#大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Toyonaka|en}}[[ja:豊中市]], Osaka Prefecture. * {{ill|Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:軽里大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Habikino|en}}[[ja:羽曳野市]], Osaka Prefecture. Officially designated by the {{ill|Imperial Household Agency|en}}[[ja:宮内庁]] as the tomb of {{ill|Yamato Takeru no Mikoto|en}}[[ja:日本武尊白鳥陵]]. * {{ill|Higashida Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:東田大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Sakurai, Nara|en}}[[ja:桜井市]], Nara Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Makimuku Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:纒向古墳群]]. * {{ill|Nakayama Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:中山大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Tenri|en}}[[ja:天理市]], Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Yanagimoto Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:柳本大塚古墳]] - Located in Tenri, Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Kuroda Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:黒田大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Tawaramoto|en}}[[ja:磯城郡田原本町]], Nara Prefecture. * {{ill|Musa Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:牟佐大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Okayama|en}}[[ja:岡山市]], Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Yada Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:箭田大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Kurashiki|en}}[[ja:倉敷市]], Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Imajuku Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:今宿大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:福岡市西区]], Fukuoka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Imajuku Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:今宿古墳群]]. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Tanushimaru Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:田主丸大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Tanushimaru, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:浮羽郡田主丸町]], Fukuoka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:田主丸古墳群]]. Designated a national historic site.
== Related Articles == * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun Cluster (disambiguation)|en}}[[ja:大塚古墳群 (曖昧さ回避)]]

Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Sōkei Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:宗慶大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Motosu|en}}[[ja:本巣市]], Gifu Prefecture. * {{ill|Shidami Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:志段味大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Moriyama-ku, Nagoya|en}}[[ja:名古屋市守山区]], Aichi Prefecture. * {{ill|Toyota Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:豊田大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Toyota|en}}[[ja:豊田市]], Aichi Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Ōtsuka Kofun (Toyonaka)|en}}[[ja:桜塚古墳群 (豊中市)#大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Toyonaka|en}}[[ja:豊中市]], Osaka Prefecture. * {{ill|Kariguri Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:軽里大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Habikino|en}}[[ja:羽曳野市]], Osaka Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Yata Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:箭田大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Kurashiki|en}}[[ja:倉敷市]], Okayama Prefecture. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Imajuku Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:今宿大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Nishi-ku, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:福岡市西区]], Fukuoka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Imajuku Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:今宿古墳群]]. Designated a national historic site. * {{ill|Tanushimaru Ōtsuka Kofun|en}}[[ja:田主丸大塚古墳]] - Located in {{ill|Tanushimaru, Fukuoka|en}}[[ja:浮羽郡田主丸町]], Fukuoka Prefecture. Part of the {{ill|Tanushimaru Kofun Cluster|en}}[[ja:田主丸古墳群]]. Designated a national historic site.

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[Noge Otsuka Kofun]] to [[Noge Ōtsuka Kofun]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name=Noge Ōtsuka Kofun | Alias=Higashi Ōtsuka, Kaminoge Kofun, etc. | image=[[File:Noge Otsuka Kofun -01.jpg|260px]]
Noge Ōtsuka Kofun (April 10, 2010) | location=Noge 1-chome, {{ill|Setagaya, Tokyo|ja|世田谷区}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}} | Position={{coord|35|36|18.1|N|139|38|35.1|E|}} | Group={{ill|Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|ja|荏原台古墳群}} | Shape=Scallop-shaped kofun | ConstructionEra=Early 5th century | BurialFacility=Main chambers 1–3: split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffins in clay coffins
Chamber 4: box-shaped stone coffin | Dimensions=82 m in length; posterior circular mound diameter 67 m; height 11 m | BuriedPerson=Unknown | ExcavatedArtifacts=Short armor, helmet, gorgets, shoulder guards, iron swords (19), iron arrowheads (25+), iron knives, iron sickles, {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡}}, bronze armlets, 19 stone replicas, over 30 combs, over 2,000 beads, and a quiver or arrow case | HistoricSite={{ill|Tokyo Designated Historic Site|ja|東京都指定史跡}}: "Noge Ōtsuka Kofun" | TangibleCulturalProperties=Excavated artifacts (Important Cultural Property of Japan) | SpecialNotes= | Map2={{location map|Japan Wards of Tokyo|label=Noge
Ōtsuka Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=36|lat_sec=18.1|lon_deg=139|lon_min=38|lon_sec=35.1|mark=帆立貝形古墳-南西.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Tokyo}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|36|18.1|N|139|38|35.1|E}}}} }} '''Noge Ōtsuka Kofun''' (野毛大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Setagaya, Tokyo|ja|世田谷区}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}}. It is scallop-shaped and designated as a {{ill|Tokyo Designated Historic Site|ja|東京都指定史跡}}. Artifacts excavated from the tumulus are designated as Important Cultural Property of Japan. == location == Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is situated in {{ill|Tamagawa Nogemachi Park|ja|玉川野毛町公園}}, within the {{ill|Musashino Plateau|ja|武蔵野台地}} at an elevation of approximately 30–33 meters.{{Cite book|和書|author=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「城南の遺跡」-世田谷周辺発掘調査最新報告- |publisher=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1993|pages=2-3}} Known since the late Edo period, it was historically referred to as "Higashi Ōtsuka." It has also been called "Kaminoge Kofun" and "Todoroki Golf Links Kofun." The tumulus is part of the {{ill|Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|ja|荏原台古墳群}} and represents the region's ancient burial practices. == Overview == At 82 meters in length, with a diameter of 67 meters and a height of 11 meters, Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is one of Japan's largest scallop-shaped kofun (hotategai-shiki kofun). An excavation conducted in 1897 (Meiji 30) revealed a stone coffin, and the artifacts were transferred to the Imperial Museum (now {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}). The mound is equipped with a stone covering (fukishi) and haniwa, and a horseshoe-shaped moat surrounds it.{{Cite book|和書|author1=世田谷区教育委員会|authorlink1=世田谷区教育委員会|author2=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「世田谷の三万年」-最近の発掘調査の成果から- |publisher=世田谷区教育委員会・世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1988|page=16}} == Burial Chambers == Four burial chambers are located in the posterior circular mound. The first chamber's split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin measures 8.2 meters in length and contains numerous grave goods, including: * Weapons and armor: short armor, helmets, gorgets, shoulder guards, 19 iron swords, and 25+ arrowheads * Mirrors and ornaments: {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡}}, bronze armlets, over 2,000 beads, and over 30 combs * Stone replicas: including axes and ceremonial tools Artifacts from the box-shaped stone coffin include magatama, tubular beads, iron weapons, armor fragments, and a wide variety of stone replicas, such as bowls, plates, and knives made from steatite. == Cultural Significance == The kofun is recognized as an archaeological benchmark for understanding 5th-century burial practices. Detailed findings were compiled by {{ill|Morikazu Gotō|ja|後藤守一}} in 1936. == Designations == * Important Cultural Property of Japan (Excavated artifacts): The artifacts are preserved at the {{ill|Setagaya Local History Museum|ja|世田谷区立郷土資料館}} and the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. * Tokyo Designated Historic Site: Designated as a Historic Site on February 6, 1975.{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.syougai.metro.tokyo.jp/iseki0/iseki/list/ruins/13112/112ruins.htm |title=世田谷区-遺跡一覧 東京都遺跡地図情報インターネット提供サービス |publisher=[[東京都教育委員会]] |accessdate=2016-07-03}} == Related Information == * The landscape stamp of {{ill|Setagaya Noge Post Office|ja|世田谷野毛郵便局}} features Noge Ōtsuka Kofun.[https://www.post.japanpost.jp/kitte_hagaki/stamp/fuke/detail.php?id=468 Landscape Stamp: Setagaya Noge Post Office] (Japan Post). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Noge-Otsuka Kofun}} * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/00011732 Excavated Artifacts of Noge Ōtsuka Kofun (Setagaya City Ownership)] - Cultural Heritage Online (Agency for Cultural Affairs) * [http://www.city.setagaya.lg.jp/kurashi/107/159/778/h26/d00135671.html Experience Ancient Life at Noge Ōtsuka Kofun Festival] - Setagaya City website {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Kofun in Tokyo]] [[Category:History of Setagaya]] [[Category:Scallop-shaped kofun]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Tokyo]] [[Category:Tokyo Designated Historic Sites]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Noge Otsuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Noge Otsuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Noge Otsuka Kofun]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Noge Otsuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[category:Noge Otsuka Kofun]][[Category:ZZZZZZZZZ Unlinked]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] [[Category:Noge Otsuka Kofun]][[Category:attempting to force efresh]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Noge Otsuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:attempting to force refresh]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Noge Otsuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:pp]][[category:Noge Otsuka Kofun]][[Category:pp]] [[Category:Noge Otsuka Kofun]][[Category:Noge Otsuka Kofun]][[Category:Noge Otsuka Kofun]][[ja:野毛大塚古墳]]

revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name=Noge Ōtsuka Kofun | Alias=Higashi Ōtsuka, Kaminoge Kofun, etc. | image=[[File:Noge Otsuka Kofun -01.jpg|260px]]
Noge Ōtsuka Kofun (April 10, 2010) | location=Noge 1-chome, {{ill|Setagaya, Tokyo|ja|世田谷区}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}} | Position={{coord|35|36|18.1|N|139|38|35.1|E|}} | Group={{ill|Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|ja|荏原台古墳群}} | Shape=Scallop-shaped kofun | ConstructionEra=Early 5th century | BurialFacility=Main chambers 1–3: split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffins in clay coffins
Chamber 4: box-shaped stone coffin | Dimensions=82 m in length; posterior circular mound diameter 67 m; height 11 m | BuriedPerson=Unknown | ExcavatedArtifacts=Short armor, helmet, gorgets, shoulder guards, iron swords (19), iron arrowheads (25+), iron knives, iron sickles, {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡}}, bronze armlets, 19 stone replicas, over 30 combs, over 2,000 beads, and a quiver or arrow case | HistoricSite={{ill|Tokyo Designated Historic Site|ja|東京都指定史跡}}: "Noge Ōtsuka Kofun" | TangibleCulturalProperties=Excavated artifacts (Important Cultural Property of Japan) | SpecialNotes= | Map2={{location map|Japan Wards of Tokyo|label=Noge
Ōtsuka Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=36|lat_sec=18.1|lon_deg=139|lon_min=38|lon_sec=35.1|mark=帆立貝形古墳-南西.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Tokyo}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|36|18.1|N|139|38|35.1|E}}}} }} '''Noge Ōtsuka Kofun''' (野毛大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Setagaya, Tokyo|ja|世田谷区}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}}. It is scallop-shaped and designated as a {{ill|Tokyo Designated Historic Site|ja|東京都指定史跡}}. Artifacts excavated from the tumulus are designated as Important Cultural Property of Japan. == location == Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is situated in {{ill|Tamagawa Nogemachi Park|ja|玉川野毛町公園}}, within the {{ill|Musashino Plateau|ja|武蔵野台地}} at an elevation of approximately 30–33 meters.{{Cite book|和書|author=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「城南の遺跡」-世田谷周辺発掘調査最新報告- |publisher=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1993|pages=2-3}} Known since the late Edo period, it was historically referred to as "Higashi Ōtsuka." It has also been called "Kaminoge Kofun" and "Todoroki Golf Links Kofun." The tumulus is part of the {{ill|Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|ja|荏原台古墳群}} and represents the region's ancient burial practices. == Overview == At 82 meters in length, with a diameter of 67 meters and a height of 11 meters, Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is one of Japan's largest scallop-shaped kofun (hotategai-shiki kofun). An excavation conducted in 1897 (Meiji 30) revealed a stone coffin, and the artifacts were transferred to the Imperial Museum (now {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}). The mound is equipped with a stone covering (fukishi) and haniwa, and a horseshoe-shaped moat surrounds it.{{Cite book|和書|author1=世田谷区教育委員会|authorlink1=世田谷区教育委員会|author2=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「世田谷の三万年」-最近の発掘調査の成果から- |publisher=世田谷区教育委員会・世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1988|page=16}} == Burial Chambers == Four burial chambers are located in the posterior circular mound. The first chamber's split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin measures 8.2 meters in length and contains numerous grave goods, including: * Weapons and armor: short armor, helmets, gorgets, shoulder guards, 19 iron swords, and 25+ arrowheads * Mirrors and ornaments: {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡}}, bronze armlets, over 2,000 beads, and over 30 combs * Stone replicas: including axes and ceremonial tools Artifacts from the box-shaped stone coffin include magatama, tubular beads, iron weapons, armor fragments, and a wide variety of stone replicas, such as bowls, plates, and knives made from steatite. == Cultural Significance == The kofun is recognized as an archaeological benchmark for understanding 5th-century burial practices. Detailed findings were compiled by {{ill|Morikazu Gotō|ja|後藤守一}} in 1936. == Designations == * Important Cultural Property of Japan (Excavated artifacts): The artifacts are preserved at the {{ill|Setagaya Local History Museum|ja|世田谷区立郷土資料館}} and the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. * Tokyo Designated Historic Site: Designated as a Historic Site on February 6, 1975.{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.syougai.metro.tokyo.jp/iseki0/iseki/list/ruins/13112/112ruins.htm |title=世田谷区-遺跡一覧 東京都遺跡地図情報インターネット提供サービス |publisher=[[東京都教育委員会]] |accessdate=2016-07-03}} == Related Information == * The landscape stamp of {{ill|Setagaya Noge Post Office|ja|世田谷野毛郵便局}} features Noge Ōtsuka Kofun.[https://www.post.japanpost.jp/kitte_hagaki/stamp/fuke/detail.php?id=468 Landscape Stamp: Setagaya Noge Post Office] (Japan Post). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Noge-Otsuka Kofun}} * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/00011732 Excavated Artifacts of Noge Ōtsuka Kofun (Setagaya City Ownership)] - Cultural Heritage Online (Agency for Cultural Affairs) * [http://www.city.setagaya.lg.jp/kurashi/107/159/778/h26/d00135671.html Experience Ancient Life at Noge Ōtsuka Kofun Festival] - Setagaya City website {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Kofun in Tokyo]] [[Category:History of Setagaya]] [[Category:Scallop-shaped kofun]] The first chamber's split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin measures 8.2 meters in length and contains numerous grave goods, including: * Weapons and armor: short armor, helmets, gorgets, shoulder guards, 19 iron swords, and 25+ arrowheads * Mirrors and ornaments: {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡}}, bronze armlets, over 2,000 beads, and over 30 combs * Stone replicas: including axes and ceremonial tools Artifacts from the box-shaped stone coffin include magatama, tubular beads, iron weapons, armor fragments, and a wide variety of stone replicas, such as bowls, plates, and knives made from steatite. == Cultural Significance == The kofun is recognized as an archaeological benchmark for understanding 5th-century burial practices. Detailed findings were compiled by {{ill|Morikazu Gotō|ja|後藤守一}} in 1936. == Designations == * Important Cultural Property of Japan (Excavated artifacts): The artifacts are preserved at the {{ill|Setagaya Local History Museum|ja|世田谷区立郷土資料館}} and the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. * Tokyo Designated Historic Site: Designated as a Historic Site on February 6, 1975.{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.syougai.metro.tokyo.jp/iseki0/iseki/list/ruins/13112/112ruins.htm |title=世田谷区-遺跡一覧 東京都遺跡地図情報インターネット提供サービス |publisher=[[東京都教育委員会]] |accessdate=2016-07-03}} == Related Information == * The landscape stamp of {{ill|Setagaya Noge Post Office|ja|世田谷野毛郵便局}} features Noge Ōtsuka Kofun.[https://www.post.japanpost.jp/kitte_hagaki/stamp/fuke/detail.php?id=468 Landscape Stamp: Setagaya Noge Post Office] (Japan Post). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Noge-Otsuka Kofun}} * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/00011732 Excavated Artifacts of Noge Ōtsuka Kofun (Setagaya City Ownership)] - Cultural Heritage Online (Agency for Cultural Affairs) * [http://www.city.setagaya.lg.jp/kurashi/107/159/778/h26/d00135671.html {{Infobox ancient site | name=Noge Ōtsuka Kofun | Alias=Higashi Ōtsuka, Kaminoge Kofun, etc. | image=[[File:Noge Otsuka Kofun -01.jpg|260px]]
Noge Ōtsuka Kofun (April 10, 2010) | location=Noge 1-chome, {{ill|Setagaya, Tokyo|ja|世田谷区}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}} | Position={{coord|35|36|18.1|N|139|38|35.1|E|}} | Group={{ill|Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|ja|荏原台古墳群}} | Shape=Scallop-shaped kofun | ConstructionEra=Early 5th century | BurialFacility=Main chambers 1–3: split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffins in clay coffins
Chamber 4: box-shaped stone coffin | Dimensions=82 m in length; posterior circular mound diameter 67 m; height 11 m | BuriedPerson=Unknown | ExcavatedArtifacts=Short armor, helmet, gorgets, shoulder guards, iron swords (19), iron arrowheads (25+), iron knives, iron sickles, {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡}}, bronze armlets, 19 stone replicas, over 30 combs, over 2,000 beads, and a quiver or arrow case | HistoricSite={{ill|Tokyo Designated Historic Site|ja|東京都指定史跡}}: "Noge Ōtsuka Kofun" | TangibleCulturalProperties=Excavated artifacts (Important Cultural Property of Japan) | SpecialNotes= | Map2={{location map|Japan Wards of Tokyo|label=Noge
Ōtsuka Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=36|lat_sec=18.1|lon_deg=139|lon_min=38|lon_sec=35.1|mark=帆立貝形古墳-南西.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Tokyo}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|36|18.1|N|139|38|35.1|E}}}} }} '''Noge Ōtsuka Kofun''' (野毛大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Setagaya, Tokyo|ja|世田谷区}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}}. It is scallop-shaped and designated as a {{ill|Tokyo Designated Historic Site|ja|東京都指定史跡}}. Artifacts excavated from the tumulus are designated as Important Cultural Property of Japan. == location == Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is situated in {{ill|Tamagawa Nogemachi Park|ja|玉川野毛町公園}}, within the {{ill|Musashino Plateau|ja|武蔵野台地}} at an elevation of approximately 30–33 meters.{{Cite book|和書|author=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「城南の遺跡」-世田谷周辺発掘調査最新報告- |publisher=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1993|pages=2-3}} Known since the late Edo period, it was historically referred to as "Higashi Ōtsuka." It has also been called "Kaminoge Kofun" and "Todoroki Golf Links Kofun." The tumulus is part of the {{ill|Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|ja|荏原台古墳群}} and represents the region's ancient burial practices. == Overview == At 82 meters in length, with a diameter of 67 meters and a height of 11 meters, Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is one of Japan's largest scallop-shaped kofun (hotategai-shiki kofun). An excavation conducted in 1897 (Meiji 30) revealed a stone coffin, and the artifacts were transferred to the Imperial Museum (now {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}). The mound is equipped with a stone covering (fukishi) and haniwa, and a horseshoe-shaped moat surrounds it.{{Cite book|和書|author1=世田谷区教育委員会|authorlink1=世田谷区教育委員会|author2=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「世田谷の三万年」-最近の発掘調査の成果から- |publisher=世田谷区教育委員会・世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1988|page=16}} == Burial Chambers == Four burial chambers are located in the posterior circular mound. The first chamber's split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin measures 8.2 meters in length and contains numerous grave goods, including: * Weapons and armor: short armor, helmets, gorgets, shoulder guards, 19 iron swords, and 25+ arrowheads * Mirrors and ornaments: {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡}}, bronze armlets, over 2,000 beads, and over 30 combs * Stone replicas: including axes and ceremonial tools Artifacts from the box-shaped stone coffin include magatama, tubular beads, iron weapons, armor fragments, and a wide variety of stone replicas, such as bowls, plates, and knives made from steatite. == Cultural Significance == The kofun is recognized as an archaeological benchmark for understanding 5th-century burial practices. Detailed findings were compiled by {{ill|Morikazu Gotō|ja|後藤守一}} in 1936. == Designations == * Important Cultural Property of Japan (Excavated artifacts): The artifacts are preserved at the {{ill|Setagaya Local History Museum|ja|世田谷区立郷土資料館}} and the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. * Tokyo Designated Historic Site: Designated as a Historic Site on February 6, 1975.{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.syougai.metro.tokyo.jp/iseki0/iseki/list/ruins/13112/112ruins.htm |title=世田谷区-遺跡一覧 東京都遺跡地図情報インターネット提供サービス |publisher=[[東京都教育委員会]] |accessdate=2016-07-03}} == Related Information == * The landscape stamp of {{ill|Setagaya Noge Post Office|ja|世田谷野毛郵便局}} features Noge Ōtsuka Kofun.[https://www.post.japanpost.jp/kitte_hagaki/stamp/fuke/detail.php?id=468 Landscape Stamp: Setagaya Noge Post Office] (Japan Post). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Noge-Otsuka Kofun}} * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/00011732 Excavated Artifacts of Noge Ōtsuka Kofun (Setagaya City Ownership)] - Cultural Heritage Online (Agency for Cultural Affairs) * [http://www.city.setagaya.lg.jp/kurashi/107/159/778/h26/d00135671.html Experience Ancient Life at Noge Ōtsuka Kofun Festival] - Setagaya City website {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Tokyo]] Chamber 4: box-shaped stone coffin | Dimensions=82 m in length; posterior circular mound diameter 67 m; height 11 m | BuriedPerson=Unknown | ExcavatedArtifacts=Short armor, helmet, gorgets, shoulder guards, iron swords (19), iron arrowheads (25+), iron knives, iron sickles, {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡}}, bronze armlets, 19 stone replicas, over 30 combs, over 2,000 beads, and a quiver or arrow case | HistoricSite={{ill|Tokyo Designated Historic Site|ja|東京都指定史跡}}: "Noge Ōtsuka Kofun" | TangibleCulturalProperties=Excavated artifacts (Important Cultural Property of Japan) | SpecialNotes= | Map2={{location map|Japan Wards of Tokyo|label=Noge
Ōtsuka Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=36|lat_sec=18.1|lon_deg=139|lon_min=38|lon_sec=35.1|mark=帆立貝形古墳-南西.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Tokyo}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|36|18.1|N|139|38|35.1|E}}}} }} '''Noge Ōtsuka Kofun''' (野毛大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Setagaya, Tokyo|ja|世田谷区}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}}. It is scallop-shaped and designated as a {{ill|Tokyo Designated Historic Site|ja|東京都指定史跡}}. Artifacts excavated from the tumulus are designated as Important Cultural Property of Japan. == location == Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is situated in {{ill|Tamagawa Nogemachi Park|ja|玉川野毛町公園}}, within the {{ill|Musashino Plateau|ja|武蔵野台地}} at an elevation of approximately 30–33 meters.{{Cite book|和書|author=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「城南の遺跡」-世田谷周辺発掘調査最新報告- |publisher=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1993|pages=2-3}} Known since the late Edo period, it was historically referred to as "Higashi Ōtsuka." It has also been called "Kaminoge Kofun" and "Todoroki Golf Links Kofun." The tumulus is part of the {{ill|Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|ja|荏原台古墳群}} and represents the region's ancient burial practices. == Overview == At 82 meters in length, with a diameter of 67 meters and a height of 11 meters, Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is one of Japan's largest scallop-shaped kofun (hotategai-shiki kofun). An excavation conducted in 1897 (Meiji 30) revealed a stone coffin, and the artifacts were transferred to the Imperial Museum (now {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}). The mound is equipped with a stone covering (fukishi) and haniwa, and a horseshoe-shaped moat surrounds it.{{Cite book|和書|author1=世田谷区教育委員会|authorlink1=世田谷区教育委員会|author2=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「世田谷の三万年」-最近の発掘調査の成果から- |publisher=世田谷区教育委員会・世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1988|page=16}} == Burial Chambers == Four burial chambers are located in the posterior circular mound. The first chamber's split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin measures 8.2 meters in length and contains numerous grave goods, including: * Weapons and armor: short armor, helmets, gorgets, shoulder guards, 19 iron swords, and 25+ arrowheads * Mirrors and ornaments: {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡}}, bronze armlets, over 2,000 beads, and over 30 combs * Stone replicas: including axes and ceremonial tools Artifacts from the box-shaped stone coffin include magatama, tubular beads, iron weapons, armor fragments, and a wide variety of stone replicas, such as bowls, plates, and knives made from steatite. == Cultural Significance == The kofun is recognized as an archaeological benchmark for understanding 5th-century burial practices. Detailed findings were compiled by {{ill|Morikazu Gotō|ja|後藤守一}} in 1936. == Designations == * Important Cultural Property of Japan (Excavated artifacts): The artifacts are preserved at the {{ill|Setagaya Local History Museum|ja|世田谷区立郷土資料館}} and the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. * Tokyo Designated Historic Site: Designated as a Historic Site on February 6, 1975.{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.syougai.metro.tokyo.jp/iseki0/iseki/list/ruins/13112/112ruins.htm |title=世田谷区-遺跡一覧 東京都遺跡地図情報インターネット提供サービス |publisher=[[東京都教育委員会]] |accessdate=2016-07-03}} == Related Information == * The landscape stamp of {{ill|Setagaya Noge Post Office|ja|世田谷野毛郵便局}} features Noge Ōtsuka Kofun.[https://www.post.japanpost.jp/kitte_hagaki/stamp/fuke/detail.php?id=468 Landscape Stamp: Setagaya Noge Post Office] (Japan Post). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Noge-Otsuka Kofun}} * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/00011732 {{Infobox ancient site | name=Noge Ōtsuka Kofun | Alias=Higashi Ōtsuka, Kaminoge Kofun, etc. | image=[[File:Noge Otsuka Kofun -01.jpg|260px]]
Noge Ōtsuka Kofun (April 10, 2010) | location=Noge 1-chome, {{ill|Setagaya, Tokyo|ja|世田谷区}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}} | Position={{coord|35|36|18.1|N|139|38|35.1|E|}} | Group={{ill|Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|ja|荏原台古墳群}} | Shape=Scallop-shaped kofun | ConstructionEra=Early 5th century | BurialFacility=Main chambers 1–3: split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffins in clay coffins
Chamber 4: box-shaped stone coffin | Dimensions=82 m in length; posterior circular mound diameter 67 m; height 11 m | BuriedPerson=Unknown | ExcavatedArtifacts=Short armor, helmet, gorgets, shoulder guards, iron swords (19), iron arrowheads (25+), iron knives, iron sickles, {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡}}, bronze armlets, 19 stone replicas, over 30 combs, over 2,000 beads, and a quiver or arrow case | HistoricSite={{ill|Tokyo Designated Historic Site|ja|東京都指定史跡}}: "Noge Ōtsuka Kofun" | TangibleCulturalProperties=Excavated artifacts (Important Cultural Property of Japan) | SpecialNotes= | Map2={{location map|Japan Wards of Tokyo|label=Noge
Ōtsuka Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=36|lat_sec=18.1|lon_deg=139|lon_min=38|lon_sec=35.1|mark=帆立貝形古墳-南西.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Tokyo}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|36|18.1|N|139|38|35.1|E}}}} }} '''Noge Ōtsuka Kofun''' (野毛大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Setagaya, Tokyo|ja|世田谷区}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}}. It is scallop-shaped and designated as a {{ill|Tokyo Designated Historic Site|ja|東京都指定史跡}}. Artifacts excavated from the tumulus are designated as Important Cultural Property of Japan. == location == Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is situated in {{ill|Tamagawa Nogemachi Park|ja|玉川野毛町公園}}, within the {{ill|Musashino Plateau|ja|武蔵野台地}} at an elevation of approximately 30–33 meters.{{Cite book|和書|author=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「城南の遺跡」-世田谷周辺発掘調査最新報告- |publisher=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1993|pages=2-3}} Known since the late Edo period, it was historically referred to as "Higashi Ōtsuka." It has also been called "Kaminoge Kofun" and "Todoroki Golf Links Kofun." The tumulus is part of the {{ill|Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|ja|荏原台古墳群}} and represents the region's ancient burial practices. == Overview == At 82 meters in length, with a diameter of 67 meters and a height of 11 meters, Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is one of Japan's largest scallop-shaped kofun (hotategai-shiki kofun). An excavation conducted in 1897 (Meiji 30) revealed a stone coffin, and the artifacts were transferred to the Imperial Museum (now {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}). The mound is equipped with a stone covering (fukishi) and haniwa, and a horseshoe-shaped moat surrounds it.{{Cite book|和書|author1=世田谷区教育委員会|authorlink1=世田谷区教育委員会|author2=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「世田谷の三万年」-最近の発掘調査の成果から- |publisher=世田谷区教育委員会・世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1988|page=16}} == Burial Chambers == Four burial chambers are located in the posterior circular mound. The first chamber's split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin measures 8.2 meters in length and contains numerous grave goods, including: * Weapons and armor: short armor, helmets, gorgets, shoulder guards, 19 iron swords, and 25+ arrowheads * Mirrors and ornaments: {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡}}, bronze armlets, over 2,000 beads, and over 30 combs * Stone replicas: including axes and ceremonial tools Artifacts from the box-shaped stone coffin include magatama, tubular beads, iron weapons, armor fragments, and a wide variety of stone replicas, such as bowls, plates, and knives made from steatite. == Cultural Significance == The kofun is recognized as an archaeological benchmark for understanding 5th-century burial practices. Detailed findings were compiled by {{ill|Morikazu Gotō|ja|後藤守一}} in 1936. == Designations == * Important Cultural Property of Japan (Excavated artifacts): The artifacts are preserved at the {{ill|Setagaya Local History Museum|ja|世田谷区立郷土資料館}} and the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. * Tokyo Designated Historic Site: Designated as a Historic Site on February 6, 1975.{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.syougai.metro.tokyo.jp/iseki0/iseki/list/ruins/13112/112ruins.htm |title=世田谷区-遺跡一覧 東京都遺跡地図情報インターネット提供サービス |publisher=[[東京都教育委員会]] |accessdate=2016-07-03}} == Related Information == * The landscape stamp of {{ill|Setagaya Noge Post Office|ja|世田谷野毛郵便局}} features Noge Ōtsuka Kofun.[https://www.post.japanpost.jp/kitte_hagaki/stamp/fuke/detail.php?id=468 Landscape Stamp: Setagaya Noge Post Office] (Japan Post). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Noge-Otsuka Kofun}} * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/00011732 Excavated Artifacts of Noge Ōtsuka Kofun (Setagaya City Ownership)] - Cultural Heritage Online (Agency for Cultural Affairs) * [http://www.city.setagaya.lg.jp/kurashi/107/159/778/h26/d00135671.html Experience Ancient Life at Noge Ōtsuka Kofun Festival] - Setagaya City website {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Tokyo]] It is scallop-shaped and designated as a {{ill|Tokyo Designated Historic Site|ja|東京都指定史跡}}. It has also been called "Kaminoge Kofun" and "Todoroki Golf Links Kofun." The tumulus is part of the {{ill|Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|ja|荏原台古墳群}} and represents the region's ancient burial practices. == Overview == At 82 meters in length, with a diameter of 67 meters and a height of 11 meters, Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is one of Japan's largest scallop-shaped kofun (hotategai-shiki kofun). An excavation conducted in 1897 (Meiji 30) revealed a stone coffin, and the artifacts were transferred to the Imperial Museum (now {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}). The mound is equipped with a stone covering (fukishi) and haniwa, and a horseshoe-shaped moat surrounds it.{{Cite book|和書|author1=世田谷区教育委員会|authorlink1=世田谷区教育委員会|author2=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「世田谷の三万年」-最近の発掘調査の成果から- |publisher=世田谷区教育委員会・世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1988|page=16}} == Burial Chambers == Four burial chambers are located in the posterior circular mound. The first chamber's split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin measures 8.2 meters in length and contains numerous grave goods, including: * Weapons and armor: short armor, helmets, gorgets, shoulder guards, 19 iron swords, and 25+ arrowheads * Mirrors and ornaments: {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡}}, bronze armlets, over 2,000 beads, and over 30 combs * Stone replicas: including axes and ceremonial tools Artifacts from the box-shaped stone coffin include magatama, tubular beads, iron weapons, armor fragments, and a wide variety of stone replicas, such as bowls, plates, and knives made from steatite. == Cultural Significance == The kofun is recognized as an archaeological benchmark for understanding 5th-century burial practices. Detailed findings were compiled by {{ill|Morikazu Gotō|ja|後藤守一}} in 1936. == Designations == * Important Cultural Property of Japan (Excavated artifacts): The artifacts are preserved at the {{ill|Setagaya Local History Museum|ja|世田谷区立郷土資料館}} and the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. * Tokyo Designated Historic Site: Designated as a Historic Site on February 6, 1975.{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.syougai.metro.tokyo.jp/iseki0/iseki/list/ruins/13112/112ruins.htm |title=世田谷区-遺跡一覧 東京都遺跡地図情報インターネット提供サービス |publisher=[[東京都教育委員会]] |accessdate=2016-07-03}} == Related {{Infobox ancient site | name=Noge Ōtsuka Kofun | Alias=Higashi Ōtsuka, Kaminoge Kofun, etc. | image=[[File:Noge Otsuka Kofun -01.jpg|260px]]
Noge Ōtsuka Kofun (April 10, 2010) | location=Noge 1-chome, {{ill|Setagaya, Tokyo|ja|世田谷区}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}} | Position={{coord|35|36|18.1|N|139|38|35.1|E|}} | Group={{ill|Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|ja|荏原台古墳群}} | Shape=Scallop-shaped kofun | ConstructionEra=Early 5th century | BurialFacility=Main chambers 1–3: split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffins in clay coffins
Chamber 4: box-shaped stone coffin | Dimensions=82 m in length; posterior circular mound diameter 67 m; height 11 m | BuriedPerson=Unknown | ExcavatedArtifacts=Short armor, helmet, gorgets, shoulder guards, iron swords (19), iron arrowheads (25+), iron knives, iron sickles, {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡}}, bronze armlets, 19 stone replicas, over 30 combs, over 2,000 beads, and a quiver or arrow case | HistoricSite={{ill|Tokyo Designated Historic Site|ja|東京都指定史跡}}: "Noge Ōtsuka Kofun" | TangibleCulturalProperties=Excavated artifacts (Important Cultural Property of Japan) | SpecialNotes= | Map2={{location map|Japan Wards of Tokyo|label=Noge
Ōtsuka Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=36|lat_sec=18.1|lon_deg=139|lon_min=38|lon_sec=35.1|mark=帆立貝形古墳-南西.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Tokyo}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|36|18.1|N|139|38|35.1|E}}}} }} '''Noge Ōtsuka Kofun''' (野毛大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Setagaya, Tokyo|ja|世田谷区}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}}. It is scallop-shaped and designated as a {{ill|Tokyo Designated Historic Site|ja|東京都指定史跡}}. Artifacts excavated from the tumulus are designated as Important Cultural Property of Japan. == location == Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is situated in {{ill|Tamagawa Nogemachi Park|ja|玉川野毛町公園}}, within the {{ill|Musashino Plateau|ja|武蔵野台地}} at an elevation of approximately 30–33 meters.{{Cite book|和書|author=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「城南の遺跡」-世田谷周辺発掘調査最新報告- |publisher=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1993|pages=2-3}} Known since the late Edo period, it was historically referred to as "Higashi Ōtsuka." It has also been called "Kaminoge Kofun" and "Todoroki Golf Links Kofun." The tumulus is part of the {{ill|Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|ja|荏原台古墳群}} and represents the region's ancient burial practices. == Overview == At 82 meters in length, with a diameter of 67 meters and a height of 11 meters, Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is one of Japan's largest scallop-shaped kofun (hotategai-shiki kofun). An excavation conducted in 1897 (Meiji 30) revealed a stone coffin, and the artifacts were transferred to the Imperial Museum (now {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}). The mound is equipped with a stone covering (fukishi) and haniwa, and a horseshoe-shaped moat surrounds it.{{Cite book|和書|author1=世田谷区教育委員会|authorlink1=世田谷区教育委員会|author2=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「世田谷の三万年」-最近の発掘調査の成果から- |publisher=世田谷区教育委員会・世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1988|page=16}} == Burial Chambers == Four burial chambers are located in the posterior circular mound. The first chamber's split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin measures 8.2 meters in length and contains numerous grave goods, including: * Weapons and armor: short armor, helmets, gorgets, shoulder guards, 19 iron swords, and 25+ arrowheads * Mirrors and ornaments: {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡}}, bronze armlets, over 2,000 beads, and over 30 combs * Stone replicas: including axes and ceremonial tools Artifacts from the box-shaped stone coffin include magatama, tubular beads, iron weapons, armor fragments, and a wide variety of stone replicas, such as bowls, plates, and knives made from steatite. == Cultural Significance == The kofun is recognized as an archaeological benchmark for understanding 5th-century burial practices. Detailed findings were compiled by {{ill|Morikazu Gotō|ja|後藤守一}} in 1936. == Designations == * Important Cultural Property of Japan (Excavated artifacts): The artifacts are preserved at the {{ill|Setagaya Local History Museum|ja|世田谷区立郷土資料館}} and the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. * Tokyo Designated Historic Site: Designated as a Historic Site on February 6, 1975.{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.syougai.metro.tokyo.jp/iseki0/iseki/list/ruins/13112/112ruins.htm |title=世田谷区-遺跡一覧 東京都遺跡地図情報インターネット提供サービス |publisher=[[東京都教育委員会]] |accessdate=2016-07-03}} == Related Information == * The landscape stamp of {{ill|Setagaya Noge Post Office|ja|世田谷野毛郵便局}} features Noge Ōtsuka Kofun.[https://www.post.japanpost.jp/kitte_hagaki/stamp/fuke/detail.php?id=468 Landscape Stamp: Setagaya Noge Post Office] (Japan Post). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Noge-Otsuka Kofun}} * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/00011732 Excavated Artifacts of Noge Ōtsuka Kofun (Setagaya City Ownership)] - Cultural Heritage Online (Agency for Cultural Affairs) * [http://www.city.setagaya.lg.jp/kurashi/107/159/778/h26/d00135671.html Experience Ancient Life at Noge Ōtsuka Kofun Festival] - Setagaya City website {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Tokyo]] {{Infobox ancient site | name=Noge Ōtsuka Kofun | Alias=Higashi Ōtsuka, Kaminoge Kofun, etc. | image=[[File:Noge Otsuka Kofun -01.jpg|260px]]
Noge Ōtsuka Kofun (April 10, 2010) | location=Noge 1-chome, {{ill|Setagaya, Tokyo|ja|世田谷区}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}} | coordinates={{coord|35|36|18.1|N|139|38|35.1|E|}} | Group={{ill|Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|ja|荏原台古墳群}} | Shape=Scallop-shaped kofun | ConstructionEra=Early 5th century | BurialFacility=Main chambers 1–3: split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffins in clay coffins
Chamber 4: box-shaped stone coffin | Dimensions=82 m in length; posterior circular mound diameter 67 m; height 11 m | BuriedPerson=Unknown | ExcavatedArtifacts=Short armor, helmet, gorgets, shoulder guards, iron swords (19), iron arrowheads (25+), iron knives, iron sickles, {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡}}, bronze armlets, 19 stone replicas, over 30 combs, over 2,000 beads, and a quiver or arrow case | HistoricSite={{ill|Tokyo Designated Historic Site|ja|東京都指定史跡}}: "Noge Ōtsuka Kofun" | TangibleCulturalProperties=Excavated artifacts (Important Cultural Property of Japan) | SpecialNotes= | Map2={{location map|Japan Wards of Tokyo|label=Noge
Ōtsuka Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=36|lat_sec=18.1|lon_deg=139|lon_min=38|lon_sec=35.1|mark=帆立貝形古墳-南西.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Tokyo}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|36|18.1|N|139|38|35.1|E}}}} }} '''Noge Ōtsuka Kofun''' (野毛大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Setagaya, Tokyo|ja|世田谷区}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}}. It is scallop-shaped and designated as a {{ill|Tokyo Designated Historic Site|ja|東京都指定史跡}}. Artifacts excavated from the tumulus are designated as Important Cultural Property of Japan. == location == Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is situated in {{ill|Tamagawa Nogemachi Park|ja|玉川野毛町公園}}, within the {{ill|Musashino Plateau|ja|武蔵野台地}} at an elevation of approximately 30–33 meters.{{Cite book|和書|author=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「城南の遺跡」-世田谷周辺発掘調査最新報告- |publisher=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1993|pages=2-3}} Known since the late Edo period, it was historically referred to as "Higashi Ōtsuka." It has also been called "Kaminoge Kofun" and "Todoroki Golf Links Kofun." The tumulus is part of the {{ill|Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|ja|荏原台古墳群}} and represents the region's ancient burial practices. == Overview == At 82 meters in length, with a diameter of 67 meters and a height of 11 meters, Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is one of Japan's largest scallop-shaped kofun (hotategai-shiki kofun). An excavation conducted in 1897 (Meiji 30) revealed a stone coffin, and the artifacts were transferred to the Imperial Museum (now {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}). The mound is equipped with a stone covering (fukishi) and haniwa, and a horseshoe-shaped moat surrounds it.{{Cite book|和書|author1=世田谷区教育委員会|authorlink1=世田谷区教育委員会|author2=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「世田谷の三万年」-最近の発掘調査の成果から- |publisher=世田谷区教育委員会・世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1988|page=16}} == Burial Chambers == Four burial chambers are located in the posterior circular mound. The first chamber's split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin measures 8.2 meters in length and contains numerous grave goods, including: * Weapons and armor: short armor, helmets, gorgets, shoulder guards, 19 iron swords, and 25+ arrowheads * Mirrors and ornaments: {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡}}, bronze armlets, over 2,000 beads, and over 30 combs * Stone replicas: including axes and ceremonial tools Artifacts from the box-shaped stone coffin include magatama, tubular beads, iron weapons, armor fragments, and a wide variety of stone replicas, such as bowls, plates, and knives made from steatite. == Cultural Significance == The kofun is recognized as an archaeological benchmark for understanding 5th-century burial practices. Detailed findings were compiled by {{ill|Morikazu Gotō|ja|後藤守一}} in 1936. == Designations == * Important Cultural Property of Japan (Excavated artifacts): The artifacts are preserved at the {{ill|Setagaya Local History Museum|ja|世田谷区立郷土資料館}} and the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. * Tokyo Designated Historic Site: Designated as a Historic Site on February 6, 1975.{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.syougai.metro.tokyo.jp/iseki0/iseki/list/ruins/13112/112ruins.htm |title=世田谷区-遺跡一覧 東京都遺跡地図情報インターネット提供サービス |publisher=[[東京都教育委員会]] |accessdate=2016-07-03}} == Related Information == * The landscape stamp of {{ill|Setagaya Noge Post Office|ja|世田谷野毛郵便局}} features Noge Ōtsuka Kofun.[https://www.post.japanpost.jp/kitte_hagaki/stamp/fuke/detail.php?id=468 Landscape Stamp: Setagaya Noge Post Office] (Japan Post). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Noge-Otsuka Kofun}} * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/00011732 Excavated Artifacts of Noge Ōtsuka Kofun (Setagaya City Ownership)] - Cultural Heritage Online (Agency for Cultural Affairs) * [http://www.city.setagaya.lg.jp/kurashi/107/159/778/h26/d00135671.html Experience Ancient Life at Noge Ōtsuka Kofun Festival] - Setagaya City website {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Tokyo]] {{Infobox ancient site | name=Noge Ōtsuka Kofun | Alias=Higashi Ōtsuka, Kaminoge Kofun, etc. | image=[[File:Noge Otsuka Kofun -01.jpg|260px]]
Noge Ōtsuka Kofun (April 10, 2010) | location=Noge 1-chome, {{ill|Setagaya, Tokyo|ja|世田谷区}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}} | coordinates={{coord|35|36|18.1|N|139|38|35.1|E|}} | Group={{ill|Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|ja|荏原台古墳群}} | Shape=Scallop-shaped kofun | ConstructionEra=Early 5th century | BurialFacility=Main chambers 1–3: split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffins in clay coffins
Chamber 4: box-shaped stone coffin | Dimensions=82 m in length; posterior circular mound diameter 67 m; height 11 m | BuriedPerson=Unknown | ExcavatedArtifacts=Short armor, helmet, gorgets, shoulder guards, iron swords (19), iron arrowheads (25+), iron knives, iron sickles, {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡}}, bronze armlets, 19 stone replicas, over 30 combs, over 2,000 beads, and a quiver or arrow case | HistoricSite={{ill|Tokyo Designated Historic Site|ja|東京都指定史跡}}: "Noge Ōtsuka Kofun" | TangibleCulturalProperties=Excavated artifacts (Important Cultural Property of Japan) | SpecialNotes= | Map2={{location map|Japan Wards of Tokyo|label=Noge
Ōtsuka Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=36|lat_sec=18.1|lon_deg=139|lon_min=38|lon_sec=35.1|mark=帆立貝形古墳-南西.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Tokyo}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|36|18.1|N|139|38|35.1|E}}}} }} '''Noge Ōtsuka Kofun''' (野毛大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Setagaya, Tokyo|ja|世田谷区}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}}. It is scallop-shaped and designated as a {{ill|Tokyo Designated Historic Site|ja|東京都指定史跡}}. Artifacts excavated from the tumulus are designated as Important Cultural Property of Japan. == location == Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is situated in {{ill|Tamagawa Nogemachi Park|ja|玉川野毛町公園}}, within the {{ill|Musashino Plateau|ja|武蔵野台地}} at an elevation of approximately 30–33 meters.{{Cite book|和書|author=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「城南の遺跡」-世田谷周辺発掘調査最新報告- |publisher=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1993|pages=2-3}} Known since the late Edo period, it was historically referred to as "Higashi Ōtsuka." It has also been called "Kaminoge Kofun" and "Todoroki Golf Links Kofun." The tumulus is part of the {{ill|Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|ja|荏原台古墳群}} and represents the region's ancient burial practices. == Overview == At 82 meters in length, with a diameter of 67 meters and a height of 11 meters, Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is one of Japan's largest scallop-shaped kofun (hotategai-shiki kofun). An excavation conducted in 1897 (Meiji 30) revealed a stone coffin, and the artifacts were transferred to the Imperial Museum (now {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}). The mound is equipped with a stone covering (fukishi) and haniwa, and a horseshoe-shaped moat surrounds it.{{Cite book|和書|author1=世田谷区教育委員会|authorlink1=世田谷区教育委員会|author2=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「世田谷の三万年」-最近の発掘調査の成果から- |publisher=世田谷区教育委員会・世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1988|page=16}} == Burial Chambers == Four burial chambers are located in the posterior circular mound. The first chamber's split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin measures 8.2 meters in length and contains numerous grave goods, including: * Weapons and armor: short armor, helmets, gorgets, shoulder guards, 19 iron swords, and 25+ arrowheads * Mirrors and ornaments: {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡}}, bronze armlets, over 2,000 beads, and over 30 combs * Stone replicas: including axes and ceremonial tools Artifacts from the box-shaped stone coffin include magatama, tubular beads, iron weapons, armor fragments, and a wide variety of stone replicas, such as bowls, plates, and knives made from steatite. == Cultural Significance == The kofun is recognized as an archaeological benchmark for understanding 5th-century burial practices. Detailed findings were compiled by {{ill|Morikazu Gotō|ja|後藤守一}} in 1936. == Designations == * Important Cultural Property of Japan (Excavated artifacts): The artifacts are preserved at the {{ill|Setagaya Local History Museum|ja|世田谷区立郷土資料館}} and the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. * Tokyo Designated Historic Site: Designated as a Historic Site on February 6, 1975.{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.syougai.metro.tokyo.jp/iseki0/iseki/list/ruins/13112/112ruins.htm |title=世田谷区-遺跡一覧 東京都遺跡地図情報インターネット提供サービス |publisher=[[東京都教育委員会]] |accessdate=2016-07-03}} == Related Information == * The landscape stamp of {{ill|Setagaya Noge Post Office|ja|世田谷野毛郵便局}} features Noge Ōtsuka Kofun.[https://www.post.japanpost.jp/kitte_hagaki/stamp/fuke/detail.php?id=468 Landscape Stamp: Setagaya Noge Post Office] (Japan Post). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Noge-Otsuka Kofun}} * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/00011732 Excavated Artifacts of Noge Ōtsuka Kofun (Setagaya City Ownership)] - Cultural Heritage Online (Agency for Cultural Affairs) * [http://www.city.setagaya.lg.jp/kurashi/107/159/778/h26/d00135671.html Experience Ancient Life at Noge Ōtsuka Kofun Festival] - Setagaya City website {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Tokyo]] revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name=Noge Ōtsuka Kofun | Alias=Higashi Ōtsuka, Kaminoge Kofun, etc. | image=[[File:Noge Otsuka Kofun -01.jpg|260px]]
Noge Ōtsuka Kofun (April 10, 2010) | location=Noge 1-chome, {{ill|Setagaya, Tokyo|ja|世田谷区}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}} | coordinates={{coord|35|36|18.1|N|139|38|35.1|E|}} | Group={{ill|Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|ja|荏原台古墳群}} | Shape=Scallop-shaped kofun | ConstructionEra=Early 5th century | BurialFacility=Main chambers 1–3: split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffins in clay coffins
Chamber 4: box-shaped stone coffin | Dimensions=82 m in length; posterior circular mound diameter 67 m; height 11 m | BuriedPerson=Unknown | ExcavatedArtifacts=Short armor, helmet, gorgets, shoulder guards, iron swords (19), iron arrowheads (25+), iron knives, iron sickles, {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡}}, bronze armlets, 19 stone replicas, over 30 combs, over 2,000 beads, and a quiver or arrow case | HistoricSite={{ill|Tokyo Designated Historic Site|ja|東京都指定史跡}}: "Noge Ōtsuka Kofun" | TangibleCulturalProperties=Excavated artifacts (Important Cultural Property of Japan) | SpecialNotes= | Map2={{location map|Japan Wards of Tokyo|label=Noge
Ōtsuka Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=36|lat_sec=18.1|lon_deg=139|lon_min=38|lon_sec=35.1|mark=帆立貝形古墳-南西.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Tokyo}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|36|18.1|N|139|38|35.1|E}}}} }} '''Noge Ōtsuka Kofun''' (野毛大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Setagaya, Tokyo|ja|世田谷区}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}}. It is scallop-shaped and designated as a {{ill|Tokyo Designated Historic Site|ja|東京都指定史跡}}. Artifacts excavated from the tumulus are designated as Important Cultural Property of Japan. == location == Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is situated in {{ill|Tamagawa Nogemachi Park|ja|玉川野毛町公園}}, within the {{ill|Musashino Plateau|ja|武蔵野台地}} at an elevation of approximately 30–33 meters.{{Cite book|和書|author=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「城南の遺跡」-世田谷周辺発掘調査最新報告- |publisher=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1993|pages=2-3}} Known since the late Edo period, it was historically referred to as "Higashi Ōtsuka." It has also been called "Kaminoge Kofun" and "Todoroki Golf Links Kofun." The tumulus is part of the {{ill|Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|ja|荏原台古墳群}} and represents the region's ancient burial practices. == Overview == At 82 meters in length, with a diameter of 67 meters and a height of 11 meters, Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is one of Japan's largest scallop-shaped kofun (hotategai-shiki kofun). An excavation conducted in 1897 (Meiji 30) revealed a stone coffin, and the artifacts were transferred to the Imperial Museum (now {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}). The mound is equipped with a stone covering (fukishi) and haniwa, and a horseshoe-shaped moat surrounds it.{{Cite book|和書|author1=世田谷区教育委員会|authorlink1=世田谷区教育委員会|author2=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「世田谷の三万年」-最近の発掘調査の成果から- |publisher=世田谷区教育委員会・世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1988|page=16}} == Burial Chambers == Four burial chambers are located in the posterior circular mound. The first chamber's split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin measures 8.2 meters in length and contains numerous grave goods, including: * Weapons and armor: short armor, helmets, gorgets, shoulder guards, 19 iron swords, and 25+ arrowheads * Mirrors and ornaments: {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡}}, bronze armlets, over 2,000 beads, and over 30 combs * Stone replicas: including axes and ceremonial tools Artifacts from the box-shaped stone coffin include magatama, tubular beads, iron weapons, armor fragments, and a wide variety of stone replicas, such as bowls, plates, and knives made from steatite. == Cultural Significance == The kofun is recognized as an archaeological benchmark for understanding 5th-century burial practices. Detailed findings were compiled by {{ill|Morikazu Gotō|ja|後藤守一}} in 1936. == Designations == * Important Cultural Property of Japan (Excavated artifacts): The artifacts are preserved at the {{ill|Setagaya Local History Museum|ja|世田谷区立郷土資料館}} and the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. * Tokyo Designated Historic Site: Designated as a Historic Site on February 6, 1975.{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.syougai.metro.tokyo.jp/iseki0/iseki/list/ruins/13112/112ruins.htm |title=世田谷区-遺跡一覧 東京都遺跡地図情報インターネット提供サービス |publisher=[[東京都教育委員会]] |accessdate=2016-07-03}} == Related Information == * The landscape stamp of {{ill|Setagaya Noge Post Office|ja|世田谷野毛郵便局}} features Noge Ōtsuka Kofun.[https://www.post.japanpost.jp/kitte_hagaki/stamp/fuke/detail.php?id=468 Landscape Stamp: Setagaya Noge Post Office] (Japan Post). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Noge-Otsuka Kofun}} * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/00011732 Excavated Artifacts of Noge Ōtsuka Kofun (Setagaya City Ownership)] - Cultural Heritage Online (Agency for Cultural Affairs) * [http://www.city.setagaya.lg.jp/kurashi/107/159/778/h26/d00135671.html Experience Ancient Life at Noge Ōtsuka Kofun Festival] - Setagaya City website {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Tokyo]]
