The mound is equipped with a stone covering (fukishi) and haniwa, and a horseshoe-shaped moat surrounds it.{{Cite book|和書|author1=世田谷区教育委員会|authorlink1=世田谷区教育委員会|author2=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「世田谷の三万年」-最近の発掘調査の成果から- |publisher=世田谷区教育委員会・世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1988|page=16}} == Burial Chambers == Four burial chambers are located in the posterior circular mound. The first chamber's split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin measures 8.2 meters in length and contains numerous grave goods, including: * Weapons and armor: short armor, helmets, gorgets, shoulder guards, 19 iron swords, and 25+ arrowheads * Mirrors and ornaments: {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡}}, bronze armlets, over 2,000 beads, and over 30 combs * Stone replicas: including axes and ceremonial tools Artifacts from the box-shaped stone coffin include magatama, tubular beads, iron weapons, armor fragments, and a wide variety of stone replicas, such as bowls, plates, and knives made from steatite. == Cultural Significance == The kofun is recognized as an archaeological benchmark for understanding 5th-century burial practices. Detailed findings were compiled by {{ill|Morikazu Gotō|ja|後藤守一}} in 1936. == Designations == * Important Cultural Property of Japan (Excavated artifacts): The artifacts are preserved at the {{ill|Setagaya Local History Museum|ja|世田谷区立郷土資料館}} and the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. * Tokyo Designated Historic Site: Designated as a Historic Site on February 6, 1975.{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.syougai.metro.tokyo.jp/iseki0/iseki/list/ruins/13112/112ruins.htm |title=世田谷区-遺跡一覧 東京都遺跡地図情報インターネット提供サービス |publisher=[[東京都教育委員会]] |accessdate=2016-07-03}} == Related {{Infobox ancient site | name=Noge Ōtsuka Kofun | Alias=Higashi Ōtsuka, Kaminoge Kofun, etc. | image=[[File:Noge Otsuka Kofun -01.jpg|260px]]
Noge Ōtsuka Kofun (April 10, 2010) | location=Noge 1-chome, {{ill|Setagaya, Tokyo|ja|世田谷区}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}} | coordinates={{coord|35|36|18.1|N|139|38|35.1|E|}} | Group={{ill|Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|ja|荏原台古墳群}} | Shape=Scallop-shaped kofun | ConstructionEra=Early 5th century | BurialFacility=Main chambers 1–3: split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffins in clay coffins
Chamber 4: box-shaped stone coffin | Dimensions=82 m in length; posterior circular mound diameter 67 m; height 11 m | BuriedPerson=Unknown | ExcavatedArtifacts=Short armor, helmet, gorgets, shoulder guards, iron swords (19), iron arrowheads (25+), iron knives, iron sickles, {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡}}, bronze armlets, 19 stone replicas, over 30 combs, over 2,000 beads, and a quiver or arrow case | HistoricSite={{ill|Tokyo Designated Historic Site|ja|東京都指定史跡}}: "Noge Ōtsuka Kofun" | TangibleCulturalProperties=Excavated artifacts (Important Cultural Property of Japan) | SpecialNotes= | Map2={{location map|Japan Wards of Tokyo|label=Noge
Ōtsuka Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=36|lat_sec=18.1|lon_deg=139|lon_min=38|lon_sec=35.1|mark=帆立貝形古墳-南西.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Tokyo}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|36|18.1|N|139|38|35.1|E}}}} }} '''Noge Ōtsuka Kofun''' (野毛大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Setagaya, Tokyo|ja|世田谷区}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}}. It is scallop-shaped and designated as a {{ill|Tokyo Designated Historic Site|ja|東京都指定史跡}}. Artifacts excavated from the tumulus are designated as Important Cultural Property of Japan. == location == Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is situated in {{ill|Tamagawa Nogemachi Park|ja|玉川野毛町公園}}, within the {{ill|Musashino Plateau|ja|武蔵野台地}} at an elevation of approximately 30–33 meters.{{Cite book|和書|author=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「城南の遺跡」-世田谷周辺発掘調査最新報告- |publisher=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1993|pages=2-3}} Known since the late Edo period, it was historically referred to as "Higashi Ōtsuka." It has also been called "Kaminoge Kofun" and "Todoroki Golf Links Kofun." The tumulus is part of the {{ill|Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|ja|荏原台古墳群}} and represents the region's ancient burial practices. == Overview == At 82 meters in length, with a diameter of 67 meters and a height of 11 meters, Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is one of Japan's largest scallop-shaped kofun (hotategai-shiki kofun). An excavation conducted in 1897 (Meiji 30) revealed a stone coffin, and the artifacts were transferred to the Imperial Museum (now {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}). The mound is equipped with a stone covering (fukishi) and haniwa, and a horseshoe-shaped moat surrounds it.{{Cite book|和書|author1=世田谷区教育委員会|authorlink1=世田谷区教育委員会|author2=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「世田谷の三万年」-最近の発掘調査の成果から- |publisher=世田谷区教育委員会・世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1988|page=16}} == Burial Chambers == Four burial chambers are located in the posterior circular mound. The first chamber's split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin measures 8.2 meters in length and contains numerous grave goods, including: * Weapons and armor: short armor, helmets, gorgets, shoulder guards, 19 iron swords, and 25+ arrowheads * Mirrors and ornaments: {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡}}, bronze armlets, over 2,000 beads, and over 30 combs * Stone replicas: including axes and ceremonial tools Artifacts from the box-shaped stone coffin include magatama, tubular beads, iron weapons, armor fragments, and a wide variety of stone replicas, such as bowls, plates, and knives made from steatite. == Cultural Significance == The kofun is recognized as an archaeological benchmark for understanding 5th-century burial practices. Detailed findings were compiled by {{ill|Morikazu Gotō|ja|後藤守一}} in 1936. == Designations == * Important Cultural Property of Japan (Excavated artifacts): The artifacts are preserved at the {{ill|Setagaya Local History Museum|ja|世田谷区立郷土資料館}} and the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. * Tokyo Designated Historic Site: Designated as a Historic Site on February 6, 1975.{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.syougai.metro.tokyo.jp/iseki0/iseki/list/ruins/13112/112ruins.htm |title=世田谷区-遺跡一覧 東京都遺跡地図情報インターネット提供サービス |publisher=[[東京都教育委員会]] |accessdate=2016-07-03}} == Related Information == * The landscape stamp of {{ill|Setagaya Noge Post Office|ja|世田谷野毛郵便局}} features Noge Ōtsuka Kofun.[https://www.post.japanpost.jp/kitte_hagaki/stamp/fuke/detail.php?id=468 Landscape Stamp: Setagaya Noge Post Office] (Japan Post). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Noge-Otsuka Kofun}} * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/00011732 The first chamber's split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin measures 8.2 meters in length and contains numerous grave goods, including: * Weapons and armor: short armor, helmets, gorgets, shoulder guards, 19 iron swords, and 25+ arrowheads * Mirrors and ornaments: {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡}}, bronze armlets, over 2,000 beads, and over 30 combs * Stone replicas: including axes and ceremonial tools Artifacts from the box-shaped stone coffin include magatama, tubular beads, iron weapons, armor fragments, and a wide variety of stone replicas, such as bowls, plates, and knives made from steatite. == Cultural Significance == The kofun is recognized as an archaeological benchmark for understanding 5th-century burial practices. Detailed findings were compiled by {{ill|Morikazu Gotō|ja|後藤守一}} in 1936. == Designations == * Important Cultural Property of Japan (Excavated artifacts): The artifacts are preserved at the {{ill|Setagaya Local History Museum|ja|世田谷区立郷土資料館}} and the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. * Tokyo Designated Historic Site: Designated as a Historic Site on February 6, 1975.{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.syougai.metro.tokyo.jp/iseki0/iseki/list/ruins/13112/112ruins.htm |title=世田谷区-遺跡一覧 東京都遺跡地図情報インターネット提供サービス |publisher=[[東京都教育委員会]] |accessdate=2016-07-03}} == Related {{Infobox ancient site | name=Noge Ōtsuka Kofun | Alias=Higashi Ōtsuka, Kaminoge Kofun, etc. | image=[[File:Noge Otsuka Kofun -01.jpg|260px]]
Noge Ōtsuka Kofun (April 10, 2010) | location=Noge 1-chome, {{ill|Setagaya, Tokyo|ja|世田谷区}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}} | coordinates={{coord|35|36|18.1|N|139|38|35.1|E|}} | Group={{ill|Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|ja|荏原台古墳群}} | Shape=Scallop-shaped kofun | ConstructionEra=Early 5th century | BurialFacility=Main chambers 1–3: split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffins in clay coffins
Chamber 4: box-shaped stone coffin | Dimensions=82 m in length; posterior circular mound diameter 67 m; height 11 m | BuriedPerson=Unknown | ExcavatedArtifacts=Short armor, helmet, gorgets, shoulder guards, iron swords (19), iron arrowheads (25+), iron knives, iron sickles, {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡}}, bronze armlets, 19 stone replicas, over 30 combs, over 2,000 beads, and a quiver or arrow case | HistoricSite={{ill|Tokyo Designated Historic Site|ja|東京都指定史跡}}: "Noge Ōtsuka Kofun" | TangibleCulturalProperties=Excavated artifacts (Important Cultural Property of Japan) | SpecialNotes= | Map2={{location map|Japan Wards of Tokyo|label=Noge
Ōtsuka Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=36|lat_sec=18.1|lon_deg=139|lon_min=38|lon_sec=35.1|mark=帆立貝形古墳-南西.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Tokyo}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|36|18.1|N|139|38|35.1|E}}}} }} '''Noge Ōtsuka Kofun''' (野毛大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Setagaya, Tokyo|ja|世田谷区}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}}. It is scallop-shaped and designated as a {{ill|Tokyo Designated Historic Site|ja|東京都指定史跡}}. Artifacts excavated from the tumulus are designated as Important Cultural Property of Japan. == location == Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is situated in {{ill|Tamagawa Nogemachi Park|ja|玉川野毛町公園}}, within the {{ill|Musashino Plateau|ja|武蔵野台地}} at an elevation of approximately 30–33 meters.{{Cite book|和書|author=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「城南の遺跡」-世田谷周辺発掘調査最新報告- |publisher=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1993|pages=2-3}} Known since the late Edo period, it was historically referred to as "Higashi Ōtsuka." It has also been called "Kaminoge Kofun" and "Todoroki Golf Links Kofun." The tumulus is part of the {{ill|Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|ja|荏原台古墳群}} and represents the region's ancient burial practices. == Overview == At 82 meters in length, with a diameter of 67 meters and a height of 11 meters, Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is one of Japan's largest scallop-shaped kofun (hotategai-shiki kofun). An excavation conducted in 1897 (Meiji 30) revealed a stone coffin, and the artifacts were transferred to the Imperial Museum (now {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}). The mound is equipped with a stone covering (fukishi) and haniwa, and a horseshoe-shaped moat surrounds it.{{Cite book|和書|author1=世田谷区教育委員会|authorlink1=世田谷区教育委員会|author2=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「世田谷の三万年」-最近の発掘調査の成果から- |publisher=世田谷区教育委員会・世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1988|page=16}} == Burial Chambers == Four burial chambers are located in the posterior circular mound. The first chamber's split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin measures 8.2 meters in length and contains numerous grave goods, including: * Weapons and armor: short armor, helmets, gorgets, shoulder guards, 19 iron swords, and 25+ arrowheads * Mirrors and ornaments: {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡}}, bronze armlets, over 2,000 beads, and over 30 combs * Stone replicas: including axes and ceremonial tools Artifacts from the box-shaped stone coffin include magatama, tubular beads, iron weapons, armor fragments, and a wide variety of stone replicas, such as bowls, plates, and knives made from steatite. == Cultural Significance == The kofun is recognized as an archaeological benchmark for understanding 5th-century burial practices. Detailed findings were compiled by {{ill|Morikazu Gotō|ja|後藤守一}} in 1936. == Designations == * Important Cultural Property of Japan (Excavated artifacts): The artifacts are preserved at the {{ill|Setagaya Local History Museum|ja|世田谷区立郷土資料館}} and the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. * Tokyo Designated Historic Site: Designated as a Historic Site on February 6, 1975.{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.syougai.metro.tokyo.jp/iseki0/iseki/list/ruins/13112/112ruins.htm |title=世田谷区-遺跡一覧 東京都遺跡地図情報インターネット提供サービス |publisher=[[東京都教育委員会]] |accessdate=2016-07-03}} == Related Information == * The landscape stamp of {{ill|Setagaya Noge Post Office|ja|世田谷野毛郵便局}} features Noge Ōtsuka Kofun.[https://www.post.japanpost.jp/kitte_hagaki/stamp/fuke/detail.php?id=468 Landscape Stamp: Setagaya Noge Post Office] (Japan Post). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Noge-Otsuka Kofun}} * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/00011732 It is scallop-shaped and designated as a {{ill|Tokyo Designated Historic Site|ja|東京都指定史跡}}. Artifacts excavated from the tumulus are designated as Important Cultural Property of Japan. == location == Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is situated in {{ill|Tamagawa Nogemachi Park|ja|玉川野毛町公園}}, within the {{ill|Musashino Plateau|ja|武蔵野台地}} at an elevation of approximately 30–33 meters.{{Cite book|和書|author=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「城南の遺跡」-世田谷周辺発掘調査最新報告- |publisher=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1993|pages=2-3}} Known since the late Edo period, it was historically referred to as "Higashi Ōtsuka." It has also been called "Kaminoge Kofun" and "Todoroki Golf Links Kofun." The tumulus is part of the {{ill|Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|ja|荏原台古墳群}} and represents the region's ancient burial practices. == Overview == At 82 meters in length, with a diameter of 67 meters and a height of 11 meters, Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is one of Japan's largest scallop-shaped kofun (hotategai-shiki kofun). An excavation conducted in 1897 (Meiji 30) revealed a stone coffin, and the artifacts were transferred to the Imperial Museum (now {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}). The mound is equipped with a stone covering (fukishi) and haniwa, and a horseshoe-shaped moat surrounds it.{{Cite book|和書|author1=世田谷区教育委員会|authorlink1=世田谷区教育委員会|author2=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「世田谷の三万年」-最近の発掘調査の成果から- |publisher=世田谷区教育委員会・世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1988|page=16}} == Burial Chambers == Four burial chambers are located in the posterior circular mound. The first chamber's split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin measures 8.2 meters in length and contains numerous grave goods, including: * Weapons and armor: short armor, helmets, gorgets, shoulder guards, 19 iron swords, and 25+ arrowheads * Mirrors and ornaments: {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡}}, bronze armlets, over 2,000 beads, and over 30 combs * Stone replicas: including axes and ceremonial tools Artifacts from the box-shaped stone coffin include magatama, tubular beads, iron weapons, armor fragments, and a wide variety of stone replicas, such as bowls, plates, and knives made from steatite. == Cultural Significance == The kofun is recognized as an archaeological benchmark for understanding 5th-century burial practices. Detailed findings were compiled by {{ill|Morikazu Gotō|ja|後藤守一}} in 1936. == Designations == * Important Cultural Property of Japan (Excavated artifacts): The artifacts are preserved at the {{ill|Setagaya Local History Museum|ja|世田谷区立郷土資料館}} and the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. * Tokyo Designated Historic Site: Designated as a Historic Site on February 6, 1975.{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.syougai.metro.tokyo.jp/iseki0/iseki/list/ruins/13112/112ruins.htm |title=世田谷区-遺跡一覧 東京都遺跡地図情報インターネット提供サービス |publisher=[[東京都教育委員会]] |accessdate=2016-07-03}} == Related Information == * The landscape stamp of {{ill|Setagaya Noge Post Office|ja|世田谷野毛郵便局}} features Noge Ōtsuka Kofun.[https://www.post.japanpost.jp/kitte_hagaki/stamp/fuke/detail.php?id=468 Landscape Stamp: Setagaya Noge Post Office] (Japan Post). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Noge-Otsuka Kofun}} * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/00011732 It is scallop-shaped and designated as a {{ill|Tokyo Designated Historic Site|ja|東京都指定史跡}}. Artifacts excavated from the tumulus are designated as Important Cultural Property of Japan. == location == Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is situated in {{ill|Tamagawa Nogemachi Park|ja|玉川野毛町公園}}, within the {{ill|Musashino Plateau|ja|武蔵野台地}} at an elevation of approximately 30–33 meters.{{Cite book|和書|author=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「城南の遺跡」-世田谷周辺発掘調査最新報告- |publisher=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1993|pages=2-3}} Known since the late Edo period, it was historically referred to as "Higashi Ōtsuka." The tumulus is part of the {{ill|Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|ja|荏原台古墳群}} and represents the region's ancient burial practices. == Overview == At 82 meters in length, with a diameter of 67 meters and a height of 11 meters, Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is one of Japan's largest scallop-shaped kofun (hotategai-shiki kofun). An excavation conducted in 1897 (Meiji 30) revealed a stone coffin, and the artifacts were transferred to the Imperial Museum (now {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}). The mound is equipped with a stone covering (fukishi) and haniwa, and a horseshoe-shaped moat surrounds it.{{Cite book|和書|author1=世田谷区教育委員会|authorlink1=世田谷区教育委員会|author2=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「世田谷の三万年」-最近の発掘調査の成果から- |publisher=世田谷区教育委員会・世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1988|page=16}} == Burial Chambers == Four burial chambers are located in the posterior circular mound. The first chamber's split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin measures 8.2 meters in length and contains numerous grave goods, including: * Weapons and armor: short armor, helmets, gorgets, shoulder guards, 19 iron swords, and 25+ arrowheads * Mirrors and ornaments: {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡}}, bronze armlets, over 2,000 beads, and over 30 combs * Stone replicas: including axes and ceremonial tools Artifacts from the box-shaped stone coffin include magatama, tubular beads, iron weapons, armor fragments, and a wide variety of stone replicas, such as bowls, plates, and knives made from steatite. == Cultural Significance == The kofun is recognized as an archaeological benchmark for understanding 5th-century burial practices. Detailed findings were compiled by {{ill|Morikazu Gotō|ja|後藤守一}} in 1936. == Designations == * Important Cultural Property of Japan (Excavated artifacts): The artifacts are preserved at the {{ill|Setagaya Local History Museum|ja|世田谷区立郷土資料館}} and the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. * Tokyo Designated Historic Site: Designated as a Historic Site on February 6, 1975.{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.syougai.metro.tokyo.jp/iseki0/iseki/list/ruins/13112/112ruins.htm |title=世田谷区-遺跡一覧 東京都遺跡地図情報インターネット提供サービス |publisher=[[東京都教育委員会]] |accessdate=2016-07-03}} == Related Noge Ōtsuka Kofun (April 10, 2010) | location=Noge 1-chome, {{ill|Setagaya, Tokyo|ja|世田谷区}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}} | coordinates={{coord|35|36|18.1|N|139|38|35.1|E|}} | Group={{ill|Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|ja|荏原台古墳群}} | Shape=Scallop-shaped kofun | ConstructionEra=Early 5th century | BurialFacility=Main chambers 1–3: split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffins in clay coffins
Chamber 4: box-shaped stone coffin | Dimensions=82 m in length; posterior circular mound diameter 67 m; height 11 m | BuriedPerson=Unknown | ExcavatedArtifacts=Short armor, helmet, gorgets, shoulder guards, iron swords (19), iron arrowheads (25+), iron knives, iron sickles, {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡}}, bronze armlets, 19 stone replicas, over 30 combs, over 2,000 beads, and a quiver or arrow case | HistoricSite={{ill|Tokyo Designated Historic Site|ja|東京都指定史跡}}: "Noge Ōtsuka Kofun" | TangibleCulturalProperties=Excavated artifacts (Important Cultural Property of Japan) | SpecialNotes= | Map2={{location map|Japan Wards of Tokyo|label=Noge
Ōtsuka Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=36|lat_sec=18.1|lon_deg=139|lon_min=38|lon_sec=35.1|mark=帆立貝形古墳-南西.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Tokyo}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|36|18.1|N|139|38|35.1|E}}}} }} '''Noge Ōtsuka Kofun''' (野毛大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Setagaya, Tokyo|ja|世田谷区}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}}. It is scallop-shaped and designated as a {{ill|Tokyo Designated Historic Site|ja|東京都指定史跡}}. Artifacts excavated from the tumulus are designated as Important Cultural Property of Japan. == location == Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is situated in {{ill|Tamagawa Nogemachi Park|ja|玉川野毛町公園}}, within the {{ill|Musashino Plateau|ja|武蔵野台地}} at an elevation of approximately 30–33 meters.{{Cite book|和書|author=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「城南の遺跡」-世田谷周辺発掘調査最新報告- |publisher=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1993|pages=2-3}} Known since the late Edo period, it was historically referred to as "Higashi Ōtsuka." It has also been called "Kaminoge Kofun" and "Todoroki Golf Links Kofun." The tumulus is part of the {{ill|Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|ja|荏原台古墳群}} and represents the region's ancient burial practices. == Overview == At 82 meters in length, with a diameter of 67 meters and a height of 11 meters, Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is one of Japan's largest scallop-shaped kofun (hotategai-shiki kofun). An excavation conducted in 1897 (Meiji 30) revealed a stone coffin, and the artifacts were transferred to the Imperial Museum (now {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}). The mound is equipped with a stone covering (fukishi) and haniwa, and a horseshoe-shaped moat surrounds it.{{Cite book|和書|author1=世田谷区教育委員会|authorlink1=世田谷区教育委員会|author2=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「世田谷の三万年」-最近の発掘調査の成果から- |publisher=世田谷区教育委員会・世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1988|page=16}} == Burial Chambers == Four burial chambers are located in the posterior circular mound. The first chamber's split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin measures 8.2 meters in length and contains numerous grave goods, including: * Weapons and armor: short armor, helmets, gorgets, shoulder guards, 19 iron swords, and 25+ arrowheads * Mirrors and ornaments: {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡}}, bronze armlets, over 2,000 beads, and over 30 combs * Stone replicas: including axes and ceremonial tools Artifacts from the box-shaped stone coffin include magatama, tubular beads, iron weapons, armor fragments, and a wide variety of stone replicas, such as bowls, plates, and knives made from steatite. == Cultural Significance == The kofun is recognized as an archaeological benchmark for understanding 5th-century burial practices. Detailed findings were compiled by {{ill|Morikazu Gotō|ja|後藤守一}} in 1936. == Designations == * Important Cultural Property of Japan (Excavated artifacts): The artifacts are preserved at the {{ill|Setagaya Local History Museum|ja|世田谷区立郷土資料館}} and the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. * Tokyo Designated Historic Site: Designated as a Historic Site on February 6, 1975.{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.syougai.metro.tokyo.jp/iseki0/iseki/list/ruins/13112/112ruins.htm |title=世田谷区-遺跡一覧 東京都遺跡地図情報インターネット提供サービス |publisher=[[東京都教育委員会]] |accessdate=2016-07-03}} == Related Information == * The landscape stamp of {{ill|Setagaya Noge Post Office|ja|世田谷野毛郵便局}} features Noge Ōtsuka Kofun.[https://www.post.japanpost.jp/kitte_hagaki/stamp/fuke/detail.php?id=468 Landscape Stamp: Setagaya Noge Post Office] (Japan Post). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Noge-Otsuka Kofun}} * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/00011732 Excavated Artifacts of Noge Ōtsuka Kofun (Setagaya City Ownership)] - Cultural Heritage Online (Agency for Cultural Affairs) * [http://www.city.setagaya.lg.jp/kurashi/107/159/778/h26/d00135671.html Experience Ancient Life at Noge Ōtsuka Kofun Festival] - Setagaya City website {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Tokyo]] An excavation conducted in 1897 (Meiji 30) revealed a stone coffin, and the artifacts were transferred to the Imperial Museum (now {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}). The mound is equipped with a stone covering (fukishi) and haniwa, and a horseshoe-shaped moat surrounds it.{{Cite book|和書|author1=世田谷区教育委員会|authorlink1=世田谷区教育委員会|author2=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「世田谷の三万年」-最近の発掘調査の成果から- |publisher=世田谷区教育委員会・世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1988|page=16}} == Burial Chambers == Four burial chambers are located in the posterior circular mound. The first chamber's split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin measures 8.2 meters in length and contains numerous grave goods, including: * Weapons and armor: short armor, helmets, gorgets, shoulder guards, 19 iron swords, and 25+ arrowheads * Mirrors and ornaments: {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡}}, bronze armlets, over 2,000 beads, and over 30 combs * Stone replicas: including axes and ceremonial tools Artifacts from the box-shaped stone coffin include magatama, tubular beads, iron weapons, armor fragments, and a wide variety of stone replicas, such as bowls, plates, and knives made from steatite. == Cultural Significance == The kofun is recognized as an archaeological benchmark for understanding 5th-century burial practices. Detailed findings were compiled by {{ill|Morikazu Gotō|ja|後藤守一}} in 1936. == Designations == * Important Cultural Property of Japan (Excavated artifacts): The artifacts are preserved at the {{ill|Setagaya Local History Museum|ja|世田谷区立郷土資料館}} and the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. * Tokyo Designated Historic Site: Designated as a Historic Site on February 6, 1975.{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.syougai.metro.tokyo.jp/iseki0/iseki/list/ruins/13112/112ruins.htm |title=世田谷区-遺跡一覧 東京都遺跡地図情報インターネット提供サービス |publisher=[[東京都教育委員会]] |accessdate=2016-07-03}} == Related {{Infobox ancient site | name=Noge Ōtsuka Kofun | Alias=Higashi Ōtsuka, Kaminoge Kofun, etc. | image=[[File:Noge Otsuka Kofun -01.jpg|260px]]
Noge Ōtsuka Kofun (April 10, 2010) | location=Noge 1-chome, {{ill|Setagaya, Tokyo|ja|世田谷区}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}} | coordinates={{coord|35|36|18.1|N|139|38|35.1|E|}} | Group={{ill|Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|ja|荏原台古墳群}} | Shape=Scallop-shaped kofun | ConstructionEra=Early 5th century | BurialFacility=Main chambers 1–3: split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffins in clay coffins
Chamber 4: box-shaped stone coffin | Dimensions=82 m in length; posterior circular mound diameter 67 m; height 11 m | BuriedPerson=Unknown | ExcavatedArtifacts=Short armor, helmet, gorgets, shoulder guards, iron swords (19), iron arrowheads (25+), iron knives, iron sickles, {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡}}, bronze armlets, 19 stone replicas, over 30 combs, over 2,000 beads, and a quiver or arrow case | HistoricSite={{ill|Tokyo Designated Historic Site|ja|東京都指定史跡}}: "Noge Ōtsuka Kofun" | TangibleCulturalProperties=Excavated artifacts (Important Cultural Property of Japan) | SpecialNotes= | Map2={{location map|Japan Wards of Tokyo|label=Noge
Ōtsuka Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=36|lat_sec=18.1|lon_deg=139|lon_min=38|lon_sec=35.1|mark=帆立貝形古墳-南西.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Tokyo}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|36|18.1|N|139|38|35.1|E}}}} }} '''Noge Ōtsuka Kofun''' (野毛大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Setagaya, Tokyo|ja|世田谷区}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}}. It is scallop-shaped and designated as a {{ill|Tokyo Designated Historic Site|ja|東京都指定史跡}}. Artifacts excavated from the tumulus are designated as Important Cultural Property of Japan. == location == Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is situated in {{ill|Tamagawa Nogemachi Park|ja|玉川野毛町公園}}, within the {{ill|Musashino Plateau|ja|武蔵野台地}} at an elevation of approximately 30–33 meters.{{Cite book|和書|author=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「城南の遺跡」-世田谷周辺発掘調査最新報告- |publisher=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1993|pages=2-3}} Known since the late Edo period, it was historically referred to as "Higashi Ōtsuka." It has also been called "Kaminoge Kofun" and "Todoroki Golf Links Kofun." The tumulus is part of the {{ill|Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|ja|荏原台古墳群}} and represents the region's ancient burial practices. == Overview == At 82 meters in length, with a diameter of 67 meters and a height of 11 meters, Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is one of Japan's largest scallop-shaped kofun (hotategai-shiki kofun). An excavation conducted in 1897 (Meiji 30) revealed a stone coffin, and the artifacts were transferred to the Imperial Museum (now {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}). The mound is equipped with a stone covering (fukishi) and haniwa, and a horseshoe-shaped moat surrounds it.{{Cite book|和書|author1=世田谷区教育委員会|authorlink1=世田谷区教育委員会|author2=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「世田谷の三万年」-最近の発掘調査の成果から- |publisher=世田谷区教育委員会・世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1988|page=16}} == Burial Chambers == Four burial chambers are located in the posterior circular mound. The first chamber's split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin measures 8.2 meters in length and contains numerous grave goods, including: * Weapons and armor: short armor, helmets, gorgets, shoulder guards, 19 iron swords, and 25+ arrowheads * Mirrors and ornaments: {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡}}, bronze armlets, over 2,000 beads, and over 30 combs * Stone replicas: including axes and ceremonial tools Artifacts from the box-shaped stone coffin include magatama, tubular beads, iron weapons, armor fragments, and a wide variety of stone replicas, such as bowls, plates, and knives made from steatite. == Cultural Significance == The kofun is recognized as an archaeological benchmark for understanding 5th-century burial practices. Detailed findings were compiled by {{ill|Morikazu Gotō|ja|後藤守一}} in 1936. == Designations == * Important Cultural Property of Japan (Excavated artifacts): The artifacts are preserved at the {{ill|Setagaya Local History Museum|ja|世田谷区立郷土資料館}} and the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. * Tokyo Designated Historic Site: Designated as a Historic Site on February 6, 1975.{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.syougai.metro.tokyo.jp/iseki0/iseki/list/ruins/13112/112ruins.htm |title=世田谷区-遺跡一覧 東京都遺跡地図情報インターネット提供サービス |publisher=[[東京都教育委員会]] |accessdate=2016-07-03}} == Related Information == * The landscape stamp of {{ill|Setagaya Noge Post Office|ja|世田谷野毛郵便局}} features Noge Ōtsuka Kofun.[https://www.post.japanpost.jp/kitte_hagaki/stamp/fuke/detail.php?id=468 Landscape Stamp: Setagaya Noge Post Office] (Japan Post). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Noge-Otsuka Kofun}} * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/00011732 Excavated Artifacts of Noge Ōtsuka Kofun (Setagaya City Ownership)] - Cultural Heritage Online (Agency for Cultural Affairs) * [http://www.city.setagaya.lg.jp/kurashi/107/159/778/h26/d00135671.html {{Infobox ancient site | name=Noge Ōtsuka Kofun | Alias=Higashi Ōtsuka, Kaminoge Kofun, etc. | image=[[File:Noge Otsuka Kofun -01.jpg|260px]]
Noge Ōtsuka Kofun (April 10, 2010) | location=Noge 1-chome, {{ill|Setagaya, Tokyo|ja|世田谷区}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}} | coordinates={{coord|35|36|18.1|N|139|38|35.1|E|}} | Group={{ill|Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|ja|荏原台古墳群}} | Shape=Scallop-shaped kofun | ConstructionEra=Early 5th century | BurialFacility=Main chambers 1–3: split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffins in clay coffins
Chamber 4: box-shaped stone coffin | Dimensions=82 m in length; posterior circular mound diameter 67 m; height 11 m | BuriedPerson=Unknown | ExcavatedArtifacts=Short armor, helmet, gorgets, shoulder guards, iron swords (19), iron arrowheads (25+), iron knives, iron sickles, {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡}}, bronze armlets, 19 stone replicas, over 30 combs, over 2,000 beads, and a quiver or arrow case | HistoricSite={{ill|Tokyo Designated Historic Site|ja|東京都指定史跡}}: "Noge Ōtsuka Kofun" | TangibleCulturalProperties=Excavated artifacts (Important Cultural Property of Japan) | SpecialNotes= | Map2={{location map|Japan Wards of Tokyo|label=Noge
Ōtsuka Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=36|lat_sec=18.1|lon_deg=139|lon_min=38|lon_sec=35.1|mark=帆立貝形古墳-南西.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Tokyo}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|36|18.1|N|139|38|35.1|E}}}} }} '''Noge Ōtsuka Kofun''' (野毛大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Setagaya, Tokyo|ja|世田谷区}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}}. It is scallop-shaped and designated as a {{ill|Tokyo Designated Historic Site|ja|東京都指定史跡}}. Artifacts excavated from the tumulus are designated as Important Cultural Property of Japan. == location == Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is situated in {{ill|Tamagawa Nogemachi Park|ja|玉川野毛町公園}}, within the {{ill|Musashino Plateau|ja|武蔵野台地}} at an elevation of approximately 30–33 meters.{{Cite book|和書|author=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「城南の遺跡」-世田谷周辺発掘調査最新報告- |publisher=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1993|pages=2-3}} Known since the late Edo period, it was historically referred to as "Higashi Ōtsuka." It has also been called "Kaminoge Kofun" and "Todoroki Golf Links Kofun." The tumulus is part of the {{ill|Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|ja|荏原台古墳群}} and represents the region's ancient burial practices. == Overview == At 82 meters in length, with a diameter of 67 meters and a height of 11 meters, Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is one of Japan's largest scallop-shaped kofun (hotategai-shiki kofun). An excavation conducted in 1897 (Meiji 30) revealed a stone coffin, and the artifacts were transferred to the Imperial Museum (now {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}). The mound is equipped with a stone covering (fukishi) and haniwa, and a horseshoe-shaped moat surrounds it.{{Cite book|和書|author1=世田谷区教育委員会|authorlink1=世田谷区教育委員会|author2=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「世田谷の三万年」-最近の発掘調査の成果から- |publisher=世田谷区教育委員会・世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1988|page=16}} == Burial Chambers == Four burial chambers are located in the posterior circular mound. The first chamber's split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin measures 8.2 meters in length and contains numerous grave goods, including: * Weapons and armor: short armor, helmets, gorgets, shoulder guards, 19 iron swords, and 25+ arrowheads * Mirrors and ornaments: {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡}}, bronze armlets, over 2,000 beads, and over 30 combs * Stone replicas: including axes and ceremonial tools Artifacts from the box-shaped stone coffin include magatama, tubular beads, iron weapons, armor fragments, and a wide variety of stone replicas, such as bowls, plates, and knives made from steatite. == Cultural Significance == The kofun is recognized as an archaeological benchmark for understanding 5th-century burial practices. Detailed findings were compiled by {{ill|Morikazu Gotō|ja|後藤守一}} in 1936. == Designations == * Important Cultural Property of Japan (Excavated artifacts): The artifacts are preserved at the {{ill|Setagaya Local History Museum|ja|世田谷区立郷土資料館}} and the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. * Tokyo Designated Historic Site: Designated as a Historic Site on February 6, 1975.{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.syougai.metro.tokyo.jp/iseki0/iseki/list/ruins/13112/112ruins.htm |title=世田谷区-遺跡一覧 東京都遺跡地図情報インターネット提供サービス |publisher=[[東京都教育委員会]] |accessdate=2016-07-03}} == Related Information == * The landscape stamp of {{ill|Setagaya Noge Post Office|ja|世田谷野毛郵便局}} features Noge Ōtsuka Kofun.[https://www.post.japanpost.jp/kitte_hagaki/stamp/fuke/detail.php?id=468 Landscape Stamp: Setagaya Noge Post Office] (Japan Post). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Noge-Otsuka Kofun}} * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/00011732 Excavated Artifacts of Noge Ōtsuka Kofun (Setagaya City Ownership)] - Cultural Heritage Online (Agency for Cultural Affairs) * [http://www.city.setagaya.lg.jp/kurashi/107/159/778/h26/d00135671.html Experience Ancient Life at Noge Ōtsuka Kofun Festival] - Setagaya City website {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Tokyo]] {{Infobox ancient site | name=Noge Ōtsuka Kofun | Alias=Higashi Ōtsuka, Kaminoge Kofun, etc. | image=[[File:Noge Otsuka Kofun -01.jpg|260px]]
Noge Ōtsuka Kofun (April 10, 2010) | location=Noge 1-chome, {{ill|Setagaya, Tokyo|ja|世田谷区}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}} | coordinates={{coord|35|36|18.1|N|139|38|35.1|E|}} | Group={{ill|Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|ja|荏原台古墳群}} | Shape=Scallop-shaped kofun | ConstructionEra=Early 5th century | BurialFacility=Main chambers 1–3: split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffins in clay coffins
Chamber 4: box-shaped stone coffin | Dimensions=82 m in length; posterior circular mound diameter 67 m; height 11 m | BuriedPerson=Unknown | ExcavatedArtifacts=Short armor, helmet, gorgets, shoulder guards, iron swords (19), iron arrowheads (25+), iron knives, iron sickles, {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡}}, bronze armlets, 19 stone replicas, over 30 combs, over 2,000 beads, and a quiver or arrow case | HistoricSite={{ill|Tokyo Designated Historic Site|ja|東京都指定史跡}}: "Noge Ōtsuka Kofun" | TangibleCulturalProperties=Excavated artifacts (Important Cultural Property of Japan) | SpecialNotes= | Map2={{location map|Japan Wards of Tokyo|label=Noge
Ōtsuka Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=36|lat_sec=18.1|lon_deg=139|lon_min=38|lon_sec=35.1|mark=帆立貝形古墳-南西.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Tokyo}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|36|18.1|N|139|38|35.1|E}}}} }} '''Noge Ōtsuka Kofun''' (野毛大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Setagaya, Tokyo|ja|世田谷区}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}}. It is scallop-shaped and designated as a {{ill|Tokyo Designated Historic Site|ja|東京都指定史跡}}. Artifacts excavated from the tumulus are designated as Important Cultural Property of Japan. == location == Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is situated in {{ill|Tamagawa Nogemachi Park|ja|玉川野毛町公園}}, within the {{ill|Musashino Plateau|ja|武蔵野台地}} at an elevation of approximately 30–33 meters.{{Cite book|和書|author=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「城南の遺跡」-世田谷周辺発掘調査最新報告- |publisher=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1993|pages=2-3}} Known since the late Edo period, it was historically referred to as "Higashi Ōtsuka." It has also been called "Kaminoge Kofun" and "Todoroki Golf Links Kofun." The tumulus is part of the {{ill|Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|ja|荏原台古墳群}} and represents the region's ancient burial practices. == Overview == At 82 meters in length, with a diameter of 67 meters and a height of 11 meters, Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is one of Japan's largest scallop-shaped kofun (hotategai-shiki kofun). An excavation conducted in 1897 (Meiji 30) revealed a stone coffin, and the artifacts were transferred to the Imperial Museum (now {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}). The mound is equipped with a stone covering (fukishi) and haniwa, and a horseshoe-shaped moat surrounds it.{{Cite book|和書|author1=世田谷区教育委員会|authorlink1=世田谷区教育委員会|author2=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「世田谷の三万年」-最近の発掘調査の成果から- |publisher=世田谷区教育委員会・世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1988|page=16}} == Burial Chambers == Four burial chambers are located in the posterior circular mound. The first chamber's split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin measures 8.2 meters in length and contains numerous grave goods, including: * Weapons and armor: short armor, helmets, gorgets, shoulder guards, 19 iron swords, and 25+ arrowheads * Mirrors and ornaments: {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡}}, bronze armlets, over 2,000 beads, and over 30 combs * Stone replicas: including axes and ceremonial tools Artifacts from the box-shaped stone coffin include magatama, tubular beads, iron weapons, armor fragments, and a wide variety of stone replicas, such as bowls, plates, and knives made from steatite. == Cultural Significance == The kofun is recognized as an archaeological benchmark for understanding 5th-century burial practices. Detailed findings were compiled by {{ill|Morikazu Gotō|ja|後藤守一}} in 1936. == Designations == * Important Cultural Property of Japan (Excavated artifacts): The artifacts are preserved at the {{ill|Setagaya Local History Museum|ja|世田谷区立郷土資料館}} and the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. * Tokyo Designated Historic Site: Designated as a Historic Site on February 6, 1975.{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.syougai.metro.tokyo.jp/iseki0/iseki/list/ruins/13112/112ruins.htm |title=世田谷区-遺跡一覧 東京都遺跡地図情報インターネット提供サービス |publisher=[[東京都教育委員会]] |accessdate=2016-07-03}} == Related Information == * The landscape stamp of {{ill|Setagaya Noge Post Office|ja|世田谷野毛郵便局}} features Noge Ōtsuka Kofun.[https://www.post.japanpost.jp/kitte_hagaki/stamp/fuke/detail.php?id=468 Landscape Stamp: Setagaya Noge Post Office] (Japan Post). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Noge-Otsuka Kofun}} * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/00011732 Excavated Artifacts of Noge Ōtsuka Kofun (Setagaya City Ownership)] - Cultural Heritage Online (Agency for Cultural Affairs) * [http://www.city.setagaya.lg.jp/kurashi/107/159/778/h26/d00135671.html Experience Ancient Life at Noge Ōtsuka Kofun Festival] - Setagaya City website {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Tokyo]] {{Infobox ancient site | name=Noge Ōtsuka Kofun | Alias=Higashi Ōtsuka, Kaminoge Kofun, etc. | image=[[File:Noge Otsuka Kofun -01.jpg|260px]]
Noge Ōtsuka Kofun (April 10, 2010) | location=Noge 1-chome, {{ill|Setagaya, Tokyo|ja|世田谷区}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}} | coordinates={{coord|35|36|18.1|N|139|38|35.1|E|}} | Group={{ill|Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|ja|荏原台古墳群}} | Shape=Scallop-shaped kofun | ConstructionEra=Early 5th century | BurialFacility=Main chambers 1–3: split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffins in clay coffins
Chamber 4: box-shaped stone coffin | Dimensions=82 m in length; posterior circular mound diameter 67 m; height 11 m | BuriedPerson=Unknown | ExcavatedArtifacts=Short armor, helmet, gorgets, shoulder guards, iron swords (19), iron arrowheads (25+), iron knives, iron sickles, {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡}}, bronze armlets, 19 stone replicas, over 30 combs, over 2,000 beads, and a quiver or arrow case | HistoricSite={{ill|Tokyo Designated Historic Site|ja|東京都指定史跡}}: "Noge Ōtsuka Kofun" | TangibleCulturalProperties=Excavated artifacts (Important Cultural Property of Japan) | SpecialNotes= | Map2={{location map|Japan Wards of Tokyo|label=Noge
Ōtsuka Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=36|lat_sec=18.1|lon_deg=139|lon_min=38|lon_sec=35.1|mark=帆立貝形古墳-南西.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Tokyo}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|36|18.1|N|139|38|35.1|E}}}} }} '''Noge Ōtsuka Kofun''' (野毛大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Setagaya, Tokyo|ja|世田谷区}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}}. It is scallop-shaped and designated as a {{ill|Tokyo Designated Historic Site|ja|東京都指定史跡}}. Artifacts excavated from the tumulus are designated as Important Cultural Property of Japan. == location == Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is situated in {{ill|Tamagawa Nogemachi Park|ja|玉川野毛町公園}}, within the {{ill|Musashino Plateau|ja|武蔵野台地}} at an elevation of approximately 30–33 meters.{{Cite book|和書|author=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「城南の遺跡」-世田谷周辺発掘調査最新報告- |publisher=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1993|pages=2-3}} Known since the late Edo period, it was historically referred to as "Higashi Ōtsuka." It has also been called "Kaminoge Kofun" and "Todoroki Golf Links Kofun." The tumulus is part of the {{ill|Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|ja|荏原台古墳群}} and represents the region's ancient burial practices. == Overview == At 82 meters in length, with a diameter of 67 meters and a height of 11 meters, Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is one of Japan's largest scallop-shaped kofun (hotategai-shiki kofun). An excavation conducted in 1897 (Meiji 30) revealed a stone coffin, and the artifacts were transferred to the Imperial Museum (now {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}). The mound is equipped with a stone covering (fukishi) and haniwa, and a horseshoe-shaped moat surrounds it.{{Cite book|和書|author1=世田谷区教育委員会|authorlink1=世田谷区教育委員会|author2=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「世田谷の三万年」-最近の発掘調査の成果から- |publisher=世田谷区教育委員会・世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1988|page=16}} == Burial Chambers == Four burial chambers are located in the posterior circular mound. The first chamber's split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin measures 8.2 meters in length and contains numerous grave goods, including: * Weapons and armor: short armor, helmets, gorgets, shoulder guards, 19 iron swords, and 25+ arrowheads * Mirrors and ornaments: {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡}}, bronze armlets, over 2,000 beads, and over 30 combs * Stone replicas: including axes and ceremonial tools Artifacts from the box-shaped stone coffin include magatama, tubular beads, iron weapons, armor fragments, and a wide variety of stone replicas, such as bowls, plates, and knives made from steatite. == Cultural Significance == The kofun is recognized as an archaeological benchmark for understanding 5th-century burial practices. Detailed findings were compiled by {{ill|Morikazu Gotō|ja|後藤守一}} in 1936. == Designations == * Important Cultural Property of Japan (Excavated artifacts): The artifacts are preserved at the {{ill|Setagaya Local History Museum|ja|世田谷区立郷土資料館}} and the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. * Tokyo Designated Historic Site: Designated as a Historic Site on February 6, 1975.{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.syougai.metro.tokyo.jp/iseki0/iseki/list/ruins/13112/112ruins.htm |title=世田谷区-遺跡一覧 東京都遺跡地図情報インターネット提供サービス |publisher=[[東京都教育委員会]] |accessdate=2016-07-03}} == Related Information == * The landscape stamp of {{ill|Setagaya Noge Post Office|ja|世田谷野毛郵便局}} features Noge Ōtsuka Kofun.[https://www.post.japanpost.jp/kitte_hagaki/stamp/fuke/detail.php?id=468 Landscape Stamp: Setagaya Noge Post Office] (Japan Post). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Noge-Otsuka Kofun}} * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/00011732 Experience Ancient Life at Noge Ōtsuka Kofun Festival] - Setagaya City website {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Tokyo]] It is scallop-shaped and designated as a {{ill|Tokyo Designated Historic Site|ja|東京都指定史跡}}. Artifacts excavated from the tumulus are designated as Important Cultural Property of Japan. == location == Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is situated in {{ill|Tamagawa Nogemachi Park|ja|玉川野毛町公園}}, within the {{ill|Musashino Plateau|ja|武蔵野台地}} at an elevation of approximately 30–33 meters.{{Cite book|和書|author=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「城南の遺跡」-世田谷周辺発掘調査最新報告- |publisher=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1993|pages=2-3}} Known since the late Edo period, it was historically referred to as "Higashi Ōtsuka." It has also been called "Kaminoge Kofun" and "Todoroki Golf Links Kofun." The tumulus is part of the {{ill|Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|ja|荏原台古墳群}} and represents the region's ancient burial practices. == Overview == At 82 meters in length, with a diameter of 67 meters and a height of 11 meters, Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is one of Japan's largest scallop-shaped kofun (hotategai-shiki kofun). An excavation conducted in 1897 (Meiji 30) revealed a stone coffin, and the artifacts were transferred to the Imperial Museum (now {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}). The mound is equipped with a stone covering (fukishi) and haniwa, and a horseshoe-shaped moat surrounds it.{{Cite book|和書|author1=世田谷区教育委員会|authorlink1=世田谷区教育委員会|author2=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「世田谷の三万年」-最近の発掘調査の成果から- |publisher=世田谷区教育委員会・世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1988|page=16}} == Burial Chambers == Four burial chambers are located in the posterior circular mound. The first chamber's split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin measures 8.2 meters in length and contains numerous grave goods, including: * Weapons and armor: short armor, helmets, gorgets, shoulder guards, 19 iron swords, and 25+ arrowheads * Mirrors and ornaments: {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡}}, bronze armlets, over 2,000 beads, and over 30 combs * Stone replicas: including axes and ceremonial tools Artifacts from the box-shaped stone coffin include magatama, tubular beads, iron weapons, armor fragments, and a wide variety of stone replicas, such as bowls, plates, and knives made from steatite. == Cultural Significance == The kofun is recognized as an archaeological benchmark for understanding 5th-century burial practices. Detailed findings were compiled by {{ill|Morikazu Gotō|ja|後藤守一}} in 1936. == Designations == * Important Cultural Property of Japan (Excavated artifacts): The artifacts are preserved at the {{ill|Setagaya Local History Museum|ja|世田谷区立郷土資料館}} and the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. * Tokyo Designated Historic Site: Designated as a Historic Site on February 6, 1975.{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.syougai.metro.tokyo.jp/iseki0/iseki/list/ruins/13112/112ruins.htm |title=世田谷区-遺跡一覧 東京都遺跡地図情報インターネット提供サービス |publisher=[[東京都教育委員会]] |accessdate=2016-07-03}} == Related Ōtsuka Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=36|lat_sec=18.1|lon_deg=139|lon_min=38|lon_sec=35.1|mark=帆立貝形古墳-南西.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Tokyo}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|36|18.1|N|139|38|35.1|E}}}} }} '''Noge Ōtsuka Kofun''' (野毛大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Setagaya, Tokyo|ja|世田谷区}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}}. It is scallop-shaped and designated as a {{ill|Tokyo Designated Historic Site|ja|東京都指定史跡}}. Artifacts excavated from the tumulus are designated as Important Cultural Property of Japan. == location == Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is situated in {{ill|Tamagawa Nogemachi Park|ja|玉川野毛町公園}}, within the {{ill|Musashino Plateau|ja|武蔵野台地}} at an elevation of approximately 30–33 meters.{{Cite book|和書|author=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「城南の遺跡」-世田谷周辺発掘調査最新報告- |publisher=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1993|pages=2-3}} Known since the late Edo period, it was historically referred to as "Higashi Ōtsuka." It has also been called "Kaminoge Kofun" and "Todoroki Golf Links Kofun." The tumulus is part of the {{ill|Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|ja|荏原台古墳群}} and represents the region's ancient burial practices. == Overview == At 82 meters in length, with a diameter of 67 meters and a height of 11 meters, Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is one of Japan's largest scallop-shaped kofun (hotategai-shiki kofun). An excavation conducted in 1897 (Meiji 30) revealed a stone coffin, and the artifacts were transferred to the Imperial Museum (now {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}). The mound is equipped with a stone covering (fukishi) and haniwa, and a horseshoe-shaped moat surrounds it.{{Cite book|和書|author1=世田谷区教育委員会|authorlink1=世田谷区教育委員会|author2=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「世田谷の三万年」-最近の発掘調査の成果から- |publisher=世田谷区教育委員会・世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1988|page=16}} == Burial Chambers == Four burial chambers are located in the posterior circular mound. The first chamber's split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin measures 8.2 meters in length and contains numerous grave goods, including: * Weapons and armor: short armor, helmets, gorgets, shoulder guards, 19 iron swords, and 25+ arrowheads * Mirrors and ornaments: {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡}}, bronze armlets, over 2,000 beads, and over 30 combs * Stone replicas: including axes and ceremonial tools Artifacts from the box-shaped stone coffin include magatama, tubular beads, iron weapons, armor fragments, and a wide variety of stone replicas, such as bowls, plates, and knives made from steatite. == Cultural Significance == The kofun is recognized as an archaeological benchmark for understanding 5th-century burial practices. Detailed findings were compiled by {{ill|Morikazu Gotō|ja|後藤守一}} in 1936. == Designations == * Important Cultural Property of Japan (Excavated artifacts): The artifacts are preserved at the {{ill|Setagaya Local History Museum|ja|世田谷区立郷土資料館}} and the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. * Tokyo Designated Historic Site: Designated as a Historic Site on February 6, 1975.{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.syougai.metro.tokyo.jp/iseki0/iseki/list/ruins/13112/112ruins.htm |title=世田谷区-遺跡一覧 東京都遺跡地図情報インターネット提供サービス |publisher=[[東京都教育委員会]] |accessdate=2016-07-03}} == Related revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name=Noge Ōtsuka Kofun | Alias=Higashi Ōtsuka, Kaminoge Kofun, etc. | image=[[File:Noge Otsuka Kofun -01.jpg|260px]]
Noge Ōtsuka Kofun (April 10, 2010) | location=Noge 1-chome, {{ill|Setagaya, Tokyo|ja|世田谷区}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}} | coordinates={{coord|35|36|18.1|N|139|38|35.1|E|}} | Group={{ill|Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|ja|荏原台古墳群}} | Shape=Scallop-shaped kofun | ConstructionEra=Early 5th century | BurialFacility=Main chambers 1–3: split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffins in clay coffins
Chamber 4: box-shaped stone coffin | Dimensions=82 m in length; posterior circular mound diameter 67 m; height 11 m | BuriedPerson=Unknown | ExcavatedArtifacts=Short armor, helmet, gorgets, shoulder guards, iron swords (19), iron arrowheads (25+), iron knives, iron sickles, {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡}}, bronze armlets, 19 stone replicas, over 30 combs, over 2,000 beads, and a quiver or arrow case | HistoricSite={{ill|Tokyo Designated Historic Site|ja|東京都指定史跡}}: "Noge Ōtsuka Kofun" | TangibleCulturalProperties=Excavated artifacts (Important Cultural Property of Japan) | SpecialNotes= | Map2={{location map|Japan Wards of Tokyo|label=Noge
Ōtsuka Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=36|lat_sec=18.1|lon_deg=139|lon_min=38|lon_sec=35.1|mark=帆立貝形古墳-南西.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Tokyo}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|36|18.1|N|139|38|35.1|E}}}} }} '''Noge Ōtsuka Kofun''' (野毛大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Setagaya, Tokyo|ja|世田谷区}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}}. It is scallop-shaped and designated as a {{ill|Tokyo Designated Historic Site|ja|東京都指定史跡}}. Artifacts excavated from the tumulus are designated as Important Cultural Property of Japan. == location == Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is situated in {{ill|Tamagawa Nogemachi Park|ja|玉川野毛町公園}}, within the {{ill|Musashino Plateau|ja|武蔵野台地}} at an elevation of approximately 30–33 meters.{{Cite book|和書|author=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「城南の遺跡」-世田谷周辺発掘調査最新報告- |publisher=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1993|pages=2-3}} Known since the late Edo period, it was historically referred to as "Higashi Ōtsuka." It has also been called "Kaminoge Kofun" and "Todoroki Golf Links Kofun." The tumulus is part of the {{ill|Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|ja|荏原台古墳群}} and represents the region's ancient burial practices. == Overview == At 82 meters in length, with a diameter of 67 meters and a height of 11 meters, Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is one of Japan's largest scallop-shaped kofun (hotategai-shiki kofun). An excavation conducted in 1897 (Meiji 30) revealed a stone coffin, and the artifacts were transferred to the Imperial Museum (now {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}). The mound is equipped with a stone covering (fukishi) and haniwa, and a horseshoe-shaped moat surrounds it.{{Cite book|和書|author1=世田谷区教育委員会|authorlink1=世田谷区教育委員会|author2=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「世田谷の三万年」-最近の発掘調査の成果から- |publisher=世田谷区教育委員会・世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1988|page=16}} == Burial Chambers == Four burial chambers are located in the posterior circular mound. The first chamber's split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin measures 8.2 meters in length and contains numerous grave goods, including: * Weapons and armor: short armor, helmets, gorgets, shoulder guards, 19 iron swords, and 25+ arrowheads * Mirrors and ornaments: {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡}}, bronze armlets, over 2,000 beads, and over 30 combs * Stone replicas: including axes and ceremonial tools Artifacts from the box-shaped stone coffin include magatama, tubular beads, iron weapons, armor fragments, and a wide variety of stone replicas, such as bowls, plates, and knives made from steatite. == Cultural Significance == The kofun is recognized as an archaeological benchmark for understanding 5th-century burial practices. Detailed findings were compiled by {{ill|Morikazu Gotō|ja|後藤守一}} in 1936. == Designations == * Important Cultural Property of Japan (Excavated artifacts): The artifacts are preserved at the {{ill|Setagaya Local History Museum|ja|世田谷区立郷土資料館}} and the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. * Tokyo Designated Historic Site: Designated as a Historic Site on February 6, 1975.{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.syougai.metro.tokyo.jp/iseki0/iseki/list/ruins/13112/112ruins.htm |title=世田谷区-遺跡一覧 東京都遺跡地図情報インターネット提供サービス |publisher=[[東京都教育委員会]] |accessdate=2016-07-03}} == Related Information == * The landscape stamp of {{ill|Setagaya Noge Post Office|ja|世田谷野毛郵便局}} features Noge Ōtsuka Kofun.[https://www.post.japanpost.jp/kitte_hagaki/stamp/fuke/detail.php?id=468 Landscape Stamp: Setagaya Noge Post Office] (Japan Post). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Noge-Otsuka Kofun}} * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/00011732 Excavated Artifacts of Noge Ōtsuka Kofun (Setagaya City Ownership)] - Cultural Heritage Online (Agency for Cultural Affairs) * [http://www.city.setagaya.lg.jp/kurashi/107/159/778/h26/d00135671.html Experience Ancient Life at Noge Ōtsuka Kofun Festival] - Setagaya City website {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Tokyo]] The first chamber's split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin measures 8.2 meters in length and contains numerous grave goods, including: * Weapons and armor: short armor, helmets, gorgets, shoulder guards, 19 iron swords, and 25+ arrowheads * Mirrors and ornaments: {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡}}, bronze armlets, over 2,000 beads, and over 30 combs * Stone replicas: including axes and ceremonial tools Artifacts from the box-shaped stone coffin include magatama, tubular beads, iron weapons, armor fragments, and a wide variety of stone replicas, such as bowls, plates, and knives made from steatite. == Cultural Significance == The kofun is recognized as an archaeological benchmark for understanding 5th-century burial practices. {{ill|Setagaya Local History Museum|ja|世田谷区立郷土資料館}} and the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. * Tokyo Designated Historic Site: Designated as a Historic Site on February 6, 1975.{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.syougai.metro.tokyo.jp/iseki0/iseki/list/ruins/13112/112ruins.htm |title=世田谷区-遺跡一覧 東京都遺跡地図情報インターネット提供サービス |publisher=[[東京都教育委員会]] |accessdate=2016-07-03}} == Related revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name=Noge Ōtsuka Kofun | Alias=Higashi Ōtsuka, Kaminoge Kofun, etc. | image=[[File:Noge Otsuka Kofun -01.jpg|260px]]
Noge Ōtsuka Kofun (April 10, 2010) | location=Noge 1-chome, {{ill|Setagaya, Tokyo|ja|世田谷区}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}} | coordinates={{coord|35|36|18.1|N|139|38|35.1|E|}} | Group={{ill|Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|ja|荏原台古墳群}} | Shape=Scallop-shaped kofun | ConstructionEra=Early 5th century | BurialFacility=Main chambers 1–3: split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffins in clay coffins
Chamber 4: box-shaped stone coffin | Dimensions=82 m in length; posterior circular mound diameter 67 m; height 11 m | BuriedPerson=Unknown | ExcavatedArtifacts=Short armor, helmet, gorgets, shoulder guards, iron swords (19), iron arrowheads (25+), iron knives, iron sickles, {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡}}, bronze armlets, 19 stone replicas, over 30 combs, over 2,000 beads, and a quiver or arrow case | HistoricSite={{ill|Tokyo Designated Historic Site|ja|東京都指定史跡}}: "Noge Ōtsuka Kofun" | TangibleCulturalProperties=Excavated artifacts (Important Cultural Property of Japan) | SpecialNotes= | Map2={{location map|Japan Wards of Tokyo|label=Noge
Ōtsuka Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=36|lat_sec=18.1|lon_deg=139|lon_min=38|lon_sec=35.1|mark=帆立貝形古墳-南西.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Tokyo}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|36|18.1|N|139|38|35.1|E}}}} }} '''Noge Ōtsuka Kofun''' (野毛大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Setagaya, Tokyo|ja|世田谷区}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}}. It is scallop-shaped and designated as a {{ill|Tokyo Designated Historic Site|ja|東京都指定史跡}}. Artifacts excavated from the tumulus are designated as Important Cultural Property of Japan. == location == Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is situated in {{ill|Tamagawa Nogemachi Park|ja|玉川野毛町公園}}, within the {{ill|Musashino Plateau|ja|武蔵野台地}} at an elevation of approximately 30–33 meters.{{Cite book|和書|author=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「城南の遺跡」-世田谷周辺発掘調査最新報告- |publisher=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1993|pages=2-3}} Known since the late Edo period, it was historically referred to as "Higashi Ōtsuka." It has also been called "Kaminoge Kofun" and "Todoroki Golf Links Kofun." The tumulus is part of the {{ill|Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|ja|荏原台古墳群}} and represents the region's ancient burial practices. == Overview == At 82 meters in length, with a diameter of 67 meters and a height of 11 meters, Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is one of Japan's largest scallop-shaped kofun (hotategai-shiki kofun). An excavation conducted in 1897 (Meiji 30) revealed a stone coffin, and the artifacts were transferred to the Imperial Museum (now {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}). The mound is equipped with a stone covering (fukishi) and haniwa, and a horseshoe-shaped moat surrounds it.{{Cite book|和書|author1=世田谷区教育委員会|authorlink1=世田谷区教育委員会|author2=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「世田谷の三万年」-最近の発掘調査の成果から- |publisher=世田谷区教育委員会・世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1988|page=16}} == Burial Chambers == Four burial chambers are located in the posterior circular mound. The first chamber's split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin measures 8.2 meters in length and contains numerous grave goods, including: * Weapons and armor: short armor, helmets, gorgets, shoulder guards, 19 iron swords, and 25+ arrowheads * Mirrors and ornaments: {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡}}, bronze armlets, over 2,000 beads, and over 30 combs * Stone replicas: including axes and ceremonial tools Artifacts from the box-shaped stone coffin include magatama, tubular beads, iron weapons, armor fragments, and a wide variety of stone replicas, such as bowls, plates, and knives made from steatite. == Cultural Significance == The kofun is recognized as an archaeological benchmark for understanding 5th-century burial practices. Detailed findings were compiled by {{ill|Morikazu Gotō|ja|後藤守一}} in 1936. == Designations == * Important Cultural Property of Japan (Excavated artifacts): The artifacts are preserved at the {{ill|Setagaya Local History Museum|ja|世田谷区立郷土資料館}} and the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. * Tokyo Designated Historic Site: Designated as a Historic Site on February 6, 1975.{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.syougai.metro.tokyo.jp/iseki0/iseki/list/ruins/13112/112ruins.htm |title=世田谷区-遺跡一覧 東京都遺跡地図情報インターネット提供サービス |publisher=[[東京都教育委員会]] |accessdate=2016-07-03}} == Related Information == * The landscape stamp of {{ill|Setagaya Noge Post Office|ja|世田谷野毛郵便局}} features Noge Ōtsuka Kofun.[https://www.post.japanpost.jp/kitte_hagaki/stamp/fuke/detail.php?id=468 Landscape Stamp: Setagaya Noge Post Office] (Japan Post). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Noge-Otsuka Kofun}} * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/00011732 Excavated Artifacts of Noge Ōtsuka Kofun (Setagaya City Ownership)] - Cultural Heritage Online (Agency for Cultural Affairs) * [http://www.city.setagaya.lg.jp/kurashi/107/159/778/h26/d00135671.html Experience Ancient Life at Noge Ōtsuka Kofun Festival] - Setagaya City website {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Tokyo]] revision.comment: Adding [[Category:帆立貝形古墳]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:帆立貝形古墳]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
{{Infobox ancient site | name=Noge Ōtsuka Kofun | Alias=Higashi Ōtsuka, Kaminoge Kofun, etc. | image=[[File:Noge Otsuka Kofun -01.jpg|260px]]
Noge Ōtsuka Kofun (April 10, 2010) | location=Noge 1-chome, {{ill|Setagaya, Tokyo|ja|世田谷区}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}} | coordinates={{coord|35|36|18.1|N|139|38|35.1|E|}} | Group={{ill|Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|ja|荏原台古墳群}} | Shape=Scallop-shaped kofun | ConstructionEra=Early 5th century | BurialFacility=Main chambers 1–3: split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffins in clay coffins
Chamber 4: box-shaped stone coffin | Dimensions=82 m in length; posterior circular mound diameter 67 m; height 11 m | BuriedPerson=Unknown | ExcavatedArtifacts=Short armor, helmet, gorgets, shoulder guards, iron swords (19), iron arrowheads (25+), iron knives, iron sickles, {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡}}, bronze armlets, 19 stone replicas, over 30 combs, over 2,000 beads, and a quiver or arrow case | HistoricSite={{ill|Tokyo Designated Historic Site|ja|東京都指定史跡}}: "Noge Ōtsuka Kofun" | TangibleCulturalProperties=Excavated artifacts (Important Cultural Property of Japan) | SpecialNotes= | Map2={{location map|Japan Wards of Tokyo|label=Noge
Ōtsuka Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=36|lat_sec=18.1|lon_deg=139|lon_min=38|lon_sec=35.1|mark=帆立貝形古墳-南西.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Tokyo}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|36|18.1|N|139|38|35.1|E}}}} }} '''Noge Ōtsuka Kofun''' (野毛大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Setagaya, Tokyo|ja|世田谷区}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}}. It is scallop-shaped and designated as a {{ill|Tokyo Designated Historic Site|ja|東京都指定史跡}}. Artifacts excavated from the tumulus are designated as Important Cultural Property of Japan. == location == Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is situated in {{ill|Tamagawa Nogemachi Park|ja|玉川野毛町公園}}, within the {{ill|Musashino Plateau|ja|武蔵野台地}} at an elevation of approximately 30–33 meters.{{Cite book|和書|author=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「城南の遺跡」-世田谷周辺発掘調査最新報告- |publisher=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1993|pages=2-3}} Known since the late Edo period, it was historically referred to as "Higashi Ōtsuka." It has also been called "Kaminoge Kofun" and "Todoroki Golf Links Kofun." The tumulus is part of the {{ill|Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|ja|荏原台古墳群}} and represents the region's ancient burial practices. == Overview == At 82 meters in length, with a diameter of 67 meters and a height of 11 meters, Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is one of Japan's largest scallop-shaped kofun (hotategai-shiki kofun). An excavation conducted in 1897 (Meiji 30) revealed a stone coffin, and the artifacts were transferred to the Imperial Museum (now {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}). The mound is equipped with a stone covering (fukishi) and haniwa, and a horseshoe-shaped moat surrounds it.{{Cite book|和書|author1=世田谷区教育委員会|authorlink1=世田谷区教育委員会|author2=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「世田谷の三万年」-最近の発掘調査の成果から- |publisher=世田谷区教育委員会・世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1988|page=16}} == Burial Chambers == Four burial chambers are located in the posterior circular mound. The first chamber's split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin measures 8.2 meters in length and contains numerous grave goods, including: * Weapons and armor: short armor, helmets, gorgets, shoulder guards, 19 iron swords, and 25+ arrowheads * Mirrors and ornaments: {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡}}, bronze armlets, over 2,000 beads, and over 30 combs * Stone replicas: including axes and ceremonial tools Artifacts from the box-shaped stone coffin include magatama, tubular beads, iron weapons, armor fragments, and a wide variety of stone replicas, such as bowls, plates, and knives made from steatite. == Cultural Significance == The kofun is recognized as an archaeological benchmark for understanding 5th-century burial practices. Detailed findings were compiled by {{ill|Morikazu Gotō|ja|後藤守一}} in 1936. == Designations == * Important Cultural Property of Japan (Excavated artifacts): The artifacts are preserved at the {{ill|Setagaya Local History Museum|ja|世田谷区立郷土資料館}} and the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. * Tokyo Designated Historic Site: Designated as a Historic Site on February 6, 1975.{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.syougai.metro.tokyo.jp/iseki0/iseki/list/ruins/13112/112ruins.htm |title=世田谷区-遺跡一覧 東京都遺跡地図情報インターネット提供サービス |publisher=[[東京都教育委員会]] |accessdate=2016-07-03}} == Related Information == * The landscape stamp of {{ill|Setagaya Noge Post Office|ja|世田谷野毛郵便局}} features Noge Ōtsuka Kofun.[https://www.post.japanpost.jp/kitte_hagaki/stamp/fuke/detail.php?id=468 Landscape Stamp: Setagaya Noge Post Office] (Japan Post). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Noge-Otsuka Kofun}} * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/00011732 Excavated Artifacts of Noge Ōtsuka Kofun (Setagaya City Ownership)] - Cultural Heritage Online (Agency for Cultural Affairs) * [http://www.city.setagaya.lg.jp/kurashi/107/159/778/h26/d00135671.html Experience Ancient Life at Noge Ōtsuka Kofun Festival] - Setagaya City website {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Tokyo]] Noge Ōtsuka Kofun (April 10, 2010) | location=Noge 1-chome, {{ill|Setagaya, Tokyo|ja|世田谷区}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}} | coordinates={{coord|35|36|18.1|N|139|38|35.1|E|}} | Group={{ill|Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|ja|荏原台古墳群}} | Shape=Scallop-shaped kofun | ConstructionEra=Early 5th century | BurialFacility=Main chambers 1–3: split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffins in clay coffins
Chamber 4: box-shaped stone coffin | Dimensions=82 m in length; posterior circular mound diameter 67 m; height 11 m | BuriedPerson=Unknown | ExcavatedArtifacts=Short armor, helmet, gorgets, shoulder guards, iron swords (19), iron arrowheads (25+), iron knives, iron sickles, {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡}}, bronze armlets, 19 stone replicas, over 30 combs, over 2,000 beads, and a quiver or arrow case | HistoricSite={{ill|Tokyo Designated Historic Site|ja|東京都指定史跡}}: "Noge Ōtsuka Kofun" | TangibleCulturalProperties=Excavated artifacts (Important Cultural Property of Japan) | SpecialNotes= | Map2={{location map|Japan Wards of Tokyo|label=Noge
Ōtsuka Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=36|lat_sec=18.1|lon_deg=139|lon_min=38|lon_sec=35.1|mark=帆立貝形古墳-南西.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Tokyo}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|36|18.1|N|139|38|35.1|E}}}} }} '''Noge Ōtsuka Kofun''' (野毛大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Setagaya, Tokyo|ja|世田谷区}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}}. It is scallop-shaped and designated as a {{ill|Tokyo Designated Historic Site|ja|東京都指定史跡}}. Artifacts excavated from the tumulus are designated as Important Cultural Property of Japan. == location == Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is situated in {{ill|Tamagawa Nogemachi Park|ja|玉川野毛町公園}}, within the {{ill|Musashino Plateau|ja|武蔵野台地}} at an elevation of approximately 30–33 meters.{{Cite book|和書|author=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「城南の遺跡」-世田谷周辺発掘調査最新報告- |publisher=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1993|pages=2-3}} Known since the late Edo period, it was historically referred to as "Higashi Ōtsuka." It has also been called "Kaminoge Kofun" and "Todoroki Golf Links Kofun." The tumulus is part of the {{ill|Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|ja|荏原台古墳群}} and represents the region's ancient burial practices. == Overview == At 82 meters in length, with a diameter of 67 meters and a height of 11 meters, Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is one of Japan's largest scallop-shaped kofun (hotategai-shiki kofun). An excavation conducted in 1897 (Meiji 30) revealed a stone coffin, and the artifacts were transferred to the Imperial Museum (now {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}). The mound is equipped with a stone covering (fukishi) and haniwa, and a horseshoe-shaped moat surrounds it.{{Cite book|和書|author1=世田谷区教育委員会|authorlink1=世田谷区教育委員会|author2=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「世田谷の三万年」-最近の発掘調査の成果から- |publisher=世田谷区教育委員会・世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1988|page=16}} == Burial Chambers == Four burial chambers are located in the posterior circular mound. The first chamber's split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin measures 8.2 meters in length and contains numerous grave goods, including: * Weapons and armor: short armor, helmets, gorgets, shoulder guards, 19 iron swords, and 25+ arrowheads * Mirrors and ornaments: {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡}}, bronze armlets, over 2,000 beads, and over 30 combs * Stone replicas: including axes and ceremonial tools Artifacts from the box-shaped stone coffin include magatama, tubular beads, iron weapons, armor fragments, and a wide variety of stone replicas, such as bowls, plates, and knives made from steatite. == Cultural Significance == The kofun is recognized as an archaeological benchmark for understanding 5th-century burial practices. Detailed findings were compiled by {{ill|Morikazu Gotō|ja|後藤守一}} in 1936. == Designations == * Important Cultural Property of Japan (Excavated artifacts): The artifacts are preserved at the {{ill|Setagaya Local History Museum|ja|世田谷区立郷土資料館}} and the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. * Tokyo Designated Historic Site: Designated as a Historic Site on February 6, 1975.{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.syougai.metro.tokyo.jp/iseki0/iseki/list/ruins/13112/112ruins.htm |title=世田谷区-遺跡一覧 東京都遺跡地図情報インターネット提供サービス |publisher=[[東京都教育委員会]] |accessdate=2016-07-03}} == Related Information == * The landscape stamp of {{ill|Setagaya Noge Post Office|ja|世田谷野毛郵便局}} features Noge Ōtsuka Kofun.[https://www.post.japanpost.jp/kitte_hagaki/stamp/fuke/detail.php?id=468 Landscape Stamp: Setagaya Noge Post Office] (Japan Post). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Noge-Otsuka Kofun}} * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/00011732 Excavated Artifacts of Noge Ōtsuka Kofun (Setagaya City Ownership)] - Cultural Heritage Online (Agency for Cultural Affairs) * [http://www.city.setagaya.lg.jp/kurashi/107/159/778/h26/d00135671.html Experience Ancient Life at Noge Ōtsuka Kofun Festival] - Setagaya City website {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:kk]][[Category:dd]][[Category:kk]][[Category:Kofun in Tokyo]] Chamber 4: box-shaped stone coffin | Dimensions=82 m in length; posterior circular mound diameter 67 m; height 11 m | BuriedPerson=Unknown | ExcavatedArtifacts=Short armor, helmet, gorgets, shoulder guards, iron swords (19), iron arrowheads (25+), iron knives, iron sickles, {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡}}, bronze armlets, 19 stone replicas, over 30 combs, over 2,000 beads, and a quiver or arrow case | HistoricSite={{ill|Tokyo Designated Historic Site|ja|東京都指定史跡}}: "Noge Ōtsuka Kofun" | TangibleCulturalProperties=Excavated artifacts (Important Cultural Property of Japan) | SpecialNotes= | Map2={{location map|Japan Wards of Tokyo|label=Noge
Ōtsuka Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=36|lat_sec=18.1|lon_deg=139|lon_min=38|lon_sec=35.1|mark=帆立貝形古墳-南西.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Tokyo}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|36|18.1|N|139|38|35.1|E}}}} }} '''Noge Ōtsuka Kofun''' (野毛大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳}} located in {{ill|Setagaya, Tokyo|ja|世田谷区}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}}. It is scallop-shaped and designated as a {{ill|Tokyo Designated Historic Site|ja|東京都指定史跡}}. Artifacts excavated from the tumulus are designated as Important Cultural Property of Japan. == location == Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is situated in {{ill|Tamagawa Nogemachi Park|ja|玉川野毛町公園}}, within the {{ill|Musashino Plateau|ja|武蔵野台地}} at an elevation of approximately 30–33 meters.{{Cite book|和書|author=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「城南の遺跡」-世田谷周辺発掘調査最新報告- |publisher=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1993|pages=2-3}} Known since the late Edo period, it was historically referred to as "Higashi Ōtsuka." It has also been called "Kaminoge Kofun" and "Todoroki Golf Links Kofun." The tumulus is part of the {{ill|Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|ja|荏原台古墳群}} and represents the region's ancient burial practices. == Overview == At 82 meters in length, with a diameter of 67 meters and a height of 11 meters, Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is one of Japan's largest scallop-shaped kofun (hotategai-shiki kofun). An excavation conducted in 1897 (Meiji 30) revealed a stone coffin, and the artifacts were transferred to the Imperial Museum (now {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}). The mound is equipped with a stone covering (fukishi) and haniwa, and a horseshoe-shaped moat surrounds it.{{Cite book|和書|author1=世田谷区教育委員会|authorlink1=世田谷区教育委員会|author2=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「世田谷の三万年」-最近の発掘調査の成果から- |publisher=世田谷区教育委員会・世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1988|page=16}} == Burial Chambers == Four burial chambers are located in the posterior circular mound. The first chamber's split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin measures 8.2 meters in length and contains numerous grave goods, including: * Weapons and armor: short armor, helmets, gorgets, shoulder guards, 19 iron swords, and 25+ arrowheads * Mirrors and ornaments: {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡}}, bronze armlets, over 2,000 beads, and over 30 combs * Stone replicas: including axes and ceremonial tools Artifacts from the box-shaped stone coffin include magatama, tubular beads, iron weapons, armor fragments, and a wide variety of stone replicas, such as bowls, plates, and knives made from steatite. == Cultural Significance == The kofun is recognized as an archaeological benchmark for understanding 5th-century burial practices. Detailed findings were compiled by {{ill|Morikazu Gotō|ja|後藤守一}} in 1936. == Designations == * Important Cultural Property of Japan (Excavated artifacts): The artifacts are preserved at the {{ill|Setagaya Local History Museum|ja|世田谷区立郷土資料館}} and the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. * Tokyo Designated Historic Site: Designated as a Historic Site on February 6, 1975.{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.syougai.metro.tokyo.jp/iseki0/iseki/list/ruins/13112/112ruins.htm |title=世田谷区-遺跡一覧 東京都遺跡地図情報インターネット提供サービス |publisher=[[東京都教育委員会]] |accessdate=2016-07-03}} == Related
