{{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} | name=Noge Ōtsuka Kofun | Alias=Higashi Ōtsuka, Kaminoge Kofun, etc. | image=[[File:Noge Otsuka Kofun -01.jpg|260px]]
Noge Ōtsuka Kofun (April 10, 2010) | location=Noge 1-chome, {{ill|Setagaya, Tokyo|ja|世田谷区}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}} | coordinates={{coord|35|36|18.1|N|139|38|35.1|E|}} | Group={{ill|Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|ja|荏原台古墳群}} | Shape=Scallop-shaped kofun | ConstructionEra=Early 5th century | BurialFacility=Main chambers 1–3: split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffins in clay coffins
Chamber 4: box-shaped stone coffin | Dimensions=82 m in length; posterior circular mound diameter 67 m; height 11 m | BuriedPerson=Unknown | ExcavatedArtifacts=Short armor, helmet, gorgets, shoulder guards, iron swords (19), iron arrowheads (25+), iron knives, iron sickles, {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡}}, bronze armlets, 19 stone replicas, over 30 combs, over 2,000 beads, and a quiver or arrow case | HistoricSite={{ill|Tokyo Designated Historic Site|ja|東京都指定史跡}}: "Noge Ōtsuka Kofun" | TangibleCulturalProperties=Excavated artifacts (Important Cultural Property of Japan) | SpecialNotes= | Map2={{location map|Japan Wards of Tokyo|label=Noge
Ōtsuka Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=36|lat_sec=18.1|lon_deg=139|lon_min=38|lon_sec=35.1|mark=帆立貝形古墳-南西.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Tokyo}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|36|18.1|N|139|38|35.1|E}}}} }} '''Noge Ōtsuka Kofun''' (野毛大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Setagaya, Tokyo|ja|世田谷区}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}}. It is scallop-shaped and designated as a {{ill|Tokyo Designated Historic Site|ja|東京都指定史跡}}. Artifacts excavated from the tumulus are designated as Important Cultural Property of Japan. == location == Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is situated in {{ill|Tamagawa Nogemachi Park|ja|玉川野毛町公園}}, within the {{ill|Musashino Plateau|ja|武蔵野台地}} at an elevation of [[Category:pp error]]approximately 30–33 meters.{{Cite book|和書|author=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「城南の遺跡」-世田谷周辺発掘調査最新報告- |publisher=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1993|pages=2-3}} Known since the late Edo period, it was historically referred to as "Higashi Ōtsuka." It has also been called "Kaminoge Kofun" and "Todoroki Golf Links Kofun." The tumulus is part of the {{ill|Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|ja|荏原台古墳群}} and represents the region's ancient burial practices. == Overview == At 82 meters in length, with a diameter of 67 meters and a height of 11 meters, Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is one of Japan's largest scallop-shaped kofun (hotategai-shiki kofun). An excavation conducted in 1897 (Meiji 30) revealed a stone coffin, and the artifacts were transferred to the Imperial Museum (now {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}). The mound is equi11ed with a stone covering (fukishi) and haniwa, and a horseshoe-shaped moat surrounds it.{{Cite book|和書|author1=世田谷区教育委員会|authorlink1=世田谷区教育委員会|author2=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「世田谷の三万年」-最近の発掘調査の成果から- |publisher=世田谷区教育委員会・世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1988|page=16}} == Burial Chambers == Four burial chambers are located in the posterior circular mound. The first chamber's split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin measures 8.2 meters in length and contains numerous grave goods, including: * Weapons and armor: short armor, helmets, gorgets, shoulder guards, 19 iron swords, and 25+ arrowheads * Mirrors and ornaments: {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡}}, bronze armlets, over 2,000 beads, and over 30 combs * Stone replicas: including axes and ceremonial tools Artifacts from the box-shaped stone coffin include magatama, tubular beads, iron weapons, armor fragments, and a wide variety of stone replicas, such as bowls, plates, and knives made from steatite. == Cultural Significance == The kofun is recognized as an archaeological benchmark for understanding 5th-century burial practices. Detailed findings were compiled by {{ill|Morikazu Gotō|ja|後藤守一}} in 1936. == Designations == * Important Cultural Property of Japan (Excavated artifacts): The artifacts are preserved at the {{ill|Setagaya Local History Museum|ja|世田谷区立郷土資料館}} and the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. * Tokyo Designated Historic Site: Designated as a Historic Site on February 6, 1975.{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.syougai.metro.tokyo.jp/iseki0/iseki/list/ruins/13112/112ruins.htm |title=世田谷区-遺跡一覧 東京都遺跡地図情報インターネット提供サービス |publisher=[[東京都教育委員会]] |accessdate=2016-07-03}} == Related Information == * The landscape stamp of {{ill|Setagaya Noge Post Office|ja|世田谷野毛郵便局}} features Noge Ōtsuka Kofun.[https://www.post.japanpost.jp/kitte_hagaki/stamp/fuke/detail.php?id=468 Landscape Stamp: Setagaya Noge Post Office] (Japan Post). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Noge-Otsuka Kofun}} * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/00011732 Excavated Artifacts of Noge Ōtsuka Kofun (Setagaya City Ownership)] - Cultural Heritage Online (Agency for Cultural Affairs) * [http://www.city.setagaya.lg.jp/kurashi/107/159/778/h26/d00135671.html Experience Ancient Life at Noge Ōtsuka Kofun Festival] - Setagaya City website {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Kofun in Tokyo]] [[Category:History of Setagaya]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] It is scallop-shaped and designated as a {{ill|Tokyo Designated Historic Site|ja|東京都指定史跡}}. It has also been called "Kaminoge Kofun" and "Todoroki Golf Links Kofun." An excavation conducted in 1897 (Meiji 30) revealed a stone coffin, and the artifacts were transferred to the Imperial Museum (now {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}). The mound is equi11ed with a stone covering (fukishi) and haniwa, and a horseshoe-shaped moat surrounds it.{{Cite book|和書|author1=世田谷区教育委員会|authorlink1=世田谷区教育委員会|author2=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「世田谷の三万年」-最近の発掘調査の成果から- |publisher=世田谷区教育委員会・世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1988|page=16}} == Burial Chambers == Four burial chambers are located in the posterior circular mound. The first chamber's split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin measures 8.2 meters in length and contains numerous grave goods, including: * Weapons and armor: short armor, helmets, gorgets, shoulder guards, 19 iron swords, and 25+ arrowheads * Mirrors and ornaments: {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡}}, bronze armlets, over 2,000 beads, and over 30 combs * Stone replicas: including axes and ceremonial tools Artifacts from the box-shaped stone coffin include magatama, tubular beads, iron weapons, armor fragments, and a wide variety of stone replicas, such as bowls, plates, and knives made from steatite. == Cultural Significance == The kofun is recognized as an archaeological benchmark for understanding 5th-century burial practices. Detailed findings were compiled by {{ill|Morikazu Gotō|ja|後藤守一}} in 1936. == Designations == * Important Cultural Property of Japan (Excavated artifacts): The artifacts are preserved at the {{ill|Setagaya Local History Museum|ja|世田谷区立郷土資料館}} and the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. * Tokyo Designated Historic Site: Designated as a Historic Site on February 6, 1975.{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.syougai.metro.tokyo.jp/iseki0/iseki/list/ruins/13112/112ruins.htm |title=世田谷区-遺跡一覧 東京都遺跡地図情報インターネット提供サービス |publisher=[[東京都教育委員会]] |accessdate=2016-07-03}} == Related revision.comment: doing more mass replacements to fix the issues
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=now we have a 'Burial Facility' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} | name=Noge Ōtsuka Kofun | Alias=Higashi Ōtsuka, Kaminoge Kofun, etc. | image=[[File:Noge Otsuka Kofun -01.jpg|260px]]
Noge Ōtsuka Kofun (April 10, 2010) | location=Noge 1-chome, {{ill|Setagaya, Tokyo|ja|世田谷区}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}} | coordinates={{coord|35|36|18.1|N|139|38|35.1|E|}} | Group={{ill|Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|ja|荏原台古墳群}} | Shape=Scallop-shaped kofun | ConstructionEra=Early 5th century | BurialFacility=Main chambers 1–3: split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffins in clay coffins
Chamber 4: box-shaped stone coffin | Dimensions=82 m in length; posterior circular mound diameter 67 m; height 11 m | BuriedPerson=Unknown | ExcavatedArtifacts=Short armor, helmet, gorgets, shoulder guards, iron swords (19), iron arrowheads (25+), iron knives, iron sickles, {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡}}, bronze armlets, 19 stone replicas, over 30 combs, over 2,000 beads, and a quiver or arrow case | HistoricSite={{ill|Tokyo Designated Historic Site|ja|東京都指定史跡}}: "Noge Ōtsuka Kofun" | TangibleCulturalProperties=excavations (Important Cultural Property of Japan) | SpecialNotes= | map={{location map|Japan Wards of Tokyo|label=Noge
Ōtsuka Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=36|lat_sec=18.1|lon_deg=139|lon_min=38|lon_sec=35.1|mark=帆立貝形古墳-南西.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Tokyo}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|36|18.1|N|139|38|35.1|E}}}} }} '''Noge Ōtsuka Kofun''' (野毛大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Setagaya, Tokyo|ja|世田谷区}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}}. It is scallop-shaped and designated as a {{ill|Tokyo Designated Historic Site|ja|東京都指定史跡}}. Artifacts excavated from the tumulus are designated as Important Cultural Property of Japan. == location == Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is situated in {{ill|Tamagawa Nogemachi Park|ja|玉川野毛町公園}}, within the {{ill|Musashino Plateau|ja|武蔵野台地}} at an elevation of [[Category:pp error]]approximately 30–33 meters.{{Cite book|和書|author=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「城南の遺跡」-世田谷周辺発掘調査最新報告- |publisher=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1993|pages=2-3}} Known since the late Edo period, it was historically referred to as "Higashi Ōtsuka." It has also been called "Kaminoge Kofun" and "Todoroki Golf Links Kofun." The tumulus is part of the {{ill|Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|ja|荏原台古墳群}} and represents the region's ancient burial practices. == Overview == At 82 meters in length, with a diameter of 67 meters and a height of 11 meters, Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is one of Japan's largest scallop-shaped kofun (hotategai-shiki kofun). An excavation conducted in 1897 (Meiji 30) revealed a stone coffin, and the artifacts were transferred to the Imperial Museum (now {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}). The mound is equi11ed with a stone covering (fukishi) and haniwa, and a horseshoe-shaped moat surrounds it.{{Cite book|和書|author1=世田谷区教育委員会|authorlink1=世田谷区教育委員会|author2=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「世田谷の三万年」-最近の発掘調査の成果から- |publisher=世田谷区教育委員会・世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1988|page=16}} == Burial Chambers == Four burial chambers are located in the posterior circular mound. The first chamber's split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin measures 8.2 meters in length and contains numerous grave goods, including: * Weapons and armor: short armor, helmets, gorgets, shoulder guards, 19 iron swords, and 25+ arrowheads * Mirrors and ornaments: {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡}}, bronze armlets, over 2,000 beads, and over 30 combs * Stone replicas: including axes and ceremonial tools Artifacts from the box-shaped stone coffin include magatama, tubular beads, iron weapons, armor fragments, and a wide variety of stone replicas, such as bowls, plates, and knives made from steatite. == Cultural Significance == The kofun is recognized as an archaeological benchmark for understanding 5th-century burial practices. Detailed findings were compiled by {{ill|Morikazu Gotō|ja|後藤守一}} in 1936. == Designations == * Important Cultural Property of Japan (excavations): The artifacts are preserved at the {{ill|Setagaya Local History Museum|ja|世田谷区立郷土資料館}} and the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. * Tokyo Designated Historic Site: Designated as a Historic Site on February 6, 1975.{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.syougai.metro.tokyo.jp/iseki0/iseki/list/ruins/13112/112ruins.htm |title=世田谷区-遺跡一覧 東京都遺跡地図情報インターネット提供サービス |publisher=[[東京都教育委員会]] |accessdate=2016-07-03}} == Related Information == * The landscape stamp of {{ill|Setagaya Noge Post Office|ja|世田谷野毛郵便局}} features Noge Ōtsuka Kofun.[https://www.post.japanpost.jp/kitte_hagaki/stamp/fuke/detail.php?id=468 Landscape Stamp: Setagaya Noge Post Office] (Japan Post). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Noge-Otsuka Kofun}} * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/00011732 Excavated Artifacts of Noge Ōtsuka Kofun (Setagaya City Ownership)] - Cultural Heritage Online (Agency for Cultural Affairs) * [http://www.city.setagaya.lg.jp/kurashi/107/159/778/h26/d00135671.html Experience Ancient Life at Noge Ōtsuka Kofun Festival] - Setagaya City website {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Kofun in Tokyo]] [[Category:History of Setagaya]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties of Tokyo]] [[Category:Tokyo Designated Historic Sites]] [[Category:Noge Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Noge Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:11]]{{DEFAULTSORT:Noge Ōtsuka Kofun}}[[Category:11]] Noge Ōtsuka Kofun (April 10, 2010) | location=Noge 1-chome, {{ill|Setagaya, Tokyo|ja|世田谷区}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}} | coordinates={{coord|35|36|18.1|N|139|38|35.1|E|}} | Group={{ill|Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|ja|荏原台古墳群}} | Shape=Scallop-shaped kofun | ConstructionEra=Early 5th century | BurialFacility=Main chambers 1–3: split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffins in clay coffins
Chamber 4: box-shaped stone coffin | area=82 m in length; posterior circular mound diameter 67 m; height 11 m | BuriedPerson=Unknown | ExcavatedArtifacts=Short armor, helmet, gorgets, shoulder guards, iron swords (19), iron arrowheads (25+), iron knives, iron sickles, {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡}}, bronze armlets, 19 stone replicas, over 30 combs, over 2,000 beads, and a quiver or arrow case | HistoricSite={{ill|Tokyo Designated Historic Site|ja|東京都指定史跡}}: "Noge Ōtsuka Kofun" | TangibleCulturalProperties=excavations (Important Cultural Property of Japan) | SpecialNotes= | map={{location map|Japan Wards of Tokyo|label=Noge
Ōtsuka Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=36|lat_sec=18.1|lon_deg=139|lon_min=38|lon_sec=35.1|mark=帆立貝形古墳-南西.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Tokyo}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|36|18.1|N|139|38|35.1|E}}}} }} '''Noge Ōtsuka Kofun''' (野毛大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Setagaya, Tokyo|ja|世田谷区}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}}. It is scallop-shaped and designated as a {{ill|Tokyo Designated Historic Site|ja|東京都指定史跡}}. Artifacts excavated from the tumulus are designated as Important Cultural Property of Japan. == location == Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is situated in {{ill|Tamagawa Nogemachi Park|ja|玉川野毛町公園}}, within the {{ill|Musashino Plateau|ja|武蔵野台地}} at an elevation of [[Category:pp error]]approximately 30–33 meters.{{Cite book|和書|author=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「城南の遺跡」-世田谷周辺発掘調査最新報告- |publisher=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1993|pages=2-3}} Known since the late Edo period, it was historically referred to as "Higashi Ōtsuka." It has also been called "Kaminoge Kofun" and "Todoroki Golf Links Kofun." The tumulus is part of the {{ill|Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|ja|荏原台古墳群}} and represents the region's ancient burial practices. == Overview == At 82 meters in length, with a diameter of 67 meters and a height of 11 meters, Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is one of Japan's largest scallop-shaped kofun (hotategai-shiki kofun). An excavation conducted in 1897 (Meiji 30) revealed a stone coffin, and the artifacts were transferred to the Imperial Museum (now {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}). The mound is equi11ed with a stone covering (fukishi) and haniwa, and a horseshoe-shaped moat surrounds it.{{Cite book|和書|author1=世田谷区教育委員会|authorlink1=世田谷区教育委員会|author2=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「世田谷の三万年」-最近の発掘調査の成果から- |publisher=世田谷区教育委員会・世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1988|page=16}} == Burial Chambers == Four burial chambers are located in the posterior circular mound. The first chamber's split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin measures 8.2 meters in length and contains numerous grave goods, including: * Weapons and armor: short armor, helmets, gorgets, shoulder guards, 19 iron swords, and 25+ arrowheads * Mirrors and ornaments: {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡}}, bronze armlets, over 2,000 beads, and over 30 combs * Stone replicas: including axes and ceremonial tools Artifacts from the box-shaped stone coffin include magatama, tubular beads, iron weapons, armor fragments, and a wide variety of stone replicas, such as bowls, plates, and knives made from steatite. == Cultural Significance == The kofun is recognized as an archaeological benchmark for understanding 5th-century burial practices. Detailed findings were compiled by {{ill|Morikazu Gotō|ja|後藤守一}} in 1936. == Designations == * Important Cultural Property of Japan (excavations): The artifacts are preserved at the {{ill|Setagaya Local History Museum|ja|世田谷区立郷土資料館}} and the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. * Tokyo Designated Historic Site: Designated as a Historic Site on February 6, 1975.{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.syougai.metro.tokyo.jp/iseki0/iseki/list/ruins/13112/112ruins.htm |title=世田谷区-遺跡一覧 東京都遺跡地図情報インターネット提供サービス |publisher=[[東京都教育委員会]] |accessdate=2016-07-03}} == Related Information == * The landscape stamp of {{ill|Setagaya Noge Post Office|ja|世田谷野毛郵便局}} features Noge Ōtsuka Kofun.[https://www.post.japanpost.jp/kitte_hagaki/stamp/fuke/detail.php?id=468 Landscape Stamp: Setagaya Noge Post Office] (Japan Post). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Noge-Otsuka Kofun}} * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/00011732 Excavated Artifacts of Noge Ōtsuka Kofun (Setagaya City Ownership)] - Cultural Heritage Online (Agency for Cultural Affairs) * [http://www.city.setagaya.lg.jp/kurashi/107/159/778/h26/d00135671.html Experience Ancient Life at Noge Ōtsuka Kofun Festival] - Setagaya City website {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Kofun in Tokyo]] [[Category:History of Setagaya]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:Important Cultural Noge Ōtsuka Kofun (April 10, 2010) | location=Noge 1-chome, {{ill|Setagaya, Tokyo|ja|世田谷区}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}} | coordinates={{coord|35|36|18.1|N|139|38|35.1|E|}} | Group={{ill|Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|ja|荏原台古墳群}} | type=Scallop-shaped kofun | ConstructionEra=Early 5th century | BurialFacility=Main chambers 1–3: split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffins in clay coffins
Chamber 4: box-shaped stone coffin | area=82 m in length; posterior circular mound diameter 67 m; height 11 m | BuriedPerson=Unknown | ExcavatedArtifacts=Short armor, helmet, gorgets, shoulder guards, iron swords (19), iron arrowheads (25+), iron knives, iron sickles, {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡}}, bronze armlets, 19 stone replicas, over 30 combs, over 2,000 beads, and a quiver or arrow case | HistoricSite={{ill|Tokyo Designated Historic Site|ja|東京都指定史跡}}: "Noge Ōtsuka Kofun" | TangibleCulturalProperties=excavations (Important Cultural Property of Japan) | SpecialNotes= | map={{location map|Japan Wards of Tokyo|label=Noge
Ōtsuka Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=36|lat_sec=18.1|lon_deg=139|lon_min=38|lon_sec=35.1|mark=帆立貝形古墳-南西.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Tokyo}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|36|18.1|N|139|38|35.1|E}}}} }} '''Noge Ōtsuka Kofun''' (野毛大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Setagaya, Tokyo|ja|世田谷区}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}}. It is scallop-shaped and designated as a {{ill|Tokyo Designated Historic Site|ja|東京都指定史跡}}. Artifacts excavated from the tumulus are designated as Important Cultural Property of Japan. == location == Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is situated in {{ill|Tamagawa Nogemachi Park|ja|玉川野毛町公園}}, within the {{ill|Musashino Plateau|ja|武蔵野台地}} at an elevation of [[Category:pp error]]approximately 30–33 meters.{{Cite book|和書|author=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「城南の遺跡」-世田谷周辺発掘調査最新報告- |publisher=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1993|pages=2-3}} Known since the late Edo period, it was historically referred to as "Higashi Ōtsuka." It has also been called "Kaminoge Kofun" and "Todoroki Golf Links Kofun." The tumulus is part of the {{ill|Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|ja|荏原台古墳群}} and represents the region's ancient burial practices. == Overview == At 82 meters in length, with a diameter of 67 meters and a height of 11 meters, Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is one of Japan's largest scallop-shaped kofun (hotategai-shiki kofun). An excavation conducted in 1897 (Meiji 30) revealed a stone coffin, and the artifacts were transferred to the Imperial Museum (now {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}). The mound is equi11ed with a stone covering (fukishi) and haniwa, and a horseshoe-shaped moat surrounds it.{{Cite book|和書|author1=世田谷区教育委員会|authorlink1=世田谷区教育委員会|author2=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「世田谷の三万年」-最近の発掘調査の成果から- |publisher=世田谷区教育委員会・世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1988|page=16}} == Burial Chambers == Four burial chambers are located in the posterior circular mound. The first chamber's split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin measures 8.2 meters in length and contains numerous grave goods, including: * Weapons and armor: short armor, helmets, gorgets, shoulder guards, 19 iron swords, and 25+ arrowheads * Mirrors and ornaments: {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡}}, bronze armlets, over 2,000 beads, and over 30 combs * Stone replicas: including axes and ceremonial tools Artifacts from the box-shaped stone coffin include magatama, tubular beads, iron weapons, armor fragments, and a wide variety of stone replicas, such as bowls, plates, and knives made from steatite. == Cultural Significance == The kofun is recognized as an archaeological benchmark for understanding 5th-century burial practices. Detailed findings were compiled by {{ill|Morikazu Gotō|ja|後藤守一}} in 1936. == Designations == * Important Cultural Property of Japan (excavations): The artifacts are preserved at the {{ill|Setagaya Local History Museum|ja|世田谷区立郷土資料館}} and the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. * Tokyo Designated Historic Site: Designated as a Historic Site on February 6, 1975.{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.syougai.metro.tokyo.jp/iseki0/iseki/list/ruins/13112/112ruins.htm |title=世田谷区-遺跡一覧 東京都遺跡地図情報インターネット提供サービス |publisher=[[東京都教育委員会]] |accessdate=2016-07-03}} == Related Information == * The landscape stamp of {{ill|Setagaya Noge Post Office|ja|世田谷野毛郵便局}} features Noge Ōtsuka Kofun.[https://www.post.japanpost.jp/kitte_hagaki/stamp/fuke/detail.php?id=468 Landscape Stamp: Setagaya Noge Post Office] (Japan Post). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Noge-Otsuka Kofun}} * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/00011732 burial goods of Noge Ōtsuka Noge Ōtsuka Kofun (April 10, 2010) | location=Noge 1-chome, {{ill|Setagaya, Tokyo|ja|世田谷区}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}} | coordinates={{coord|35|36|18.1|N|139|38|35.1|E|}} | Group={{ill|Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|ja|荏原台古墳群}} | type=Scallop-shaped kofun | ConstructionEra=Early 5th century | BurialFacility=Main chambers 1–3: split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffins in clay coffins
Chamber 4: box-shaped stone coffin | area=82 m in length; posterior circular mound diameter 67 m; height 11 m | BuriedPerson=Unknown | ExcavatedArtifacts=Short armor, helmet, gorgets, shoulder guards, iron swords (19), iron arrowheads (25+), iron knives, iron sickles, {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡}}, bronze armlets, 19 stone replicas, over 30 combs, over 2,000 beads, and a quiver or arrow case | HistoricSite={{ill|Tokyo Designated Historic Site|ja|東京都指定史跡}}: "Noge Ōtsuka Kofun" | TangibleCulturalProperties=excavations (Important Cultural Property of Japan) | SpecialNotes= | map={{location map|Japan Wards of Tokyo|label=Noge
Ōtsuka Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=36|lat_sec=18.1|lon_deg=139|lon_min=38|lon_sec=35.1|mark=帆立貝形古墳-南西.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Tokyo}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|36|18.1|N|139|38|35.1|E}}}} }} '''Noge Ōtsuka Kofun''' (野毛大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Setagaya, Tokyo|ja|世田谷区}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}}. It is scallop-shaped and designated as a {{ill|Tokyo Designated Historic Site|ja|東京都指定史跡}}. Artifacts excavated from the tumulus are designated as Important Cultural Property of Japan. == location == Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is situated in {{ill|Tamagawa Nogemachi Park|ja|玉川野毛町公園}}, within the {{ill|Musashino Plateau|ja|武蔵野台地}} at an elevation of [[Category:pp error]]approximately 30–33 meters.{{Cite book|和書|author=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「城南の遺跡」-世田谷周辺発掘調査最新報告- |publisher=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1993|pages=2-3}} Known since the late Edo period, it was historically referred to as "Higashi Ōtsuka." It has also been called "Kaminoge Kofun" and "Todoroki Golf Links Kofun." The tumulus is part of the {{ill|Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|ja|荏原台古墳群}} and represents the region's ancient burial practices. == Overview == At 82 meters in length, with a diameter of 67 meters and a height of 11 meters, Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is one of Japan's largest scallop-shaped kofun (hotategai-shiki kofun). An excavation conducted in 1897 (Meiji 30) revealed a stone coffin, and the artifacts were transferred to the Imperial Museum (now {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}). The mound is equi11ed with a stone covering (fukishi) and haniwa, and a horseshoe-shaped moat surrounds it.{{Cite book|和書|author1=世田谷区教育委員会|authorlink1=世田谷区教育委員会|author2=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「世田谷の三万年」-最近の発掘調査の成果から- |publisher=世田谷区教育委員会・世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1988|page=16}} == Burial Chambers == Four burial chambers are located in the posterior circular mound. The first chamber's split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin measures 8.2 meters in length and contains numerous grave goods, including: * Weapons and armor: short armor, helmets, gorgets, shoulder guards, 19 iron swords, and 25+ arrowheads * Mirrors and ornaments: {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡}}, bronze armlets, over 2,000 beads, and over 30 combs * Stone replicas: including axes and ceremonial tools Artifacts from the box-shaped stone coffin include magatama, tubular beads, iron weapons, armor fragments, and a wide variety of stone replicas, such as bowls, plates, and knives made from steatite. == Cultural Significance == The kofun is recognized as an archaeological benchmark for understanding 5th-century burial practices. Detailed findings were compiled by {{ill|Morikazu Gotō|ja|後藤守一}} in 1936. == Designations == * Important Cultural Property of Japan (excavations): The artifacts are preserved at the {{ill|Setagaya Local History Museum|ja|世田谷区立郷土資料館}} and the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. * Tokyo Designated Historic Site: Designated as a Historic Site on February 6, 1975.{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.syougai.metro.tokyo.jp/iseki0/iseki/list/ruins/13112/112ruins.htm |title=世田谷区-遺跡一覧 東京都遺跡地図情報インターネット提供サービス |publisher=[[東京都教育委員会]] |accessdate=2016-07-03}} == Related Information == * The landscape stamp of {{ill|Setagaya Noge Post Office|ja|世田谷野毛郵便局}} features Noge Ōtsuka Kofun.[https://www.post.japanpost.jp/kitte_hagaki/stamp/fuke/detail.php?id=468 Landscape Stamp: Setagaya Noge Post Office] (Japan Post). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Noge-Otsuka Kofun}} * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/00011732 burial goods of Noge Ōtsuka Kofun (Setagaya City Ownership)] - other_designation Online (Agency for Cultural Affairs) * [http://www.city.setagaya.lg.jp/kurashi/107/159/778/h26/d00135671.html Experience Ancient Life at Noge Noge Ōtsuka Kofun (April 10, 2010) | location=Noge 1-chome, {{ill|Setagaya, Tokyo|ja|世田谷区}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}} | coordinates={{coord|35|36|18.1|N|139|38|35.1|E|}} | part_of={{ill|Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|ja|荏原台古墳群}} | type=Scallop-typed kofun | ConstructionEra=Early 5th century | BurialFacility=Main chambers 1–3: split-bamboo-typed wooden coffins in clay coffins
Chamber 4: box-typed stone coffin | area=82 m in length; posterior circular mound diameter 67 m; height 11 m | BuriedPerson=Unknown | ExcavatedArtifacts=Short armor, helmet, gorgets, shoulder guards, iron swords (19), iron arrowheads (25+), iron knives, iron sickles, {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡}}, bronze armlets, 19 stone replicas, over 30 combs, over 2,000 beads, and a quiver or arrow case | HistoricSite={{ill|Tokyo Designated Historic Site|ja|東京都指定史跡}}: "Noge Ōtsuka Kofun" | TangibleCulturalProperties=excavations (Important Cultural Property of Japan) | SpecialNotes= | map={{location map|Japan Wards of Tokyo|label=Noge
Ōtsuka Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=36|lat_sec=18.1|lon_deg=139|lon_min=38|lon_sec=35.1|mark=帆立貝形古墳-南西.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Tokyo}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|36|18.1|N|139|38|35.1|E}}}} }} '''Noge Ōtsuka Kofun''' (野毛大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Setagaya, Tokyo|ja|世田谷区}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}}. It is scallop-typed and designated as a {{ill|Tokyo Designated Historic Site|ja|東京都指定史跡}}. Artifacts excavated from the tumulus are designated as Important Cultural Property of Japan. == location == Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is situated in {{ill|Tamagawa Nogemachi Park|ja|玉川野毛町公園}}, within the {{ill|Musashino Plateau|ja|武蔵野台地}} at an elevation of [[Category:pp error]]approximately 30–33 meters.{{Cite book|和書|author=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「城南の遺跡」-世田谷周辺発掘調査最新報告- |publisher=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1993|pages=2-3}} Known since the late Edo period, it was historically referred to as "Higashi Ōtsuka." It has also been called "Kaminoge Kofun" and "Todoroki Golf Links Kofun." The tumulus is part of the {{ill|Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|ja|荏原台古墳群}} and represents the region's ancient burial practices. == Overview == At 82 meters in length, with a diameter of 67 meters and a height of 11 meters, Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is one of Japan's largest scallop-typed kofun (hotategai-shiki kofun). An excavation conducted in 1897 (Meiji 30) revealed a stone coffin, and the artifacts were transferred to the Imperial Museum (now {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}). The mound is equi11ed with a stone covering (fukishi) and haniwa, and a horseshoe-typed moat surrounds it.{{Cite book|和書|author1=世田谷区教育委員会|authorlink1=世田谷区教育委員会|author2=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「世田谷の三万年」-最近の発掘調査の成果から- |publisher=世田谷区教育委員会・世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1988|page=16}} == Burial Chambers == Four burial chambers are located in the posterior circular mound. The first chamber's split-bamboo-typed wooden coffin measures 8.2 meters in length and contains numerous grave goods, including: * Weapons and armor: short armor, helmets, gorgets, shoulder guards, 19 iron swords, and 25+ arrowheads * Mirrors and ornaments: {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡}}, bronze armlets, over 2,000 beads, and over 30 combs * Stone replicas: including axes and ceremonial tools Artifacts from the box-typed stone coffin include magatama, tubular beads, iron weapons, armor fragments, and a wide variety of stone replicas, such as bowls, plates, and knives made from steatite. == Cultural Significance == The kofun is recognized as an archaeological benchmark for understanding 5th-century burial practices. Detailed findings were compiled by {{ill|Morikazu Gotō|ja|後藤守一}} in 1936. == Designations == * Important Cultural Property of Japan (excavations): The artifacts are preserved at the {{ill|Setagaya Local History Museum|ja|世田谷区立郷土資料館}} and the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. * Tokyo Designated Historic Site: Designated as a Historic Site on February 6, 1975.{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.syougai.metro.tokyo.jp/iseki0/iseki/list/ruins/13112/112ruins.htm |title=世田谷区-遺跡一覧 東京都遺跡地図情報インターネット提供サービス |publisher=[[東京都教育委員会]] |accessdate=2016-07-03}} == Related Information == * The landscape stamp of {{ill|Setagaya Noge Post Office|ja|世田谷野毛郵便局}} features Noge Ōtsuka Kofun.[https://www.post.japanpost.jp/kitte_hagaki/stamp/fuke/detail.php?id=468 Landscape Stamp: Setagaya Noge Post Office] (Japan Post). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Noge-Otsuka Kofun}} * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/00011732 burial goods of Noge Ōtsuka Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} | name=Noge Ōtsuka Kofun | Alias=Higashi Ōtsuka, Kaminoge Kofun, etc. | image=[[File:Noge Otsuka Kofun -01.jpg|260px]]
Noge Ōtsuka Kofun (April 10, 2010) | location=Noge 1-chome, {{ill|Setagaya, Tokyo|ja|世田谷区}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}} | coordinates={{coord|35|36|18.1|N|139|38|35.1|E|}} | part_of={{ill|Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|ja|荏原台古墳群}} | type=Scallop-shaped kofun | ConstructionEra=Early 5th century | BurialFacility=Main chambers 1–3: split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffins in clay coffins
Chamber 4: box-shaped stone coffin | area=82 m in length; posterior circular mound diameter 67 m; height 11 m | BuriedPerson=Unknown | ExcavatedArtifacts=Short armor, helmet, gorgets, shoulder guards, iron swords (19), iron arrowheads (25+), iron knives, iron sickles, {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡}}, bronze armlets, 19 stone replicas, over 30 combs, over 2,000 beads, and a quiver or arrow case | HistoricSite={{ill|Tokyo Designated Historic Site|ja|東京都指定史跡}}: "Noge Ōtsuka Kofun" | TangibleCulturalProperties=excavations (Important Cultural Property of Japan) | SpecialNotes= | map={{location map|Japan Wards of Tokyo|label=Noge
Ōtsuka Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=36|lat_sec=18.1|lon_deg=139|lon_min=38|lon_sec=35.1|mark=帆立貝形古墳-南西.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Tokyo}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|36|18.1|N|139|38|35.1|E}}}} }} '''Noge Ōtsuka Kofun''' (野毛大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Setagaya, Tokyo|ja|世田谷区}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}}. It is scallop-shaped and designated as a {{ill|Tokyo Designated Historic Site|ja|東京都指定史跡}}. Artifacts excavated from the tumulus are designated as Important Cultural Property of Japan. == location == Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is situated in {{ill|Tamagawa Nogemachi Park|ja|玉川野毛町公園}}, within the {{ill|Musashino Plateau|ja|武蔵野台地}} at an elevation of [[Category:pp error]]approximately 30–33 meters.{{Cite book|和書|author=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「城南の遺跡」-世田谷周辺発掘調査最新報告- |publisher=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1993|pages=2-3}} Known since the late Edo period, it was historically referred to as "Higashi Ōtsuka." It has also been called "Kaminoge Kofun" and "Todoroki Golf Links Kofun." The tumulus is part of the {{ill|Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|ja|荏原台古墳群}} and represents the region's ancient burial practices. == Overview == At 82 meters in length, with a diameter of 67 meters and a height of 11 meters, Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is one of Japan's largest scallop-shaped kofun (hotategai-shiki kofun). An excavation conducted in 1897 (Meiji 30) revealed a stone coffin, and the artifacts were transferred to the Imperial Museum (now {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}). The mound is equi11ed with a stone covering (fukishi) and haniwa, and a horseshoe-shaped moat surrounds it.{{Cite book|和書|author1=世田谷区教育委員会|authorlink1=世田谷区教育委員会|author2=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「世田谷の三万年」-最近の発掘調査の成果から- |publisher=世田谷区教育委員会・世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1988|page=16}} == Burial Chambers == Four burial chambers are located in the posterior circular mound. The first chamber's split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin measures 8.2 meters in length and contains numerous grave goods, including: * Weapons and armor: short armor, helmets, gorgets, shoulder guards, 19 iron swords, and 25+ arrowheads * Mirrors and ornaments: {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡}}, bronze armlets, over 2,000 beads, and over 30 combs * Stone replicas: including axes and ceremonial tools Artifacts from the box-shaped stone coffin include magatama, tubular beads, iron weapons, armor fragments, and a wide variety of stone replicas, such as bowls, plates, and knives made from steatite. == Cultural Significance == The kofun is recognized as an archaeological benchmark for understanding 5th-century burial practices. Detailed findings were compiled by {{ill|Morikazu Gotō|ja|後藤守一}} in 1936. == Designations == * Important Cultural Property of Japan (excavations): The artifacts are preserved at the {{ill|Setagaya Local History Museum|ja|世田谷区立郷土資料館}} and the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. * Tokyo Designated Historic Site: Designated as a Historic Site on February 6, 1975.{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.syougai.metro.tokyo.jp/iseki0/iseki/list/ruins/13112/112ruins.htm |title=世田谷区-遺跡一覧 東京都遺跡地図情報インターネット提供サービス |publisher=[[東京都教育委員会]] |accessdate=2016-07-03}} == Related Information == * The landscape stamp of {{ill|Setagaya Noge Post Office|ja|世田谷野毛郵便局}} features Noge Ōtsuka Kofun.[https://www.post.japanpost.jp/kitte_hagaki/stamp/fuke/detail.php?id=468 Landscape Stamp: Setagaya Noge Post Office] (Japan Post). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Noge-Otsuka Kofun}} * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/00011732 burial goods of Noge Ōtsuka Kofun (Setagaya City Ownership)] - other_designation Online (Agency for Cultural Affairs) * [http://www.city.setagaya.lg.jp/kurashi/107/159/778/h26/d00135671.html Experience Ancient Life at Noge Ōtsuka Kofun Festival] - Setagaya City website {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Kofun in Tokyo]] [[Category:History of Setagaya]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:Important other_designation {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} | name=Noge Ōtsuka Kofun | Alias=Higashi Ōtsuka, Kaminoge Kofun, etc. | image=[[File:Noge Otsuka Kofun -01.jpg|260px]]
Noge Ōtsuka Kofun (April 10, 2010) | location=Noge 1-chome, {{ill|Setagaya, Tokyo|ja|世田谷区}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}} | coordinates={{coord|35|36|18.1|N|139|38|35.1|E|}} | part_of={{ill|Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|ja|荏原台古墳群}} | type=Scallop-shaped kofun | ConstructionEra=Early 5th century | BurialFacility=Main chambers 1–3: split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffins in clay coffins
Chamber 4: box-shaped stone coffin | area=82 m in length; posterior circular mound diameter 67 m; height 11 m | BuriedPerson=Unknown | ExcavatedArtifacts=Short armor, helmet, gorgets, shoulder guards, iron swords (19), iron arrowheads (25+), iron knives, iron sickles, {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡}}, bronze armlets, 19 stone replicas, over 30 combs, over 2,000 beads, and a quiver or arrow case | HistoricSite={{ill|Tokyo Designated Historic Site|ja|東京都指定史跡}}: "Noge Ōtsuka Kofun" | TangibleCulturalProperties=excavations (Important Cultural Property of Japan) | SpecialNotes= | map={{location map|Japan Wards of Tokyo|label=Noge
Ōtsuka Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=36|lat_sec=18.1|lon_deg=139|lon_min=38|lon_sec=35.1|mark=帆立貝形古墳-南西.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Tokyo}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|36|18.1|N|139|38|35.1|E}}}} }} '''Noge Ōtsuka Kofun''' (野毛大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Setagaya, Tokyo|ja|世田谷区}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}}. It is scallop-shaped and designated as a {{ill|Tokyo Designated Historic Site|ja|東京都指定史跡}}. Artifacts excavated from the tumulus are designated as Important Cultural Property of Japan. == location == Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is situated in {{ill|Tamagawa Nogemachi Park|ja|玉川野毛町公園}}, within the {{ill|Musashino Plateau|ja|武蔵野台地}} at an elevation of [[Category:pp error]]approximately 30–33 meters.{{Cite book|和書|author=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「城南の遺跡」-世田谷周辺発掘調査最新報告- |publisher=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1993|pages=2-3}} Known since the late Edo period, it was historically referred to as "Higashi Ōtsuka." It has also been called "Kaminoge Kofun" and "Todoroki Golf Links Kofun." The tumulus is part of the {{ill|Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|ja|荏原台古墳群}} and represents the region's ancient burial practices. == Overview == At 82 meters in length, with a diameter of 67 meters and a height of 11 meters, Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is one of Japan's largest scallop-shaped kofun (hotategai-shiki kofun). An excavation conducted in 1897 (Meiji 30) revealed a stone coffin, and the artifacts were transferred to the Imperial Museum (now {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}). The mound is equi11ed with a stone covering (fukishi) and haniwa, and a horseshoe-shaped moat surrounds it.{{Cite book|和書|author1=世田谷区教育委員会|authorlink1=世田谷区教育委員会|author2=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「世田谷の三万年」-最近の発掘調査の成果から- |publisher=世田谷区教育委員会・世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1988|page=16}} == Burial Chambers == Four burial chambers are located in the posterior circular mound. The first chamber's split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin measures 8.2 meters in length and contains numerous grave goods, including: * Weapons and armor: short armor, helmets, gorgets, shoulder guards, 19 iron swords, and 25+ arrowheads * Mirrors and ornaments: {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡}}, bronze armlets, over 2,000 beads, and over 30 combs * Stone replicas: including axes and ceremonial tools Artifacts from the box-shaped stone coffin include magatama, tubular beads, iron weapons, armor fragments, and a wide variety of stone replicas, such as bowls, plates, and knives made from steatite. == Cultural Significance == The kofun is recognized as an archaeological benchmark for understanding 5th-century burial practices. Detailed findings were compiled by {{ill|Morikazu Gotō|ja|後藤守一}} in 1936. == Designations == * Important Cultural Property of Japan (excavations): The artifacts are preserved at the {{ill|Setagaya Local History Museum|ja|世田谷区立郷土資料館}} and the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. * Tokyo Designated Historic Site: Designated as a Historic Site on February 6, 1975.{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.syougai.metro.tokyo.jp/iseki0/iseki/list/ruins/13112/112ruins.htm |title=世田谷区-遺跡一覧 東京都遺跡地図情報インターネット提供サービス |publisher=[[東京都教育委員会]] |accessdate=2016-07-03}} == Related Information == * The landscape stamp of {{ill|Setagaya Noge Post Office|ja|世田谷野毛郵便局}} features Noge Ōtsuka Kofun.[https://www.post.japanpost.jp/kitte_hagaki/stamp/fuke/detail.php?id=468 Landscape Stamp: Setagaya Noge Post Office] (Japan Post). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Noge-Otsuka Kofun}} * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/00011732 burial goods of Noge Ōtsuka Kofun (Setagaya City Ownership)] - other_designation Online (Agency for Cultural Affairs) * [http://www.city.setagaya.lg.jp/kurashi/107/159/778/h26/d00135671.html Experience Ancient Life at Noge Ōtsuka Kofun Festival] - Setagaya City website {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Kofun in Tokyo]] [[Category:History of Setagaya]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:Important other_designation Noge Ōtsuka Kofun (April 10, 2010) | location=Noge 1-chome, {{ill|Setagaya, Tokyo|ja|世田谷区}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}} | coordinates={{coord|35|36|18.1|N|139|38|35.1|E|}} | part_of={{ill|Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|ja|荏原台古墳群}} | type=Scallop-shaped kofun | ConstructionEra=Early 5th century | BurialFacility=Main chambers 1–3: split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffins in clay coffins
Chamber 4: box-shaped stone coffin | area=82 m in length; posterior circular mound diameter 67 m; height 11 m | BuriedPerson=Unknown | ExcavatedArtifacts=Short armor, helmet, gorgets, shoulder guards, iron swords (19), iron arrowheads (25+), iron knives, iron sickles, {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡}}, bronze armlets, 19 stone replicas, over 30 combs, over 2,000 beads, and a quiver or arrow case | HistoricSite={{ill|Tokyo Designated Historic Site|ja|東京都指定史跡}}: "Noge Ōtsuka Kofun" | TangibleCulturalProperties=excavations (Important Cultural Property of Japan) | SpecialNotes= | map={{location map|Japan Wards of Tokyo|label=Noge
Ōtsuka Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=36|lat_sec=18.1|lon_deg=139|lon_min=38|lon_sec=35.1|mark=帆立貝形古墳-南西.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Tokyo}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|36|18.1|N|139|38|35.1|E}}}} }} '''Noge Ōtsuka Kofun''' (野毛大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Setagaya, Tokyo|ja|世田谷区}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}}. It is scallop-shaped and designated as a {{ill|Tokyo Designated Historic Site|ja|東京都指定史跡}}. Artifacts excavated from the tumulus are designated as Important Cultural Property of Japan. == location == Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is situated in {{ill|Tamagawa Nogemachi Park|ja|玉川野毛町公園}}, within the {{ill|Musashino Plateau|ja|武蔵野台地}} at an elevation of [[Category:pp error]]approximately 30–33 meters.{{Cite book|和書|author=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「城南の遺跡」-世田谷周辺発掘調査最新報告- |publisher=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1993|pages=2-3}} Known since the late Edo period, it was historically referred to as "Higashi Ōtsuka." It has also been called "Kaminoge Kofun" and "Todoroki Golf Links Kofun." The tumulus is part of the {{ill|Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|ja|荏原台古墳群}} and represents the region's ancient burial practices. == Overview == At 82 meters in length, with a diameter of 67 meters and a height of 11 meters, Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is one of Japan's largest scallop-shaped kofun (hotategai-shiki kofun). An excavation conducted in 1897 (Meiji 30) revealed a stone coffin, and the artifacts were transferred to the Imperial Museum (now {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}). The mound is equi11ed with a stone covering (fukishi) and haniwa, and a horseshoe-shaped moat surrounds it.{{Cite book|和書|author1=世田谷区教育委員会|authorlink1=世田谷区教育委員会|author2=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「世田谷の三万年」-最近の発掘調査の成果から- |publisher=世田谷区教育委員会・世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1988|page=16}} == Burial Chambers == Four burial chambers are located in the posterior circular mound. The first chamber's split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin measures 8.2 meters in length and contains numerous grave goods, including: * Weapons and armor: short armor, helmets, gorgets, shoulder guards, 19 iron swords, and 25+ arrowheads * Mirrors and ornaments: {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡}}, bronze armlets, over 2,000 beads, and over 30 combs * Stone replicas: including axes and ceremonial tools Artifacts from the box-shaped stone coffin include magatama, tubular beads, iron weapons, armor fragments, and a wide variety of stone replicas, such as bowls, plates, and knives made from steatite. == Cultural Significance == The kofun is recognized as an archaeological benchmark for understanding 5th-century burial practices. Detailed findings were compiled by {{ill|Morikazu Gotō|ja|後藤守一}} in 1936. == Designations == * Important Cultural Property of Japan (excavations): The artifacts are preserved at the {{ill|Setagaya Local History Museum|ja|世田谷区立郷土資料館}} and the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. * Tokyo Designated Historic Site: Designated as a Historic Site on February 6, 1975.{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.syougai.metro.tokyo.jp/iseki0/iseki/list/ruins/13112/112ruins.htm |title=世田谷区-遺跡一覧 東京都遺跡地図情報インターネット提供サービス |publisher=[[東京都教育委員会]] |accessdate=2016-07-03}} == Related Information == * The landscape stamp of {{ill|Setagaya Noge Post Office|ja|世田谷野毛郵便局}} features Noge Ōtsuka Kofun.[https://www.post.japanpost.jp/kitte_hagaki/stamp/fuke/detail.php?id=468 Landscape Stamp: Setagaya Noge Post Office] (Japan Post). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Noge-Otsuka Kofun}} * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/00011732 burial goods of Noge Ōtsuka Noge Ōtsuka Kofun (April 10, 2010) | location=Noge 1-chome, {{ill|Setagaya, Tokyo|ja|世田谷区}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}} | coordinates={{coord|35|36|18.1|N|139|38|35.1|E|}} | part_of={{ill|Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|ja|荏原台古墳群}} | type=Scallop-shaped kofun | ConstructionEra=Early 5th century | BurialFacility=Main chambers 1–3: split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffins in clay coffins
Chamber 4: box-shaped stone coffin | area=82 m in length; posterior circular mound diameter 67 m; height 11 m | BuriedPerson=Unknown | ExcavatedArtifacts=Short armor, helmet, gorgets, shoulder guards, iron swords (19), iron arrowheads (25+), iron knives, iron sickles, {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡}}, bronze armlets, 19 stone replicas, over 30 combs, over 2,000 beads, and a quiver or arrow case | HistoricSite={{ill|Tokyo Designated Historic Site|ja|東京都指定史跡}}: "Noge Ōtsuka Kofun" | TangibleCulturalProperties=excavations (Important Cultural Property of Japan) | SpecialNotes= | map={{location map|Japan Wards of Tokyo|label=Noge
Ōtsuka Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=36|lat_sec=18.1|lon_deg=139|lon_min=38|lon_sec=35.1|mark=帆立貝形古墳-南西.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Tokyo}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|36|18.1|N|139|38|35.1|E}}}} }} '''Noge Ōtsuka Kofun''' (野毛大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Setagaya, Tokyo|ja|世田谷区}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}}. It is scallop-shaped and designated as a {{ill|Tokyo Designated Historic Site|ja|東京都指定史跡}}. Artifacts excavated from the tumulus are designated as Important Cultural Property of Japan. == location == Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is situated in {{ill|Tamagawa Nogemachi Park|ja|玉川野毛町公園}}, within the {{ill|Musashino Plateau|ja|武蔵野台地}} at an elevation of [[Category:pp error]]approximately 30–33 meters.{{Cite book|和書|author=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「城南の遺跡」-世田谷周辺発掘調査最新報告- |publisher=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1993|pages=2-3}} Known since the late Edo period, it was historically referred to as "Higashi Ōtsuka." It has also been called "Kaminoge Kofun" and "Todoroki Golf Links Kofun." The tumulus is part of the {{ill|Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|ja|荏原台古墳群}} and represents the region's ancient burial practices. == Overview == At 82 meters in length, with a diameter of 67 meters and a height of 11 meters, Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is one of Japan's largest scallop-shaped kofun (hotategai-shiki kofun). An excavation conducted in 1897 (Meiji 30) revealed a stone coffin, and the artifacts were transferred to the Imperial Museum (now {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}). The mound is equi11ed with a stone covering (fukishi) and haniwa, and a horseshoe-shaped moat surrounds it.{{Cite book|和書|author1=世田谷区教育委員会|authorlink1=世田谷区教育委員会|author2=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「世田谷の三万年」-最近の発掘調査の成果から- |publisher=世田谷区教育委員会・世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1988|page=16}} == Burial Chambers == Four burial chambers are located in the posterior circular mound. The first chamber's split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin measures 8.2 meters in length and contains numerous grave goods, including: * Weapons and armor: short armor, helmets, gorgets, shoulder guards, 19 iron swords, and 25+ arrowheads * Mirrors and ornaments: {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡}}, bronze armlets, over 2,000 beads, and over 30 combs * Stone replicas: including axes and ceremonial tools Artifacts from the box-shaped stone coffin include magatama, tubular beads, iron weapons, armor fragments, and a wide variety of stone replicas, such as bowls, plates, and knives made from steatite. == Cultural Significance == The kofun is recognized as an archaeological benchmark for understanding 5th-century burial practices. Detailed findings were compiled by {{ill|Morikazu Gotō|ja|後藤守一}} in 1936. == Designations == * Important Cultural Property of Japan (excavations): The artifacts are preserved at the {{ill|Setagaya Local History Museum|ja|世田谷区立郷土資料館}} and the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. * Tokyo Designated Historic Site: Designated as a Historic Site on February 6, 1975.{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.syougai.metro.tokyo.jp/iseki0/iseki/list/ruins/13112/112ruins.htm |title=世田谷区-遺跡一覧 東京都遺跡地図情報インターネット提供サービス |publisher=[[東京都教育委員会]] |accessdate=2016-07-03}} == Related Information == * The landscape stamp of {{ill|Setagaya Noge Post Office|ja|世田谷野毛郵便局}} features Noge Ōtsuka Kofun.[https://www.post.japanpost.jp/kitte_hagaki/stamp/fuke/detail.php?id=468 Landscape Stamp: Setagaya Noge Post Office] (Japan Post). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Noge-Otsuka Kofun}} * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/00011732 burial goods of Noge Ōtsuka revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[:Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones[[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} | name=Noge Ōtsuka Kofun | alternate_name=Higashi Ōtsuka, Kaminoge Kofun, etc. | image=[[File:Noge Otsuka Kofun -01.jpg|260px]]
Noge Ōtsuka Kofun (April 10, 2010) | location=Noge 1-chome, {{ill|Setagaya, Tokyo|ja|世田谷区}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}} | coordinates={{coord|35|36|18.1|N|139|38|35.1|E|}} | part_of={{ill|Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|ja|荏原台古墳群}} | type=Scallop-shaped kofun | ConstructionEra=Early 5th century | BurialFacility=Main chambers 1–3: split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffins in clay coffins
Chamber 4: box-shaped stone coffin | area=82 m in length; posterior circular mound diameter 67 m; height 11 m | BuriedPerson=Unknown | ExcavatedArtifacts=Short armor, helmet, gorgets, shoulder guards, iron swords (19), iron arrowheads (25+), iron knives, iron sickles, {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡}}, bronze armlets, 19 stone replicas, over 30 combs, over 2,000 beads, and a quiver or arrow case | HistoricSite={{ill|Tokyo Designated other_designation|ja|東京都指定史跡}}: "Noge Ōtsuka Kofun" | TangibleCulturalProperties=excavations (Important Cultural Property of Japan) | SpecialNotes= | map={{location map|Japan Wards of Tokyo|label=Noge
Ōtsuka Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=36|lat_sec=18.1|lon_deg=139|lon_min=38|lon_sec=35.1|mark=帆立貝形古墳-南西.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Tokyo}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|36|18.1|N|139|38|35.1|E}}}} }} '''Noge Ōtsuka Kofun''' (野毛大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Setagaya, Tokyo|ja|世田谷区}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}}. It is scallop-shaped and designated as a {{ill|Tokyo Designated other_designation|ja|東京都指定史跡}}. Artifacts excavated from the tumulus are designated as Important Cultural Property of Japan. == location == Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is situated in {{ill|Tamagawa Nogemachi Park|ja|玉川野毛町公園}}, within the {{ill|Musashino Plateau|ja|武蔵野台地}} at an elevation of [[Category:pp error]]approximately 30–33 meters.{{Cite book|和書|author=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「城南の遺跡」-世田谷周辺発掘調査最新報告- |publisher=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1993|pages=2-3}} Known since the late Edo period, it was historically referred to as "Higashi Ōtsuka." It has also been called "Kaminoge Kofun" and "Todoroki Golf Links Kofun." The tumulus is part of the {{ill|Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|ja|荏原台古墳群}} and represents the region's ancient burial practices. == Overview == At 82 meters in length, with a diameter of 67 meters and a height of 11 meters, Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is one of Japan's largest scallop-shaped kofun (hotategai-shiki kofun). An excavation conducted in 1897 (Meiji 30) revealed a stone coffin, and the artifacts were transferred to the Imperial Museum (now {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}). The mound is equi11ed with a stone covering (fukishi) and haniwa, and a horseshoe-shaped moat surrounds it.{{Cite book|和書|author1=世田谷区教育委員会|authorlink1=世田谷区教育委員会|author2=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「世田谷の三万年」-最近の発掘調査の成果から- |publisher=世田谷区教育委員会・世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1988|page=16}} == Burial Chambers == Four burial chambers are located in the posterior circular mound. The first chamber's split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin measures 8.2 meters in length and contains numerous grave goods, including: * Weapons and armor: short armor, helmets, gorgets, shoulder guards, 19 iron swords, and 25+ arrowheads * Mirrors and ornaments: {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡}}, bronze armlets, over 2,000 beads, and over 30 combs * Stone replicas: including axes and ceremonial tools Artifacts from the box-shaped stone coffin include magatama, tubular beads, iron weapons, armor fragments, and a wide variety of stone replicas, such as bowls, plates, and knives made from steatite. == Cultural Significance == The kofun is recognized as an archaeological benchmark for understanding 5th-century burial practices. Detailed findings were compiled by {{ill|Morikazu Gotō|ja|後藤守一}} in 1936. == Designations == * Important Cultural Property of Japan (excavations): The artifacts are preserved at the {{ill|Setagaya Local History Museum|ja|世田谷区立郷土資料館}} and the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. * Tokyo Designated other_designation: Designated as a other_designation on February 6, 1975.{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.syougai.metro.tokyo.jp/iseki0/iseki/list/ruins/13112/112ruins.htm |title=世田谷区-遺跡一覧 東京都遺跡地図情報インターネット提供サービス |publisher=[[東京都教育委員会]] |accessdate=2016-07-03}} == Related Information == * The landscape stamp of {{ill|Setagaya Noge Post Office|ja|世田谷野毛郵便局}} features Noge Ōtsuka Kofun.[https://www.post.japanpost.jp/kitte_hagaki/stamp/fuke/detail.php?id=468 Landscape Stamp: Setagaya Noge Post Office] (Japan Post). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Noge-Otsuka Kofun}} * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/00011732 burial goods of Noge Ōtsuka Kofun (Setagaya City Ownership)] - other_designation Online (Agency for Cultural Affairs) * [http://www.city.setagaya.lg.jp/kurashi/107/159/778/h26/d00135671.html Experience Ancient Life at Noge Ōtsuka Kofun Festival] - Setagaya City website {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Kofun in Tokyo]] [[Category:History of Setagaya]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:[[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] of Tokyo]] [[Category:Tokyo Designated other_designations]] [[Category:Noge Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Noge Ōtsuka An excavation conducted in 1897 (Meiji 30) revealed a stone coffin, and the artifacts were transferred to the Imperial Museum (now {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}). The mound is equi11ed with a stone covering (fukishi) and haniwa, and a horseshoe-shaped moat surrounds it.{{Cite book|和書|author1=世田谷区教育委員会|authorlink1=世田谷区教育委員会|author2=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「世田谷の三万年」-最近の発掘調査の成果から- |publisher=世田谷区教育委員会・世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1988|page=16}} == Burial Chambers == Four burial chambers are located in the posterior circular mound. The first chamber's split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin measures 8.2 meters in length and contains numerous grave goods, including: * Weapons and armor: short armor, helmets, gorgets, shoulder guards, 19 iron swords, and 25+ arrowheads * Mirrors and ornaments: {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡}}, bronze armlets, over 2,000 beads, and over 30 combs * Stone replicas: including axes and ceremonial tools Artifacts from the box-shaped stone coffin include magatama, tubular beads, iron weapons, armor fragments, and a wide variety of stone replicas, such as bowls, plates, and knives made from steatite. == Cultural Significance == The kofun is recognized as an archaeological benchmark for understanding 5th-century burial practices. Detailed findings were compiled by {{ill|Morikazu Gotō|ja|後藤守一}} in 1936. == Designations == * Important Cultural Property of Japan (excavations): The artifacts are preserved at the {{ill|Setagaya Local History Museum|ja|世田谷区立郷土資料館}} and the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. * Tokyo Designated other_designation: Designated as a other_designation on February 6, 1975.{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.syougai.metro.tokyo.jp/iseki0/iseki/list/ruins/13112/112ruins.htm |title=世田谷区-遺跡一覧 東京都遺跡地図情報インターネット提供サービス |publisher=[[東京都教育委員会]] |accessdate=2016-07-03}} == Related Information == * Chamber 4: box-shaped stone coffin | area=82 m in length; posterior circular mound diameter 67 m; height 11 m | Interred_individual=Unknown | ExcavatedArtifacts=Short armor, helmet, gorgets, shoulder guards, iron swords (19), iron arrowheads (25+), iron knives, iron sickles, {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡}}, bronze armlets, 19 stone replicas, over 30 combs, over 2,000 beads, and a quiver or arrow case | HistoricSite={{ill|Tokyo Designated other_designation|ja|東京都指定史跡}}: "Noge Ōtsuka Kofun" | TangibleCulturalProperties=excavations (Important Cultural Property of Japan) | SpecialNotes= | map={{location map|Japan Wards of Tokyo|label=Noge
Ōtsuka Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=36|lat_sec=18.1|lon_deg=139|lon_min=38|lon_sec=35.1|mark=帆立貝形古墳-南西.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Tokyo}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|36|18.1|N|139|38|35.1|E}}}} }} '''Noge Ōtsuka Kofun''' (野毛大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Setagaya, Tokyo|ja|世田谷区}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}}. It is scallop-shaped and designated as a {{ill|Tokyo Designated other_designation|ja|東京都指定史跡}}. Artifacts excavated from the tumulus are designated as Important Cultural Property of Japan. == location == Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is situated in {{ill|Tamagawa Nogemachi Park|ja|玉川野毛町公園}}, within the {{ill|Musashino Plateau|ja|武蔵野台地}} at an elevation of [[Category:pp error]]approximately 30–33 meters.{{Cite book|和書|author=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「城南の遺跡」-世田谷周辺発掘調査最新報告- |publisher=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1993|pages=2-3}} Known since the late Edo period, it was historically referred to as "Higashi Ōtsuka." It has also been called "Kaminoge Kofun" and "Todoroki Golf Links Kofun." The tumulus is part of the {{ill|Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|ja|荏原台古墳群}} and represents the region's ancient burial practices. == Overview == At 82 meters in length, with a diameter of 67 meters and a height of 11 meters, Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is one of Japan's largest scallop-shaped kofun (hotategai-shiki kofun). An excavation conducted in 1897 (Meiji 30) revealed a stone coffin, and the artifacts were transferred to the Imperial Museum (now {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}). The mound is equi11ed with a stone covering (fukishi) and haniwa, and a horseshoe-shaped moat surrounds it.{{Cite book|和書|author1=世田谷区教育委員会|authorlink1=世田谷区教育委員会|author2=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「世田谷の三万年」-最近の発掘調査の成果から- |publisher=世田谷区教育委員会・世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1988|page=16}} == Burial Chambers == Four burial chambers are located in the posterior circular mound. The first chamber's split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin measures 8.2 meters in length and contains numerous grave goods, including: * Weapons and armor: short armor, helmets, gorgets, shoulder guards, 19 iron swords, and 25+ arrowheads * Mirrors and ornaments: {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡}}, bronze armlets, over 2,000 beads, and over 30 combs * Stone replicas: including axes and ceremonial tools Artifacts from the box-shaped stone coffin include magatama, tubular beads, iron weapons, armor fragments, and a wide variety of stone replicas, such as bowls, plates, and knives made from steatite. == Cultural Significance == The kofun is recognized as an archaeological benchmark for understanding 5th-century burial practices. Detailed findings were compiled by {{ill|Morikazu Gotō|ja|後藤守一}} in 1936. == Designations == * Important Cultural Property of Japan (excavations): The artifacts are preserved at the {{ill|Setagaya Local History Museum|ja|世田谷区立郷土資料館}} and the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. * Tokyo Designated other_designation: Designated as a other_designation on February 6, 1975.{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.syougai.metro.tokyo.jp/iseki0/iseki/list/ruins/13112/112ruins.htm |title=世田谷区-遺跡一覧 東京都遺跡地図情報インターネット提供サービス |publisher=[[東京都教育委員会]] |accessdate=2016-07-03}} == Related Information == * The landscape stamp of {{ill|Setagaya Noge Post Office|ja|世田谷野毛郵便局}} features Noge Ōtsuka Kofun.[https://www.post.japanpost.jp/kitte_hagaki/stamp/fuke/detail.php?id=468 Landscape Stamp: Setagaya Noge Artifacts excavated from the tumulus are designated as Important Cultural Property of Japan. == location == Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is situated in {{ill|Tamagawa Nogemachi Park|ja|玉川野毛町公園}}, within the {{ill|Musashino Plateau|ja|武蔵野台地}} at an elevation of [[Category:pp error]]approximately 30–33 meters.{{Cite book|和書|author=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「城南の遺跡」-世田谷周辺発掘調査最新報告- |publisher=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1993|pages=2-3}} Known since the late Edo period, it was historically referred to as "Higashi Ōtsuka." It has also been called "Kaminoge Kofun" and "Todoroki Golf Links Kofun." The tumulus is part of the {{ill|Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|ja|荏原台古墳群}} and represents the region's ancient burial practices. == Overview == At 82 meters in length, with a diameter of 67 meters and a height of 11 meters, Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is one of Japan's largest scallop-shaped kofun (hotategai-shiki kofun). An excavation conducted in 1897 (Meiji 30) revealed a stone coffin, and the artifacts were transferred to the Imperial Museum (now {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}). The mound is equi11ed with a stone covering (fukishi) and haniwa, and a horseshoe-shaped moat surrounds it.{{Cite book|和書|author1=世田谷区教育委員会|authorlink1=世田谷区教育委員会|author2=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「世田谷の三万年」-最近の発掘調査の成果から- |publisher=世田谷区教育委員会・世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1988|page=16}} == Burial Chambers == Four burial chambers are located in the posterior circular mound. The first chamber's split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin measures 8.2 meters in length and contains numerous grave goods, including: * Weapons and armor: short armor, helmets, gorgets, shoulder guards, 19 iron swords, and 25+ arrowheads * Mirrors and ornaments: {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡}}, bronze armlets, over 2,000 beads, and over 30 combs * Stone replicas: including axes and ceremonial tools Artifacts from the box-shaped stone coffin include magatama, tubular beads, iron weapons, armor fragments, and a wide variety of stone replicas, such as bowls, plates, and knives made from steatite. == Cultural Significance == The kofun is recognized as an archaeological benchmark for understanding 5th-century burial practices. Detailed findings were compiled by {{ill|Morikazu Gotō|ja|後藤守一}} in 1936. == Designations == * Important Cultural Property of Japan (excavations): The artifacts are preserved at the {{ill|Setagaya Local History Museum|ja|世田谷区立郷土資料館}} and the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. * Tokyo Designated other_designation: Designated as a other_designation on February 6, 1975.{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.syougai.metro.tokyo.jp/iseki0/iseki/list/ruins/13112/112ruins.htm |title=世田谷区-遺跡一覧 東京都遺跡地図情報インターネット提供サービス |publisher=[[東京都教育委員会]] |accessdate=2016-07-03}} == Related Information == * The landscape stamp of {{ill|Setagaya Noge Post Office|ja|世田谷野毛郵便局}} features Noge Ōtsuka Kofun.[https://www.post.japanpost.jp/kitte_hagaki/stamp/fuke/detail.php?id=468 Landscape Stamp: Setagaya Noge Ōtsuka Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=36|lat_sec=18.1|lon_deg=139|lon_min=38|lon_sec=35.1|mark=帆立貝形古墳-南西.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Tokyo}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|36|18.1|N|139|38|35.1|E}}}} }} '''Noge Ōtsuka Kofun''' (野毛大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Setagaya, Tokyo|ja|世田谷区}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}}. It is scallop-shaped and designation1 as a {{ill|Tokyo Designated other_designation|ja|東京都指定史跡}}. burial goods excavated from the tumulus are designation1 as Important Cultural Property of Japan. == location == Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is situated in {{ill|Tamagawa Nogemachi Park|ja|玉川野毛町公園}}, within the {{ill|Musashino Plateau|ja|武蔵野台地}} at an elevation of [[Category:pp error]]approximately 30–33 meters.{{Cite book|和書|author=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「城南の遺跡」-世田谷周辺発掘調査最新報告- |publisher=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1993|pages=2-3}} Known since the late Edo period, it was historically referred to as "Higashi Ōtsuka." It has also been called "Kaminoge Kofun" and "Todoroki Golf Links Kofun." The tumulus is part of the {{ill|Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|ja|荏原台古墳群}} and represents the region's ancient burial practices. == Overview == At 82 meters in length, with a diameter of 67 meters and a height of 11 meters, Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is one of Japan's largest scallop-shaped kofun (hotategai-shiki kofun). An excavation conducted in 1897 (Meiji 30) revealed a stone coffin, and the burial goods were transferred to the Imperial Museum (now {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}). The mound is equi11ed with a stone covering (fukishi) and haniwa, and a horseshoe-shaped moat surrounds it.{{Cite book|和書|author1=世田谷区教育委員会|authorlink1=世田谷区教育委員会|author2=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「世田谷の三万年」-最近の発掘調査の成果から- |publisher=世田谷区教育委員会・世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1988|page=16}} == Burial Chambers == Four burial chambers are located in the posterior circular mound. The first chamber's split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin measures 8.2 meters in length and contains numerous grave goods, including: * Weapons and armor: short armor, helmets, gorgets, shoulder guards, 19 iron swords, and 25+ arrowheads * Mirrors and ornaments: {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡}}, bronze armlets, over 2,000 beads, and over 30 combs * Stone replicas: including axes and ceremonial tools burial goods from the box-shaped stone coffin include magatama, tubular beads, iron weapons, armor fragments, and a wide variety of stone replicas, such as bowls, plates, and knives made from steatite. == Cultural Significance == The kofun is recognized as an archaeological benchmark for understanding 5th-century burial practices. Detailed findings were compiled by {{ill|Morikazu Gotō|ja|後藤守一}} in 1936. == Designations == * Important Cultural Property of Japan (excavations): The burial goods are preserved at the {{ill|Setagaya Local History Museum|ja|世田谷区立郷土資料館}} and the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. * Tokyo Designated other_designation: Designated as a other_designation on February 6, 1975.{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.syougai.metro.tokyo.jp/iseki0/iseki/list/ruins/13112/112ruins.htm |title=世田谷区-遺跡一覧 東京都遺跡地図情報インターネット提供サービス |publisher=[[東京都教育委員会]] |accessdate=2016-07-03}} == Related Information == * The landscape stamp of {{ill|Setagaya Noge Post Office|ja|世田谷野毛郵便局}} features Noge Ōtsuka Kofun.[https://www.post.japanpost.jp/kitte_hagaki/stamp/fuke/detail.php?id=468 Landscape Stamp: Setagaya Noge Post Office] (Japan Post). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Noge-Otsuka Kofun}} * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/00011732 burial goods of Noge Ōtsuka Kofun (Setagaya City Ownership)] - other_designation Online (Agency for Cultural Affairs) * [http://www.city.setagaya.lg.jp/kurashi/107/159/778/h26/d00135671.html Experience Ancient Life at Noge Ōtsuka Kofun Festival] - Setagaya City website {{Japanese-history-stub}} [[Category:Kofun in Tokyo]] [[Category:History of Setagaya]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:[[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] of Tokyo]] [[Category:Tokyo Designated other_designations]] [[Category:Noge Ōtsuka Kofun]][[Category:11]][[sn:Noge Ōtsuka
