Noge Ōtsuka Kofun (April 10, 2010) | location=Noge 1-chome, {{ill|Setagaya, Tokyo|ja|世田谷区|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都|12=draft}} | coordinates={{coord|35|36|18.1|N|139|38|35.1|E|}} | part_of={{ill|Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|ja|荏原台古墳群|12=draft}} | type=Scallop-shaped kofun | built=Early 5th century | burial=Main chambers 1–3: split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffins in clay coffins
Chamber 4: box-shaped stone coffin | area=82 m in length; posterior circular mound diameter 67 m; height 11 m | Interred_individual=Unknown | burial goods=Short armor, helmet, gorgets, shoulder guards, iron swords (19), iron arrowheads (25+), iron knives, iron sickles, {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡|12=draft}}, bronze armlets, 19 stone replicas, over 30 combs, over 2,000 beads, and a quiver or arrow case | Historic Site={{ill|Tokyo Designated Historic Site|ja|東京都指定史跡|12=draft}}: "Noge Ōtsuka Kofun" | Historic Site=excavations (Important Cultural Property of Japan) | Specialnotes= | map={{location map|Japan Wards of Tokyo|map_dot_label=Noge
Ōtsuka Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=36|lat_sec=18.1|lon_deg=139|lon_min=38|lon_sec=35.1|mark=帆立貝形古墳-南西.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Tokyo}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|36|18.1|N|139|38|35.1|E}}}} }} '''Noge Ōtsuka Kofun''' (野毛大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Setagaya, Tokyo|ja|世田谷区|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都|12=draft}}. It is scallop-shaped and Historic Site1 as a {{ill|Tokyo Designated Historic Site|ja|東京都指定史跡|12=draft}}. burial goods excavated from the tumulus are Historic Site1 as Important Cultural Property of Japan. == location == Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is situated in {{ill|Tamagawa Nogemachi Park|ja|玉川野毛町公園|12=draft}}, within the {{ill|Musashino Plateau|ja|武蔵野台地|12=draft}} at an elevation of approximately 30–33 meters.{{Cite book|和書|author=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「城南の遺跡」-世田谷周辺発掘調査最新報告- |publisher=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1993|pages=2-3}} Known since the late Edo period, it was historically referred to as "Higashi Ōtsuka." It has also been called "Kaminoge Kofun" and "Todoroki Golf Links Kofun." The tumulus is part of the {{ill|Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|ja|荏原台古墳群|12=draft}} and represents the region's ancient burial practices. == Overview == At 82 meters in length, with a diameter of 67 meters and a height of 11 meters, Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is one of Japan's largest scallop-shaped kofun (hotategai-shiki kofun). An excavation conducted in 1897 (Meiji 30) revealed a stone coffin, and the burial goods were transferred to the Imperial Museum (now {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft}}). The mound is equi11ed with a stone covering (fukishi) and haniwa, and a horseshoe-shaped moat surrounds it.{{Cite book|和書|author1=世田谷区教育委員会|authorlink1=世田谷区教育委員会|author2=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「世田谷の三万年」-最近の発掘調査の成果から- |publisher=世田谷区教育委員会・世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1988|page=16}} == Burial Chambers == Four burial chambers are located in the posterior circular mound. The first chamber's split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin measures 8.2 meters in length and contains numerous grave goods, including: * Weapons and armor: short armor, helmets, gorgets, shoulder guards, 19 iron swords, and 25+ arrowheads * Mirrors and ornaments: {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡|12=draft}}, bronze armlets, over 2,000 beads, and over 30 combs * Stone replicas: including axes and ceremonial tools burial goods from the box-shaped stone coffin include magatama, tubular beads, iron weapons, armor fragments, and a wide variety of stone replicas, such as bowls, plates, and knives made from steatite. == Cultural Significance == The kofun is recognized as an archaeological benchmark for understanding 5th-century burial practices. Detailed findings were compiled by {{ill|Morikazu Gotō|ja|後藤守一|12=draft}} in 1936. == Designations == * Important Cultural Property of Japan (excavations): The burial goods are preserved at the {{ill|Setagaya Local History Museum|ja|世田谷区立郷土資料館|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft}}. * Tokyo Designated Historic Site: Designated as a Historic Site on February 6, 1975.{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.syougai.metro.tokyo.jp/iseki0/iseki/list/ruins/13112/112ruins.htm |title=世田谷区-遺跡一覧 東京都遺跡地図情報インターネット提供サービス |publisher=[[東京都教育委員会]] |accessdate=2016-07-03}} == Related Intypeation == * The landscape stamp of {{ill|Setagaya Noge Post Office|ja|世田谷野毛郵便局|12=draft}} features Noge Ōtsuka Kofun.[https://www.post.japanpost.jp/kitte_hagaki/stamp/fuke/detail.php?id=468 Landscape Stamp: Setagaya Noge Post Office] (Japan Post). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Noge-Otsuka Kofun}} * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/00011732 burial goods of Noge Ōtsuka Kofun (Setagaya City Ownership)] - Historic Site Online (Agency for Cultural Affairs) * [http://www.city.setagaya.lg.jp/kurashi/107/159/778/h26/d00135671.html Experience Ancient Life at Noge Ōtsuka Kofun Festival] - Setagaya City website {{Japanese-history-stub}} of Tokyo]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Noge Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[sn:Noge Ōtsuka Kofun]] {{draft categories| [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Tokyo]] [[Category:History of Setagaya]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:[[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] [[Category:Tokyo Designated Historic Sites]] [[Category:Noge Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Noge Otsuka Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Noge Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[ja:野毛大塚古墳]] {{citation needed|Noge Ōtsuka Kofun}} [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] {{Kofun navbar}}

revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 =野毛大塚古墳 |別名 =東大塚、上野毛古墳など |画像 =[[画像:Noge Otsuka Kofun -01.jpg|260px]]
野毛大塚古墳（2010年4月10日） |所在地 =東京都世田谷区野毛1丁目 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|36|18.1|N|139|38|35.1|E|}} |所属 =[[荏原台古墳群]] |形状 =[[帆立貝形古墳]] |築造時期 =5世紀初頭 |埋葬施設 =第1～第3主体部:割竹形木棺を納めた粘土槨
第4主体部:箱式石棺 |規模 =全長82メートル、後円部直径67メートル、高さ11メートル |被葬者 =不明 |出土品 =短甲、冑、頸甲、肩甲、鉄剣、直刀19振
鉄鏃25本以上、鉄製刀子、鉄鎌、
内向花文鏡、銅釧、石製模造品19点、
竪櫛30点以上、玉類2000点以上、靫ないし矢筒 |史跡 =東京都指定史跡「野毛大塚古墳」 |有形文化財 =出土品（国の[[重要文化財]]） |特記事項 = |地図2 = {{Location map|Japan Wards of Tokyo|label=野毛
大塚古墳|lat_deg=35|lat_min=36|lat_sec=18.1|lon_deg=139|lon_min=38|lon_sec=35.1|mark=帆立貝形古墳-南西.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=都内の位置}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|36|18.1|N|139|38|35.1|E}}}} }} '''野毛大塚古墳'''（のげおおつかこふん）は、[[東京都]][[世田谷区]][[野毛 (世田谷区)|野毛]]にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[帆立貝式古墳]]。東京都指定史跡に指定され、出土品は国の[[重要文化財]]に指定されている。 == 立地 == 世田谷区野毛1丁目の[[玉川野毛町公園]]内、[[武蔵野台地]]上の標高約30-33メートルのところにある。存在は[[江戸時代]]後期から知られていて、'''東大塚'''の名で呼ばれていた{{Cite book|和書|author=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「城南の遺跡」-世田谷周辺発掘調査最新報告- |publisher=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1993|pages=2-3}}。その後、'''上野毛古墳'''あるいは'''等々力ゴルフリンクス古墳'''と呼称された。[[丸子川 (東京都)|丸子川]]沿いの[[荏原台古墳群]]を代表する[[古墳]]。周辺には多くの古墳が分布している。 == 古墳概要 == 全長82メートル、直径66メートル、高さ11メートルの円墳に小さな前方部が付いた[[帆立貝式古墳]]（ほたてがいしきこふん）としては日本国内でも有数の規模を誇る。[[1897年]]（[[明治]]30年）に[[発掘調査]]がおこなわれ、石棺が見つかった。このときに見つかった多量の副葬品は、帝国博物館（後の[[東京国立博物館]]）に収蔵された。 墳丘では[[葺石]]・[[埴輪]]を確認している{{Cite book|和書|author1=世田谷区教育委員会|authorlink1=世田谷区教育委員会|author2=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「世田谷の三万年」-最近の発掘調査の成果から- |publisher=世田谷区教育委員会・世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1988|page=16}}。周りに馬蹄形の溝（周壕）がめぐらされている。 また、後円部に埋葬用の棺が4基あり、その第1主体部は割竹型の木棺は長さ8.2メートル、幅0.8～0.6メートルの細長く、その中から短甲（たんこう）、冑（かぶと）、頸甲（あかべよろい）、肩甲、鉄剣、直刀19振り、鉄鏃25本以上、鉄製刀子、鉄鎌、銅鏡（内向花文鏡）、銅釧（どうくしろ）刀子・手斧などの石製模造品19点、竪櫛30点以上、玉類2000点以上、靫（ゆき）ないし矢筒などで出土した。 [[箱式石棺]]からは[[勾玉]]・管玉・臼玉・鉄刀・剣・[[甲冑]]片などの武器・武具、玉類などのほか、[[滑石]]によってつくられた多種多様で大量の器具類が[[副葬品]]として納められていることを確認した。滑石製模造器具は、石履、石の[[台]]・坏・[[盤]]・[[甑]]・[[斧]]、233点におよぶ石製の[[刀子]]などである。刀子は14形式に分類されるなど、[[5世紀]]代古墳の示標となる[[考古資料]]となった。 これら調査成果は[[後藤守一]]によって[[1936年]]（[[昭和]]11年）によって報告書のかたちでまとめられている。 東京都の[[史跡]]であり、世田谷区遺跡番号127でもある{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.syougai.metro.tokyo.jp/iseki0/iseki/list/ruins/13112/112ruins.htm |title=世田谷区-遺跡一覧 東京都遺跡地図情報インターネット提供サービス |publisher=[[東京都教育委員会]] |accessdate=2016-07-03}}。 File:Noge Otsuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|主体部（左から第2・第1・第3主体部） File:Noge Otsuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|前方部（左）と造出（右） ファイル:野毛大塚古墳出土品.JPG|出土品
{{small|[[東京国立博物館]]展示。}} ファイル:野毛大塚古墳出土 石製槽.JPG|石製槽
{{small|東京国立博物館展示。}} ファイル:野毛大塚古墳出土 石製履.JPG|石製履
{{small|東京国立博物館展示。}} {{-}} == 文化財 == === 重要文化財（国指定） === * 東京都野毛大塚古墳出土品 一括（考古資料） - 明細は後出。所有者は世田谷区。世田谷区立郷土資料館保管。2016年（平成28年）8月17日指定平成28年8月17日文部科学省告示第116号。{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|00011732|東京都野毛大塚古墳出土品}}。 * 東京都野毛大塚古墳出土品 一括（考古資料） - 明細は後出。所有者は国立文化財機構。東京国立博物館保管。2017年（平成29年）9月15日指定平成29年9月15日文部科学省告示第117号。[https://web.archive.org/web/20170310112148/http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2017031002.html 国宝・重要文化財の指定について]（文化庁サイト）{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|00011758|東京都野毛大塚古墳出土品}}。 {{hidden begin |title = 国の重要文化財「東京都野毛大塚古墳出土品」の明細 |border = #aaa solid 1px |titlestyle = text-align: center; background:lightgrey; }} '''世田谷区所有分''' * 鉄製品 41点 *青銅製品 2点 * 石製模造品 19点 * 玉 32点 * 竪櫛 20点 （以上第一主体部出土） * 玉 2点 （以上第二主体部出土） * 鉄製品 159点 * 石製模造品 14点 （以上第三主体部出土） * 鉄製品 2点、水晶丸玉 2点 （以上第四主体部出土） * （附指定）埴輪残欠 11点 * （附指定）須恵器残欠 3点 * （附指定）土師器高坏 4点 （以上墳丘出土） '''東京国立博物館保管分''' * 石製模造品 248点 * 鉄製品残欠 9点 * 玉 29点 （以上第二主体部出土） * （附指定）円筒埴輪残欠 1点 （墳丘出土） {{hidden end}} === 東京都指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 野毛大塚古墳 - 1952年（昭和27年）11月3日に東京都指定旧跡に指定、1975年（昭和50年）2月6日に東京都指定史跡に変更[http://www.city.setagaya.lg.jp/kurashi/106/152/d00128557.html 東京都指定史跡　野毛大塚古墳]（世田谷区ホームページ）。。 なお、2001年（平成13年）3月5日に出土品2967点が「野毛大塚古墳主体部出土品」（宇奈根考古資料室・世田谷区立郷土資料館保管）として東京都指定有形文化財（考古資料）に指定されていたが、2016年（平成28年）8月17日の国の重要文化財指定に伴い指定解除されている{{PDFlink|[http://www.tokyoto-koho.metro.tokyo.jp/file/koho/id/3895/f/9305/16323.pdf 平成29年3月9日東京都公報]}}より東京都教育委員会告示第7号（リンクは東京都ホームページ）。。 == その他 == * 世田谷野毛郵便局の[[風景印]]には野毛大塚古墳が使われている[https://www.post.japanpost.jp/kitte_hagaki/stamp/fuke/detail.php?id=468 風景印 世田谷野毛郵便局]日本郵便。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * [[大塚初重]]・[[小林三郎 (考古学者)|小林三郎]]『古墳辞典』[[東京堂出版]]、1982年12月。ISBN 4-490-10165-1 * 後藤守一「東京府下の古墳」『東京府史跡名勝天然記念物調査報告書13』東京府、1936年。 * 世田谷区教育委員会・世田谷区立郷土資料館編集・発行『企画展「世田谷の三万年」-最近の発掘調査の成果から-』1988年。 * 世田谷区立郷土資料館編集・発行『 企画展「城南の遺跡」-世田谷周辺発掘調査最新報告-』1993年。 == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Noge-Otsuka Kofun}} * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/00011732 東京都野毛大塚古墳出土品（世田谷区所有）]、[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/00011758 東京都野毛大塚古墳出土品（東京国立博物館所有）] - 国指定文化財等データベース（[[文化庁]]） * [http://www.city.setagaya.lg.jp/kurashi/107/159/778/h26/d00135671.html 古代の暮らしを体験～野毛大塚古墳で「古墳まつり」開催] 世田谷区役所ウェブサイト　 {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:のけおおつかこふん}} [[Category:東京都の古墳]] [[Category:世田谷区の歴史]] [[Category:帆立貝形古墳]] [[Category:東京都区部の重要文化財]] [[Category:東京都指定史跡]]

Noge Ōtsuka Kofun (April 10, 2010) | location=Noge 1-chome, {{ill|Setagaya, Tokyo|ja|世田谷区|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都|12=draft|12=draft}} | coordinates={{coord|35|36|18.1|N|139|38|35.1|E|}} | part_of={{ill|Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|ja|荏原台古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}} | type=Scallop-shaped kofun | built=Early 5th century | burial=Main chambers 1–3: split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffins in clay coffins
Chamber 4: box-shaped stone coffin | area=82 m in length; posterior circular mound diameter 67 m; height 11 m | Interred_individual=Unknown | burial goods=Short armor, helmet, gorgets, shoulder guards, iron swords (19), iron arrowheads (25+), iron knives, iron sickles, {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡|12=draft|12=draft}}, bronze armlets, 19 stone replicas, over 30 combs, over 2,000 beads, and a quiver or arrow case | Historic Site={{ill|Tokyo Designated Historic Site|ja|東京都指定史跡|12=draft|12=draft}}: "Noge Ōtsuka Kofun" | Historic Site=excavations (Important Cultural Property of Japan) | Specialnotes= | map={{location map|Japan Wards of Tokyo|map_dot_label=Noge
Ōtsuka Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=36|lat_sec=18.1|lon_deg=139|lon_min=38|lon_sec=35.1|mark=帆立貝形古墳-南西.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Tokyo}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|36|18.1|N|139|38|35.1|E}}}} }} '''Noge Ōtsuka Kofun''' (野毛大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|12=draft|12=draft}} located in {{ill|Setagaya, Tokyo|ja|世田谷区|12=draft|12=draft}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都|12=draft|12=draft}}. It is scallop-shaped and Historic Site1 as a {{ill|Tokyo Designated Historic Site|ja|東京都指定史跡|12=draft|12=draft}}. burial goods excavated from the tumulus are Historic Site1 as Important Cultural Property of Japan. == location == Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is situated in {{ill|Tamagawa Nogemachi Park|ja|玉川野毛町公園|12=draft|12=draft}}, within the {{ill|Musashino Plateau|ja|武蔵野台地|12=draft|12=draft}} at an elevation of approximately 30–33 meters.{{Cite book|和書|author=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「城南の遺跡」-世田谷周辺発掘調査最新報告- |publisher=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1993|pages=2-3}} Known since the late Edo period, it was historically referred to as "Higashi Ōtsuka." It has also been called "Kaminoge Kofun" and "Todoroki Golf Links Kofun." The tumulus is part of the {{ill|Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|ja|荏原台古墳群|12=draft|12=draft}} and represents the region's ancient burial practices. == Overview == At 82 meters in length, with a diameter of 67 meters and a height of 11 meters, Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is one of Japan's largest scallop-shaped kofun (hotategai-shiki kofun). An excavation conducted in 1897 (Meiji 30) revealed a stone coffin, and the burial goods were transferred to the Imperial Museum (now {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft|12=draft}}). The mound is equi11ed with a stone covering (fukishi) and haniwa, and a horseshoe-shaped moat surrounds it.{{Cite book|和書|author1=世田谷区教育委員会|authorlink1=世田谷区教育委員会|author2=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「世田谷の三万年」-最近の発掘調査の成果から- |publisher=世田谷区教育委員会・世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1988|page=16}} == Burial Chambers == Four burial chambers are located in the posterior circular mound. The first chamber's split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin measures 8.2 meters in length and contains numerous grave goods, including: * Weapons and armor: short armor, helmets, gorgets, shoulder guards, 19 iron swords, and 25+ arrowheads * Mirrors and ornaments: {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡|12=draft|12=draft}}, bronze armlets, over 2,000 beads, and over 30 combs * Stone replicas: including axes and ceremonial tools burial goods from the box-shaped stone coffin include magatama, tubular beads, iron weapons, armor fragments, and a wide variety of stone replicas, such as bowls, plates, and knives made from steatite. == Cultural Significance == The kofun is recognized as an archaeological benchmark for understanding 5th-century burial practices. Detailed findings were compiled by {{ill|Morikazu Gotō|ja|後藤守一|12=draft|12=draft}} in 1936. == Designations == * Important Cultural Property of Japan (excavations): The burial goods are preserved at the {{ill|Setagaya Local History Museum|ja|世田谷区立郷土資料館|12=draft|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft|12=draft}}. * Tokyo Designated Historic Site: Designated as a Historic Site on February 6, 1975.{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.syougai.metro.tokyo.jp/iseki0/iseki/list/ruins/13112/112ruins.htm |title=世田谷区-遺跡一覧 東京都遺跡地図情報インターネット提供サービス |publisher=[[東京都教育委員会]] |accessdate=2016-07-03}} == Related Intypeation == * The landscape stamp of {{ill|Setagaya Noge Post Office|ja|世田谷野毛郵便局|12=draft|12=draft}} features Noge Ōtsuka Kofun.[https://www.post.japanpost.jp/kitte_hagaki/stamp/fuke/detail.php?id=468 Landscape Stamp: Setagaya Noge Post Office] (Japan Post). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Noge-Otsuka Kofun}} * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/00011732 burial goods of Noge Ōtsuka Kofun (Setagaya City Ownership)] - Historic Site Online (Agency for Cultural Affairs) * [http://www.city.setagaya.lg.jp/kurashi/107/159/778/h26/d00135671.html Experience Ancient Life at Noge Ōtsuka Kofun Festival] - Setagaya City website {{Japanese-history-stub}} of Tokyo]] {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Noge Ōtsuka Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Tokyo]] [[Category:History of Setagaya]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:[[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] [[Category:Tokyo Designated Historic Sites]] [[Category:Noge Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Noge Otsuka Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Noge Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[ja:野毛大塚古墳]]

Chamber 4: box-shaped stone coffin | area=82 m in length; posterior circular mound diameter 67 m; height 11 m | Interred_individual=Unknown | burial goods=Short armor, helmet, gorgets, shoulder guards, iron swords (19), iron arrowheads (25+), iron knives, iron sickles, {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡}}, bronze armlets, 19 stone replicas, over 30 combs, over 2,000 beads, and a quiver or arrow case | Historic Site={{ill|Tokyo Designated Historic Site|ja|東京都指定史跡}}: "Noge Ōtsuka Kofun" | Historic Site=excavations (Important Cultural Property of Japan) | Specialnotes= | map={{location map|Japan Wards of Tokyo|map_dot_label=Noge
Ōtsuka Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=36|lat_sec=18.1|lon_deg=139|lon_min=38|lon_sec=35.1|mark=帆立貝形古墳-南西.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Tokyo}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|36|18.1|N|139|38|35.1|E}}}} }} '''Noge Ōtsuka Kofun''' (野毛大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Setagaya, Tokyo|ja|世田谷区}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}}. It is scallop-shaped and Historic Site1 as a {{ill|Tokyo Designated Historic Site|ja|東京都指定史跡}}. burial goods excavated from the tumulus are Historic Site1 as Important Cultural Property of Japan. == location == Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is situated in {{ill|Tamagawa Nogemachi Park|ja|玉川野毛町公園}}, within the {{ill|Musashino Plateau|ja|武蔵野台地}} at an elevation of approximately 30–33 meters.{{Cite book|和書|author=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「城南の遺跡」-世田谷周辺発掘調査最新報告- |publisher=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1993|pages=2-3}} Known since the late Edo period, it was historically referred to as "Higashi Ōtsuka." It has also been called "Kaminoge Kofun" and "Todoroki Golf Links Kofun." The tumulus is part of the {{ill|Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|ja|荏原台古墳群}} and represents the region's ancient burial practices. == Overview == At 82 meters in length, with a diameter of 67 meters and a height of 11 meters, Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is one of Japan's largest scallop-shaped kofun (hotategai-shiki kofun). An excavation conducted in 1897 (Meiji 30) revealed a stone coffin, and the burial goods were transferred to the Imperial Museum (now {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}). The mound is equi11ed with a stone covering (fukishi) and haniwa, and a horseshoe-shaped moat surrounds it.{{Cite book|和書|author1=世田谷区教育委員会|authorlink1=世田谷区教育委員会|author2=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「世田谷の三万年」-最近の発掘調査の成果から- |publisher=世田谷区教育委員会・世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1988|page=16}} == Burial Chambers == Four burial chambers are located in the posterior circular mound. The first chamber's split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin measures 8.2 meters in length and contains numerous grave goods, including: * Weapons and armor: short armor, helmets, gorgets, shoulder guards, 19 iron swords, and 25+ arrowheads * Mirrors and ornaments: {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡}}, bronze armlets, over 2,000 beads, and over 30 combs * Stone replicas: including axes and ceremonial tools burial goods from the box-shaped stone coffin include magatama, tubular beads, iron weapons, armor fragments, and a wide variety of stone replicas, such as bowls, plates, and knives made from steatite. == Cultural Significance == The kofun is recognized as an archaeological benchmark for understanding 5th-century burial practices. Detailed findings were compiled by {{ill|Morikazu Gotō|ja|後藤守一}} in 1936. == Designations == * Important Cultural Property of Japan (excavations): The burial goods are preserved at the {{ill|Setagaya Local History Museum|ja|世田谷区立郷土資料館}} and the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. * Tokyo Designated Historic Site: Designated as a Historic Site on February 6, 1975.{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.syougai.metro.tokyo.jp/iseki0/iseki/list/ruins/13112/112ruins.htm |title=世田谷区-遺跡一覧 東京都遺跡地図情報インターネット提供サービス |publisher=[[東京都教育委員会]] |accessdate=2016-07-03}} == Related Intypeation == * The landscape stamp of {{ill|Setagaya Noge Post Office|ja|世田谷野毛郵便局}} features Noge Ōtsuka Kofun.[https://www.post.japanpost.jp/kitte_hagaki/stamp/fuke/detail.php?id=468 Landscape Stamp: Setagaya Noge Post Office] (Japan Post). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Noge-Otsuka Kofun}} * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/00011732 burial goods of Noge Ōtsuka Kofun (Setagaya City Ownership)] - Historic Site Online (Agency for Cultural Affairs) * [http://www.city.setagaya.lg.jp/kurashi/107/159/778/h26/d00135671.html Experience Ancient Life at Noge Ōtsuka Kofun Festival] - Setagaya City website {{Japanese-history-stub}} of Tokyo]] {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Noge Ōtsuka Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Tokyo]] [[Category:History of Setagaya]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:[[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] [[Category:Tokyo Designated Historic Sites]] [[Category:Noge Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Noge Otsuka Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Noge Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[ja:野毛大塚古墳]]

revision.comment: 52 revisions imported: Bot: import full history from ja:野毛大塚古墳 up to rev 104554573
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 =野毛大塚古墳 |別名 =東大塚、上野毛古墳など |画像 =[[画像:Noge Otsuka Kofun -01.jpg|260px]]
野毛大塚古墳（2010年4月10日） |所在地 =東京都世田谷区野毛1丁目 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|36|18.1|N|139|38|35.1|E|}} |所属 =[[荏原台古墳群]] |形状 =[[帆立貝形古墳]] |築造時期 =5世紀初頭 |埋葬施設 =第1～第3主体部:割竹形木棺を納めた粘土槨
第4主体部:箱式石棺 |規模 =全長82メートル、後円部直径67メートル、高さ11メートル |被葬者 =不明 |出土品 =短甲、冑、頸甲、肩甲、鉄剣、直刀19振
鉄鏃25本以上、鉄製刀子、鉄鎌、
内向花文鏡、銅釧、石製模造品19点、
竪櫛30点以上、玉類2000点以上、靫ないし矢筒 |史跡 =東京都指定史跡「野毛大塚古墳」 |有形文化財 =出土品（国の[[重要文化財]]） |特記事項 = |地図2 = {{Location map|Japan Wards of Tokyo|label=野毛
大塚古墳|lat_deg=35|lat_min=36|lat_sec=18.1|lon_deg=139|lon_min=38|lon_sec=35.1|mark=帆立貝形古墳-南西.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=都内の位置}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|36|18.1|N|139|38|35.1|E}}}} }} '''野毛大塚古墳'''（のげおおつかこふん）は、[[東京都]][[世田谷区]][[野毛 (世田谷区)|野毛]]にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[帆立貝式古墳]]。東京都指定史跡に指定され、出土品は国の[[重要文化財]]に指定されている。 == 立地 == 世田谷区野毛1丁目の[[玉川野毛町公園]]内、[[武蔵野台地]]上の標高約30-33メートルのところにある。存在は[[江戸時代]]後期から知られていて、'''東大塚'''の名で呼ばれていた{{Cite book|和書|author=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「城南の遺跡」-世田谷周辺発掘調査最新報告- |publisher=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1993|pages=2-3}}。その後、'''上野毛古墳'''あるいは'''等々力ゴルフリンクス古墳'''と呼称された。[[丸子川 (東京都)|丸子川]]沿いの[[荏原台古墳群]]を代表する[[古墳]]。周辺には多くの古墳が分布している。 == 古墳概要 == 全長82メートル、直径66メートル、高さ11メートルの円墳に小さな前方部が付いた[[帆立貝式古墳]]（ほたてがいしきこふん）としては日本国内でも有数の規模を誇る。[[1897年]]（[[明治]]30年）に[[発掘調査]]がおこなわれ、石棺が見つかった。このときに見つかった多量の副葬品は、帝国博物館（後の[[東京国立博物館]]）に収蔵された。 墳丘では[[葺石]]・[[埴輪]]を確認している{{Cite book|和書|author1=世田谷区教育委員会|authorlink1=世田谷区教育委員会|author2=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「世田谷の三万年」-最近の発掘調査の成果から- |publisher=世田谷区教育委員会・世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1988|page=16}}。周りに馬蹄形の溝（周壕）がめぐらされている。 また、後円部に埋葬用の棺が4基あり、その第1主体部は割竹型の木棺は長さ8.2メートル、幅0.8～0.6メートルの細長く、その中から短甲（たんこう）、冑（かぶと）、頸甲（あかべよろい）、肩甲、鉄剣、直刀19振り、鉄鏃25本以上、鉄製刀子、鉄鎌、銅鏡（内向花文鏡）、銅釧（どうくしろ）刀子・手斧などの石製模造品19点、竪櫛30点以上、玉類2000点以上、靫（ゆき）ないし矢筒などで出土した。 [[箱式石棺]]からは[[勾玉]]・管玉・臼玉・鉄刀・剣・[[甲冑]]片などの武器・武具、玉類などのほか、[[滑石]]によってつくられた多種多様で大量の器具類が[[副葬品]]として納められていることを確認した。滑石製模造器具は、石履、石の[[台]]・坏・[[盤]]・[[甑]]・[[斧]]、233点におよぶ石製の[[刀子]]などである。刀子は14形式に分類されるなど、[[5世紀]]代古墳の示標となる[[考古資料]]となった。 これら調査成果は[[後藤守一]]によって[[1936年]]（[[昭和]]11年）によって報告書のかたちでまとめられている。 東京都の[[史跡]]であり、世田谷区遺跡番号127でもある{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.syougai.metro.tokyo.jp/iseki0/iseki/list/ruins/13112/112ruins.htm |title=世田谷区-遺跡一覧 東京都遺跡地図情報インターネット提供サービス |publisher=[[東京都教育委員会]] |accessdate=2016-07-03}}。 File:Noge Otsuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|主体部（左から第2・第1・第3主体部） File:Noge Otsuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|前方部（左）と造出（右） ファイル:野毛大塚古墳出土品.JPG|出土品
{{small|[[東京国立博物館]]展示。}} ファイル:野毛大塚古墳出土 石製槽.JPG|石製槽
{{small|東京国立博物館展示。}} ファイル:野毛大塚古墳出土 石製履.JPG|石製履
{{small|東京国立博物館展示。}} {{-}} == 文化財 == === 重要文化財（国指定） === * 東京都野毛大塚古墳出土品 一括（考古資料） - 明細は後出。所有者は世田谷区。世田谷区立郷土資料館保管。2016年（平成28年）8月17日指定平成28年8月17日文部科学省告示第116号。{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|00011732|東京都野毛大塚古墳出土品}}。 * 東京都野毛大塚古墳出土品 一括（考古資料） - 明細は後出。所有者は国立文化財機構。東京国立博物館保管。2017年（平成29年）9月15日指定平成29年9月15日文部科学省告示第117号。[https://web.archive.org/web/20170310112148/http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2017031002.html 国宝・重要文化財の指定について]（文化庁サイト）{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|00011758|東京都野毛大塚古墳出土品}}。 {{hidden begin |title = 国の重要文化財「東京都野毛大塚古墳出土品」の明細 |border = #aaa solid 1px |titlestyle = text-align: center; background:lightgrey; }} '''世田谷区所有分''' * 鉄製品 41点 *青銅製品 2点 * 石製模造品 19点 * 玉 32点 * 竪櫛 20点 （以上第一主体部出土） * 玉 2点 （以上第二主体部出土） * 鉄製品 159点 * 石製模造品 14点 （以上第三主体部出土） * 鉄製品 2点、水晶丸玉 2点 （以上第四主体部出土） * （附指定）埴輪残欠 11点 * （附指定）須恵器残欠 3点 * （附指定）土師器高坏 4点 （以上墳丘出土） '''東京国立博物館保管分''' * 石製模造品 248点 * 鉄製品残欠 9点 * 玉 29点 （以上第二主体部出土） * （附指定）円筒埴輪残欠 1点 （墳丘出土） {{hidden end}} === 東京都指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 野毛大塚古墳 - 1952年（昭和27年）11月3日に東京都指定旧跡に指定、1975年（昭和50年）2月6日に東京都指定史跡に変更[http://www.city.setagaya.lg.jp/kurashi/106/152/d00128557.html 東京都指定史跡　野毛大塚古墳]（世田谷区ホームページ）。。 なお、2001年（平成13年）3月5日に出土品2967点が「野毛大塚古墳主体部出土品」（宇奈根考古資料室・世田谷区立郷土資料館保管）として東京都指定有形文化財（考古資料）に指定されていたが、2016年（平成28年）8月17日の国の重要文化財指定に伴い指定解除されている{{PDFlink|[http://www.tokyoto-koho.metro.tokyo.jp/file/koho/id/3895/f/9305/16323.pdf 平成29年3月9日東京都公報]}}より東京都教育委員会告示第7号（リンクは東京都ホームページ）。。 == その他 == * 世田谷野毛郵便局の[[風景印]]には野毛大塚古墳が使われている[https://www.post.japanpost.jp/kitte_hagaki/stamp/fuke/detail.php?id=468 風景印 世田谷野毛郵便局]日本郵便。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * [[大塚初重]]・[[小林三郎 (考古学者)|小林三郎]]『古墳辞典』[[東京堂出版]]、1982年12月。ISBN 4-490-10165-1 * 後藤守一「東京府下の古墳」『東京府史跡名勝天然記念物調査報告書13』東京府、1936年。 * 世田谷区教育委員会・世田谷区立郷土資料館編集・発行『企画展「世田谷の三万年」-最近の発掘調査の成果から-』1988年。 * 世田谷区立郷土資料館編集・発行『 企画展「城南の遺跡」-世田谷周辺発掘調査最新報告-』1993年。 == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Noge-Otsuka Kofun}} * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/00011732 東京都野毛大塚古墳出土品（世田谷区所有）]、[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/00011758 東京都野毛大塚古墳出土品（東京国立博物館所有）] - 国指定文化財等データベース（[[文化庁]]） * [http://www.city.setagaya.lg.jp/kurashi/107/159/778/h26/d00135671.html 古代の暮らしを体験～野毛大塚古墳で「古墳まつり」開催] 世田谷区役所ウェブサイト　 {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:のけおおつかこふん}} [[Category:東京都の古墳]] [[Category:世田谷区の歴史]] [[Category:帆立貝形古墳]] [[Category:東京都区部の重要文化財]] [[Category:東京都指定史跡]]

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[野毛大塚古墳]] to [[Noge Ōtsuka Kofun]] without leaving a redirect: Bot: merge import
revision.text:
{{日本の古墳 |名称 =野毛大塚古墳 |別名 =東大塚、上野毛古墳など |画像 =[[画像:Noge Otsuka Kofun -01.jpg|260px]]
野毛大塚古墳（2010年4月10日） |所在地 =東京都世田谷区野毛1丁目 |位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|36|18.1|N|139|38|35.1|E|}} |所属 =[[荏原台古墳群]] |形状 =[[帆立貝形古墳]] |築造時期 =5世紀初頭 |埋葬施設 =第1～第3主体部:割竹形木棺を納めた粘土槨
第4主体部:箱式石棺 |規模 =全長82メートル、後円部直径67メートル、高さ11メートル |被葬者 =不明 |出土品 =短甲、冑、頸甲、肩甲、鉄剣、直刀19振
鉄鏃25本以上、鉄製刀子、鉄鎌、
内向花文鏡、銅釧、石製模造品19点、
竪櫛30点以上、玉類2000点以上、靫ないし矢筒 |史跡 =東京都指定史跡「野毛大塚古墳」 |有形文化財 =出土品（国の[[重要文化財]]） |特記事項 = |地図2 = {{Location map|Japan Wards of Tokyo|label=野毛
大塚古墳|lat_deg=35|lat_min=36|lat_sec=18.1|lon_deg=139|lon_min=38|lon_sec=35.1|mark=帆立貝形古墳-南西.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=都内の位置}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|36|18.1|N|139|38|35.1|E}}}} }} '''野毛大塚古墳'''（のげおおつかこふん）は、[[東京都]][[世田谷区]][[野毛 (世田谷区)|野毛]]にある[[古墳]]。形状は[[帆立貝式古墳]]。東京都指定史跡に指定され、出土品は国の[[重要文化財]]に指定されている。 == 立地 == 世田谷区野毛1丁目の[[玉川野毛町公園]]内、[[武蔵野台地]]上の標高約30-33メートルのところにある。存在は[[江戸時代]]後期から知られていて、'''東大塚'''の名で呼ばれていた{{Cite book|和書|author=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「城南の遺跡」-世田谷周辺発掘調査最新報告- |publisher=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1993|pages=2-3}}。その後、'''上野毛古墳'''あるいは'''等々力ゴルフリンクス古墳'''と呼称された。[[丸子川 (東京都)|丸子川]]沿いの[[荏原台古墳群]]を代表する[[古墳]]。周辺には多くの古墳が分布している。 == 古墳概要 == 全長82メートル、直径66メートル、高さ11メートルの円墳に小さな前方部が付いた[[帆立貝式古墳]]（ほたてがいしきこふん）としては日本国内でも有数の規模を誇る。[[1897年]]（[[明治]]30年）に[[発掘調査]]がおこなわれ、石棺が見つかった。このときに見つかった多量の副葬品は、帝国博物館（後の[[東京国立博物館]]）に収蔵された。 墳丘では[[葺石]]・[[埴輪]]を確認している{{Cite book|和書|author1=世田谷区教育委員会|authorlink1=世田谷区教育委員会|author2=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「世田谷の三万年」-最近の発掘調査の成果から- |publisher=世田谷区教育委員会・世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1988|page=16}}。周りに馬蹄形の溝（周壕）がめぐらされている。 また、後円部に埋葬用の棺が4基あり、その第1主体部は割竹型の木棺は長さ8.2メートル、幅0.8～0.6メートルの細長く、その中から短甲（たんこう）、冑（かぶと）、頸甲（あかべよろい）、肩甲、鉄剣、直刀19振り、鉄鏃25本以上、鉄製刀子、鉄鎌、銅鏡（内向花文鏡）、銅釧（どうくしろ）刀子・手斧などの石製模造品19点、竪櫛30点以上、玉類2000点以上、靫（ゆき）ないし矢筒などで出土した。 [[箱式石棺]]からは[[勾玉]]・管玉・臼玉・鉄刀・剣・[[甲冑]]片などの武器・武具、玉類などのほか、[[滑石]]によってつくられた多種多様で大量の器具類が[[副葬品]]として納められていることを確認した。滑石製模造器具は、石履、石の[[台]]・坏・[[盤]]・[[甑]]・[[斧]]、233点におよぶ石製の[[刀子]]などである。刀子は14形式に分類されるなど、[[5世紀]]代古墳の示標となる[[考古資料]]となった。 これら調査成果は[[後藤守一]]によって[[1936年]]（[[昭和]]11年）によって報告書のかたちでまとめられている。 東京都の[[史跡]]であり、世田谷区遺跡番号127でもある{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.syougai.metro.tokyo.jp/iseki0/iseki/list/ruins/13112/112ruins.htm |title=世田谷区-遺跡一覧 東京都遺跡地図情報インターネット提供サービス |publisher=[[東京都教育委員会]] |accessdate=2016-07-03}}。 File:Noge Otsuka Kofun, funcho.jpg|主体部（左から第2・第1・第3主体部） File:Noge Otsuka Kofun, zenpoubu.jpg|前方部（左）と造出（右） ファイル:野毛大塚古墳出土品.JPG|出土品
{{small|[[東京国立博物館]]展示。}} ファイル:野毛大塚古墳出土 石製槽.JPG|石製槽
{{small|東京国立博物館展示。}} ファイル:野毛大塚古墳出土 石製履.JPG|石製履
{{small|東京国立博物館展示。}} {{-}} == 文化財 == === 重要文化財（国指定） === * 東京都野毛大塚古墳出土品 一括（考古資料） - 明細は後出。所有者は世田谷区。世田谷区立郷土資料館保管。2016年（平成28年）8月17日指定平成28年8月17日文部科学省告示第116号。{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|00011732|東京都野毛大塚古墳出土品}}。 * 東京都野毛大塚古墳出土品 一括（考古資料） - 明細は後出。所有者は国立文化財機構。東京国立博物館保管。2017年（平成29年）9月15日指定平成29年9月15日文部科学省告示第117号。[https://web.archive.org/web/20170310112148/http://www.bunka.go.jp/koho_hodo_oshirase/hodohappyo/2017031002.html 国宝・重要文化財の指定について]（文化庁サイト）{{国指定文化財等データベース|201|00011758|東京都野毛大塚古墳出土品}}。 {{hidden begin |title = 国の重要文化財「東京都野毛大塚古墳出土品」の明細 |border = #aaa solid 1px |titlestyle = text-align: center; background:lightgrey; }} '''世田谷区所有分''' * 鉄製品 41点 *青銅製品 2点 * 石製模造品 19点 * 玉 32点 * 竪櫛 20点 （以上第一主体部出土） * 玉 2点 （以上第二主体部出土） * 鉄製品 159点 * 石製模造品 14点 （以上第三主体部出土） * 鉄製品 2点、水晶丸玉 2点 （以上第四主体部出土） * （附指定）埴輪残欠 11点 * （附指定）須恵器残欠 3点 * （附指定）土師器高坏 4点 （以上墳丘出土） '''東京国立博物館保管分''' * 石製模造品 248点 * 鉄製品残欠 9点 * 玉 29点 （以上第二主体部出土） * （附指定）円筒埴輪残欠 1点 （墳丘出土） {{hidden end}} === 東京都指定文化財 === * 史跡 ** 野毛大塚古墳 - 1952年（昭和27年）11月3日に東京都指定旧跡に指定、1975年（昭和50年）2月6日に東京都指定史跡に変更[http://www.city.setagaya.lg.jp/kurashi/106/152/d00128557.html 東京都指定史跡　野毛大塚古墳]（世田谷区ホームページ）。。 なお、2001年（平成13年）3月5日に出土品2967点が「野毛大塚古墳主体部出土品」（宇奈根考古資料室・世田谷区立郷土資料館保管）として東京都指定有形文化財（考古資料）に指定されていたが、2016年（平成28年）8月17日の国の重要文化財指定に伴い指定解除されている{{PDFlink|[http://www.tokyoto-koho.metro.tokyo.jp/file/koho/id/3895/f/9305/16323.pdf 平成29年3月9日東京都公報]}}より東京都教育委員会告示第7号（リンクは東京都ホームページ）。。 == その他 == * 世田谷野毛郵便局の[[風景印]]には野毛大塚古墳が使われている[https://www.post.japanpost.jp/kitte_hagaki/stamp/fuke/detail.php?id=468 風景印 世田谷野毛郵便局]日本郵便。 == 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * [[大塚初重]]・[[小林三郎 (考古学者)|小林三郎]]『古墳辞典』[[東京堂出版]]、1982年12月。ISBN 4-490-10165-1 * 後藤守一「東京府下の古墳」『東京府史跡名勝天然記念物調査報告書13』東京府、1936年。 * 世田谷区教育委員会・世田谷区立郷土資料館編集・発行『企画展「世田谷の三万年」-最近の発掘調査の成果から-』1988年。 * 世田谷区立郷土資料館編集・発行『 企画展「城南の遺跡」-世田谷周辺発掘調査最新報告-』1993年。 == 外部リンク == {{Commonscat|Noge-Otsuka Kofun}} * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/00011732 東京都野毛大塚古墳出土品（世田谷区所有）]、[https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/00011758 東京都野毛大塚古墳出土品（東京国立博物館所有）] - 国指定文化財等データベース（[[文化庁]]） * [http://www.city.setagaya.lg.jp/kurashi/107/159/778/h26/d00135671.html 古代の暮らしを体験～野毛大塚古墳で「古墳まつり」開催] 世田谷区役所ウェブサイト　 {{Japanese-history-stub}} {{DEFAULTSORT:のけおおつかこふん}} [[Category:東京都の古墳]] [[Category:世田谷区の歴史]] [[Category:帆立貝形古墳]] [[Category:東京都区部の重要文化財]] [[Category:東京都指定史跡]]

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳}} | name=Noge Ōtsuka Kofun | alternate_name=Higashi Ōtsuka, Kaminoge Kofun, etc. | image=[[File:Noge Otsuka Kofun -01.jpg|260px]]
Noge Ōtsuka Kofun (April 10, 2010) | location=Noge 1-chome, {{ill|Setagaya, Tokyo|ja|世田谷区}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}} | coordinates={{coord|35|36|18.1|N|139|38|35.1|E|}} | part_of={{ill|Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|ja|荏原台古墳群}} | type=Scallop-shaped kofun | built=Early 5th century | burial=Main chambers 1–3: split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffins in clay coffins
Chamber 4: box-shaped stone coffin | area=82 m in length; posterior circular mound diameter 67 m; height 11 m | Interred_individual=Unknown | burial goods=Short armor, helmet, gorgets, shoulder guards, iron swords (19), iron arrowheads (25+), iron knives, iron sickles, {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡}}, bronze armlets, 19 stone replicas, over 30 combs, over 2,000 beads, and a quiver or arrow case | Historic Site={{ill|Tokyo Designated Historic Site|ja|東京都指定史跡}}: "Noge Ōtsuka Kofun" | Historic Site=excavations (Important Cultural Property of Japan) | Specialnotes= | map={{location map|Japan Wards of Tokyo|map_dot_label=Noge
Ōtsuka Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=36|lat_sec=18.1|lon_deg=139|lon_min=38|lon_sec=35.1|mark=帆立貝形古墳-南西.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Tokyo}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|36|18.1|N|139|38|35.1|E}}}} }} '''Noge Ōtsuka Kofun''' (野毛大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Setagaya, Tokyo|ja|世田谷区}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都}}. It is scallop-shaped and Historic Site1 as a {{ill|Tokyo Designated Historic Site|ja|東京都指定史跡}}. burial goods excavated from the tumulus are Historic Site1 as Important Cultural Property of Japan. == location == Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is situated in {{ill|Tamagawa Nogemachi Park|ja|玉川野毛町公園}}, within the {{ill|Musashino Plateau|ja|武蔵野台地}} at an elevation of approximately 30–33 meters.{{Cite book|和書|author=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「城南の遺跡」-世田谷周辺発掘調査最新報告- |publisher=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1993|pages=2-3}} Known since the late Edo period, it was historically referred to as "Higashi Ōtsuka." It has also been called "Kaminoge Kofun" and "Todoroki Golf Links Kofun." The tumulus is part of the {{ill|Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|ja|荏原台古墳群}} and represents the region's ancient burial practices. == Overview == At 82 meters in length, with a diameter of 67 meters and a height of 11 meters, Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is one of Japan's largest scallop-shaped kofun (hotategai-shiki kofun). An excavation conducted in 1897 (Meiji 30) revealed a stone coffin, and the burial goods were transferred to the Imperial Museum (now {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}). The mound is equi11ed with a stone covering (fukishi) and haniwa, and a horseshoe-shaped moat surrounds it.{{Cite book|和書|author1=世田谷区教育委員会|authorlink1=世田谷区教育委員会|author2=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「世田谷の三万年」-最近の発掘調査の成果から- |publisher=世田谷区教育委員会・世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1988|page=16}} == Burial Chambers == Four burial chambers are located in the posterior circular mound. The first chamber's split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin measures 8.2 meters in length and contains numerous grave goods, including: * Weapons and armor: short armor, helmets, gorgets, shoulder guards, 19 iron swords, and 25+ arrowheads * Mirrors and ornaments: {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡}}, bronze armlets, over 2,000 beads, and over 30 combs * Stone replicas: including axes and ceremonial tools burial goods from the box-shaped stone coffin include magatama, tubular beads, iron weapons, armor fragments, and a wide variety of stone replicas, such as bowls, plates, and knives made from steatite. == Cultural Significance == The kofun is recognized as an archaeological benchmark for understanding 5th-century burial practices. Detailed findings were compiled by {{ill|Morikazu Gotō|ja|後藤守一}} in 1936. == Designations == * Important Cultural Property of Japan (excavations): The burial goods are preserved at the {{ill|Setagaya Local History Museum|ja|世田谷区立郷土資料館}} and the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館}}. * Tokyo Designated Historic Site: Designated as a Historic Site on February 6, 1975.{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.syougai.metro.tokyo.jp/iseki0/iseki/list/ruins/13112/112ruins.htm |title=世田谷区-遺跡一覧 東京都遺跡地図情報インターネット提供サービス |publisher=[[東京都教育委員会]] |accessdate=2016-07-03}} == Related Intypeation == * The landscape stamp of {{ill|Setagaya Noge Post Office|ja|世田谷野毛郵便局}} features Noge Ōtsuka Kofun.[https://www.post.japanpost.jp/kitte_hagaki/stamp/fuke/detail.php?id=468 Landscape Stamp: Setagaya Noge Post Office] (Japan Post). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Noge-Otsuka Kofun}} * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/00011732 burial goods of Noge Ōtsuka Kofun (Setagaya City Ownership)] - Historic Site Online (Agency for Cultural Affairs) * [http://www.city.setagaya.lg.jp/kurashi/107/159/778/h26/d00135671.html Experience Ancient Life at Noge Ōtsuka Kofun Festival] - Setagaya City website {{Japanese-history-stub}} of Tokyo]] {{draft categories| }} {{citation needed|Noge Ōtsuka Kofun}} {{Kofun navbar}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Tokyo]] [[Category:History of Setagaya]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:[[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] [[Category:Tokyo Designated Historic Sites]] [[Category:Noge Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Noge Otsuka Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Noge Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[ja:野毛大塚古墳]] {{translated page|ja|野毛大塚古墳|version=104554573|comment=Imported full ja history}}

Chamber 4: box-shaped stone coffin | area=82 m in length; posterior circular mound diameter 67 m; height 11 m | Interred_individual=Unknown | burial goods=Short armor, helmet, gorgets, shoulder guards, iron swords (19), iron arrowheads (25+), iron knives, iron sickles, {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡|12=draft}}, bronze armlets, 19 stone replicas, over 30 combs, over 2,000 beads, and a quiver or arrow case | Historic Site={{ill|Tokyo Designated Historic Site|ja|東京都指定史跡|12=draft}}: "Noge Ōtsuka Kofun" | Historic Site=excavations (Important Cultural Property of Japan) | Specialnotes= | map={{location map|Japan Wards of Tokyo|map_dot_label=Noge

It is scallop-shaped and Historic Site1 as a {{ill|Tokyo Designated Historic Site|ja|東京都指定史跡|12=draft}}. burial goods excavated from the tumulus are Historic Site1 as Important Cultural Property of Japan. == location == Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is situated in {{ill|Tamagawa Nogemachi Park|ja|玉川野毛町公園|12=draft}}, within the {{ill|Musashino Plateau|ja|武蔵野台地|12=draft}} at an elevation of approximately 30–33 meters.{{Cite book|和書|author=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「城南の遺跡」-世田谷周辺発掘調査最新報告- |publisher=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1993|pages=2-3}} Known since the late Edo period, it was historically referred to as "Higashi Ōtsuka." The tumulus is part of the {{ill|Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|ja|荏原台古墳群|12=draft}} and represents the region's ancient burial practices. == Overview == At 82 meters in length, with a diameter of 67 meters and a height of 11 meters, Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is one of Japan's largest scallop-shaped kofun (hotategai-shiki kofun). An excavation conducted in 1897 (Meiji 30) revealed a stone coffin, and the burial goods were transferred to the Imperial Museum (now {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft}}). The mound is equi11ed with a stone covering (fukishi) and haniwa, and a horseshoe-shaped moat surrounds it.{{Cite book|和書|author1=世田谷区教育委員会|authorlink1=世田谷区教育委員会|author2=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「世田谷の三万年」-最近の発掘調査の成果から- |publisher=世田谷区教育委員会・世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1988|page=16}} == Burial Chambers == Four burial chambers are located in the posterior circular mound. The first chamber's split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin measures 8.2 meters in length and contains numerous grave goods, including: * Weapons and armor: short armor, helmets, gorgets, shoulder guards, 19 iron swords, and 25+ arrowheads * Mirrors and ornaments: {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡|12=draft}}, bronze armlets, over 2,000 beads, and over 30 combs * Stone replicas: including axes and ceremonial tools burial goods from the box-shaped stone coffin include magatama, tubular beads, iron weapons, armor fragments, and a wide variety of stone replicas, such as bowls, plates, and knives made from steatite. == Cultural Significance == The kofun is recognized as an archaeological benchmark for understanding 5th-century burial practices. Detailed findings were compiled by {{ill|Morikazu Gotō|ja|後藤守一|12=draft}} in 1936. == Designations == * Important Cultural Property of Japan (excavations): The burial goods are preserved at the {{ill|Setagaya Local History Museum|ja|世田谷区立郷土資料館|12=draft}} and the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|12=draft}}. * Tokyo Designated Historic Site: Designated as a Historic Site on February 6, 1975.{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.syougai.metro.tokyo.jp/iseki0/iseki/list/ruins/13112/112ruins.htm |title=世田谷区-遺跡一覧 東京都遺跡地図情報インターネット提供サービス |publisher=[[東京都教育委員会]] |accessdate=2016-07-03}} == Related Intypeation == * The landscape stamp of {{ill|Setagaya Noge Post Office|ja|世田谷野毛郵便局|12=draft}} features Noge Ōtsuka Kofun.[https://www.post.japanpost.jp/kitte_hagaki/stamp/fuke/detail.php?id=468 Landscape Stamp: Setagaya Noge Post Office] (Japan Post). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Noge-Otsuka Kofun}} * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/00011732 burial goods of Noge Ōtsuka Kofun (Setagaya City Ownership)] - The tumulus is part of the {{ill|Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|ja|荏原台古墳群|qq=draft}} and represents the region's ancient burial practices. == Overview == At 82 meters in length, with a diameter of 67 meters and a height of 11 meters, Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is one of Japan's largest scallop-shaped kofun (hotategai-shiki kofun). An excavation conducted in 1897 (Meiji 30) revealed a stone coffin, and the burial goods were transferred to the Imperial Museum (now {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|qq=draft}}). The mound is equi11ed with a stone covering (fukishi) and haniwa, and a horseshoe-shaped moat surrounds it.{{Cite book|和書|author1=世田谷区教育委員会|authorlink1=世田谷区教育委員会|author2=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「世田谷の三万年」-最近の発掘調査の成果から- |publisher=世田谷区教育委員会・世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1988|page=16}} == Burial Chambers == Four burial chambers are located in the posterior circular mound. The first chamber's split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin measures 8.2 meters in length and contains numerous grave goods, including: * Weapons and armor: short armor, helmets, gorgets, shoulder guards, 19 iron swords, and 25+ arrowheads * Mirrors and ornaments: {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡|qq=draft}}, bronze armlets, over 2,000 beads, and over 30 combs * Stone replicas: including axes and ceremonial tools burial goods from the box-shaped stone coffin include magatama, tubular beads, iron weapons, armor fragments, and a wide variety of stone replicas, such as bowls, plates, and knives made from steatite. == Cultural Significance == The kofun is recognized as an archaeological benchmark for understanding 5th-century burial practices. Detailed findings were compiled by {{ill|Morikazu Gotō|ja|後藤守一|qq=draft}} in 1936. == Designations == * Important Cultural Property of Japan (excavations): The burial goods are preserved at the {{ill|Setagaya Local History Museum|ja|世田谷区立郷土資料館|qq=draft}} and the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|qq=draft}}. * Tokyo Designated Historic Site: Designated as a Historic Site on February 6, 1975.{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.syougai.metro.tokyo.jp/iseki0/iseki/list/ruins/13112/112ruins.htm |title=世田谷区-遺跡一覧 東京都遺跡地図情報インターネット提供サービス |publisher=[[東京都教育委員会]] |accessdate=2016-07-03}} == Related Intypeation == * The landscape stamp of {{ill|Setagaya Noge Post Office|ja|世田谷野毛郵便局|qq=draft}} features Noge Ōtsuka Kofun.[https://www.post.japanpost.jp/kitte_hagaki/stamp/fuke/detail.php?id=468 Landscape Stamp: Setagaya Noge Post Office] (Japan Post). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Noge-Otsuka Kofun}} * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/00011732 burial goods of Noge Ōtsuka Kofun (Setagaya City Ownership)] - Historic Site Online (Agency for Cultural Affairs) * [http://www.city.setagaya.lg.jp/kurashi/107/159/778/h26/d00135671.html Experience Ancient Life at Noge Ōtsuka Kofun Festival] - Setagaya City website {{Japanese-history-stub}} of Tokyo]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|野毛大塚古墳|version=104554573|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Tokyo]] [[Category:History of Setagaya]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:[[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] [[Category:Tokyo Designated Historic Sites]] [[Category:Noge Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[Category:Noge Otsuka Kofun]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Noge Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[ja:野毛大塚古墳]]

But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=}} | name=Noge Ōtsuka Kofun | alternate_name=Higashi Ōtsuka, Kaminoge Kofun, etc. | image=[[File:Noge Otsuka Kofun -01.jpg|260px]]
Noge Ōtsuka Kofun (April 10, 2010) | location=Noge 1-chome, {{ill|Setagaya, Tokyo|ja|世田谷区|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setagaya, Tokyo|qq=}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Metropolis|qq=}} | coordinates={{coord|35|36|18.1|N|139|38|35.1|E|}} | part_of={{ill|Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|ja|荏原台古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|qq=}} | type=Scallop-shaped kofun | built=Early 5th century | burial=Main chambers 1–3: split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffins in clay coffins
Chamber 4: box-shaped stone coffin | area=82 m in length; posterior circular mound diameter 67 m; height 11 m | Interred_individual=Unknown | burial goods=Short armor, helmet, gorgets, shoulder guards, iron swords (19), iron arrowheads (25+), iron knives, iron sickles, {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uchimukemonkyō|qq=}}, bronze armlets, 19 stone replicas, over 30 combs, over 2,000 beads, and a quiver or arrow case | Historic Site={{ill|Tokyo Designated Historic Site|ja|東京都指定史跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Designated Historic Site|qq=}}: "Noge Ōtsuka Kofun" | Historic Site=excavations (Important Cultural Property of Japan) | Specialnotes= | map={{location map|Japan Wards of Tokyo|map_dot_label=Noge
Ōtsuka Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=36|lat_sec=18.1|lon_deg=139|lon_min=38|lon_sec=35.1|mark=帆立貝形古墳-南西.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Tokyo}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|36|18.1|N|139|38|35.1|E}}}} }} '''Noge Ōtsuka Kofun''' (野毛大塚古墳) is a {{ill|kofun|ja|古墳|1=Kofun|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Kofun|qq=}} located in {{ill|Setagaya, Tokyo|ja|世田谷区|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setagaya, Tokyo|qq=}}, {{ill|Tokyo Metropolis|ja|東京都|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Metropolis|qq=}}. It is scallop-shaped and Historic Site1 as a {{ill|Tokyo Designated Historic Site|ja|東京都指定史跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Designated Historic Site|qq=}}. burial goods excavated from the tumulus are Historic Site1 as Important Cultural Property of Japan. == location == Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is situated in {{ill|Tamagawa Nogemachi Park|ja|玉川野毛町公園|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamagawa Nogemachi Park|qq=}}, within the {{ill|Musashino Plateau|ja|武蔵野台地|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Musashino Plateau|qq=}} at an elevation of approximately 30–33 meters.{{Cite book|和書|author=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「城南の遺跡」-世田谷周辺発掘調査最新報告- |publisher=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1993|pages=2-3}} Known since the late Edo period, it was historically referred to as "Higashi Ōtsuka." It has also been called "Kaminoge Kofun" and "Todoroki Golf Links Kofun." The tumulus is part of the {{ill|Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|ja|荏原台古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|qq=}} and represents the region's ancient burial practices. == Overview == At 82 meters in length, with a diameter of 67 meters and a height of 11 meters, Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is one of Japan's largest scallop-shaped kofun (hotategai-shiki kofun). An excavation conducted in 1897 (Meiji 30) revealed a stone coffin, and the burial goods were transferred to the Imperial Museum (now {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo National Museum|qq=}}). The mound is equi11ed with a stone covering (fukishi) and haniwa, and a horseshoe-shaped moat surrounds it.{{Cite book|和書|author1=世田谷区教育委員会|authorlink1=世田谷区教育委員会|author2=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「世田谷の三万年」-最近の発掘調査の成果から- |publisher=世田谷区教育委員会・世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1988|page=16}} == Burial Chambers == Four burial chambers are located in the posterior circular mound. The first chamber's split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin measures 8.2 meters in length and contains numerous grave goods, including: * Weapons and armor: short armor, helmets, gorgets, shoulder guards, 19 iron swords, and 25+ arrowheads * Mirrors and ornaments: {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uchimukemonkyō|qq=}}, bronze armlets, over 2,000 beads, and over 30 combs * Stone replicas: including axes and ceremonial tools burial goods from the box-shaped stone coffin include magatama, tubular beads, iron weapons, armor fragments, and a wide variety of stone replicas, such as bowls, plates, and knives made from steatite. == Cultural Significance == The kofun is recognized as an archaeological benchmark for understanding 5th-century burial practices. Detailed findings were compiled by {{ill|Morikazu Gotō|ja|後藤守一|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morikazu Gotō|qq=}} in 1936. == Designations == * Important Cultural Property of Japan (excavations): The burial goods are preserved at the {{ill|Setagaya Local History Museum|ja|世田谷区立郷土資料館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setagaya Local History Museum|qq=}} and the {{ill|Tokyo National Museum|ja|東京国立博物館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo National Museum|qq=}}. * Tokyo Designated Historic Site: Designated as a Historic Site on February 6, 1975.{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.syougai.metro.tokyo.jp/iseki0/iseki/list/ruins/13112/112ruins.htm |title=世田谷区-遺跡一覧 東京都遺跡地図情報インターネット提供サービス |publisher=[[東京都教育委員会]] |accessdate=2016-07-03}} == Related Intypeation == * The landscape stamp of {{ill|Setagaya Noge Post Office|ja|世田谷野毛郵便局|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setagaya Noge Post Office|qq=}} features Noge Ōtsuka Kofun.[https://www.post.japanpost.jp/kitte_hagaki/stamp/fuke/detail.php?id=468 Landscape Stamp: Setagaya Noge Post Office] (Japan Post). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Noge-Otsuka Kofun}} * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/00011732 burial goods of Noge Ōtsuka Kofun (Setagaya City Ownership)] - Historic Site Online (Agency for Cultural Affairs) * [http://www.city.setagaya.lg.jp/kurashi/107/159/778/h26/d00135671.html Experience Ancient Life at Noge Ōtsuka Kofun Festival] - Setagaya City website {{Japanese-history-stub}} of Tokyo]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|野毛大塚古墳|version=104554573|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Tokyo]] [[Category:History of Setagaya]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:[[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] [[Category:Tokyo Designated Historic Sites]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Noge Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[ja:野毛大塚古墳]]

An excavation conducted in 1897 (Meiji 30) revealed a stone coffin, and the burial goods were transferred to the Imperial Museum (now [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]). The mound is equi11ed with a stone covering (fukishi) and haniwa, and a horseshoe-shaped moat surrounds it.{{Cite book|和書|author1=世田谷区教育委員会|authorlink1=世田谷区教育委員会|author2=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「世田谷の三万年」-最近の発掘調査の成果から- |publisher=世田谷区教育委員会・世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1988|page=16}} == Burial Chambers == Four burial chambers are located in the posterior circular mound. The first chamber's split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin measures 8.2 meters in length and contains numerous grave goods, including: * Weapons and armor: short armor, helmets, gorgets, shoulder guards, 19 iron swords, and 25+ arrowheads * Mirrors and ornaments: {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uchimukemonkyō|qq=}}, bronze armlets, over 2,000 beads, and over 30 combs * Stone replicas: including axes and ceremonial tools burial goods from the box-shaped stone coffin include magatama, tubular beads, iron weapons, armor fragments, and a wide variety of stone replicas, such as bowls, plates, and knives made from steatite. == Cultural Significance == The kofun is recognized as an archaeological benchmark for understanding 5th-century burial practices. Detailed findings were compiled by {{ill|Morikazu Gotō|ja|後藤守一|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morikazu Gotō|qq=}} in 1936. == Designations == * Important Cultural Property of Japan (excavations): The burial goods are preserved at the {{ill|Setagaya Local History Museum|ja|世田谷区立郷土資料館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setagaya Local History Museum|qq=}} and the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]. * Tokyo Designated Historic Site: Designated as a Historic Site on February 6, 1975.{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.syougai.metro.tokyo.jp/iseki0/iseki/list/ruins/13112/112ruins.htm |title=世田谷区-遺跡一覧 東京都遺跡地図情報インターネット提供サービス |publisher=[[東京都教育委員会]] |accessdate=2016-07-03}} == Related Intypeation == * The landscape stamp of {{ill|Setagaya Noge Post Office|ja|世田谷野毛郵便局|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setagaya Noge Post Office|qq=}} features Noge Ōtsuka Kofun.[https://www.post.japanpost.jp/kitte_hagaki/stamp/fuke/detail.php?id=468 Landscape Stamp: Setagaya Noge Post Office] (Japan Post). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Noge-Otsuka Kofun}} * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/00011732 burial goods of Noge Ōtsuka Kofun (Setagaya City Ownership)] - Historic Site Online (Agency for Cultural Affairs) * [http://www.city.setagaya.lg.jp/kurashi/107/159/778/h26/d00135671.html Experience Ancient Life at Noge Ōtsuka Kofun Festival] - Setagaya City website {{Japanese-history-stub}} of Tokyo]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|野毛大塚古墳|version=104554573|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Tokyo]] [[Category:History of Setagaya]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:[[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Noge Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[ja:野毛大塚古墳]]

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=}} | name=Noge Ōtsuka Kofun | alternate_name=Higashi Ōtsuka, Kaminoge Kofun, etc. | image=[[File:Noge Otsuka Kofun -01.jpg|260px]]
Noge Ōtsuka Kofun (April 10, 2010) | location=Noge 1-chome, [[:en:Setagaya|Setagaya, Tokyo]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo Metropolis]] | coordinates={{coord|35|36|18.1|N|139|38|35.1|E|}} | part_of={{ill|Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|ja|荏原台古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|qq=}} | type=Scallop-shaped kofun | built=Early 5th century | burial=Main chambers 1–3: split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffins in clay coffins
Chamber 4: box-shaped stone coffin | area=82 m in length; posterior circular mound diameter 67 m; height 11 m | Interred_individual=Unknown | burial goods=Short armor, helmet, gorgets, shoulder guards, iron swords (19), iron arrowheads (25+), iron knives, iron sickles, {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uchimukemonkyō|qq=}}, bronze armlets, 19 stone replicas, over 30 combs, over 2,000 beads, and a quiver or arrow case | Historic Site={{ill|Tokyo Designated Historic Site|ja|東京都指定史跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Designated Historic Site|qq=}}: "Noge Ōtsuka Kofun" | Historic Site=excavations (Important Cultural Property of Japan) | Specialnotes= | map={{location map|Japan Wards of Tokyo|map_dot_label=Noge
Ōtsuka Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=36|lat_sec=18.1|lon_deg=139|lon_min=38|lon_sec=35.1|mark=帆立貝形古墳-南西.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Tokyo}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|36|18.1|N|139|38|35.1|E}}}} }} '''Noge Ōtsuka Kofun''' (野毛大塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Setagaya|Setagaya, Tokyo]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo Metropolis]]. It is scallop-shaped and Historic Site1 as a {{ill|Tokyo Designated Historic Site|ja|東京都指定史跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Designated Historic Site|qq=}}. burial goods excavated from the tumulus are Historic Site1 as Important Cultural Property of Japan. == location == Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is situated in {{ill|Tamagawa Nogemachi Park|ja|玉川野毛町公園|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamagawa Nogemachi Park|qq=}}, within the [[:en:Musashino Plateau|Musashino Plateau]] at an elevation of approximately 30–33 meters.{{Cite book|和書|author=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「城南の遺跡」-世田谷周辺発掘調査最新報告- |publisher=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1993|pages=2-3}} Known since the late Edo period, it was historically referred to as "Higashi Ōtsuka." It has also been called "Kaminoge Kofun" and "Todoroki Golf Links Kofun." The tumulus is part of the {{ill|Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|ja|荏原台古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|qq=}} and represents the region's ancient burial practices. == Overview == At 82 meters in length, with a diameter of 67 meters and a height of 11 meters, Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is one of Japan's largest scallop-shaped kofun (hotategai-shiki kofun). An excavation conducted in 1897 (Meiji 30) revealed a stone coffin, and the burial goods were transferred to the Imperial Museum (now [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]). The mound is equi11ed with a stone covering (fukishi) and haniwa, and a horseshoe-shaped moat surrounds it.{{Cite book|和書|author1=世田谷区教育委員会|authorlink1=世田谷区教育委員会|author2=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「世田谷の三万年」-最近の発掘調査の成果から- |publisher=世田谷区教育委員会・世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1988|page=16}} == Burial Chambers == Four burial chambers are located in the posterior circular mound. The first chamber's split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin measures 8.2 meters in length and contains numerous grave goods, including: * Weapons and armor: short armor, helmets, gorgets, shoulder guards, 19 iron swords, and 25+ arrowheads * Mirrors and ornaments: {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uchimukemonkyō|qq=}}, bronze armlets, over 2,000 beads, and over 30 combs * Stone replicas: including axes and ceremonial tools burial goods from the box-shaped stone coffin include magatama, tubular beads, iron weapons, armor fragments, and a wide variety of stone replicas, such as bowls, plates, and knives made from steatite. == Cultural Significance == The kofun is recognized as an archaeological benchmark for understanding 5th-century burial practices. Detailed findings were compiled by {{ill|Morikazu Gotō|ja|後藤守一|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morikazu Gotō|qq=}} in 1936. == Designations == * Important Cultural Property of Japan (excavations): The burial goods are preserved at the {{ill|Setagaya Local History Museum|ja|世田谷区立郷土資料館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setagaya Local History Museum|qq=}} and the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]. * Tokyo Designated Historic Site: Designated as a Historic Site on February 6, 1975.{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.syougai.metro.tokyo.jp/iseki0/iseki/list/ruins/13112/112ruins.htm |title=世田谷区-遺跡一覧 東京都遺跡地図情報インターネット提供サービス |publisher=[[東京都教育委員会]] |accessdate=2016-07-03}} == Related Intypeation == * The landscape stamp of {{ill|Setagaya Noge Post Office|ja|世田谷野毛郵便局|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setagaya Noge Post Office|qq=}} features Noge Ōtsuka Kofun.[https://www.post.japanpost.jp/kitte_hagaki/stamp/fuke/detail.php?id=468 Landscape Stamp: Setagaya Noge Post Office] (Japan Post). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Noge-Otsuka Kofun}} * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/00011732 burial goods of Noge Ōtsuka Kofun (Setagaya City Ownership)] - Historic Site Online (Agency for Cultural Affairs) * [http://www.city.setagaya.lg.jp/kurashi/107/159/778/h26/d00135671.html Experience Ancient Life at Noge Ōtsuka Kofun Festival] - Setagaya City website {{Japanese-history-stub}} of Tokyo]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|野毛大塚古墳|version=104554573|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Tokyo]] [[Category:History of Setagaya]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:[[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Noge Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[ja:野毛大塚古墳]] [[Category:世田谷区の歴史]]

Ōtsuka Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=36|lat_sec=18.1|lon_deg=139|lon_min=38|lon_sec=35.1|mark=帆立貝形古墳-南西.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Tokyo}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|36|18.1|N|139|38|35.1|E}}}} }} '''Noge Ōtsuka Kofun''' (野毛大塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Setagaya|Setagaya, Tokyo]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo Metropolis]]. It is scallop-shaped and Historic Site1 as a {{ill|Tokyo Designated Historic Site|ja|東京都指定史跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Designated Historic Site|qq=}}. burial goods excavated from the tumulus are Historic Site1 as Important Cultural Property of Japan. == location == Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is situated in {{ill|Tamagawa Nogemachi Park|ja|玉川野毛町公園|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamagawa Nogemachi Park|qq=}}, within the [[:en:Musashino Plateau|Musashino Plateau]] at an elevation of approximately 30–33 meters.{{Cite book|和書|author=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「城南の遺跡」-世田谷周辺発掘調査最新報告- |publisher=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1993|pages=2-3}} Known since the late Edo period, it was historically referred to as "Higashi Ōtsuka." It has also been called "Kaminoge Kofun" and "Todoroki Golf Links Kofun." The tumulus is part of the {{ill|Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|ja|荏原台古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|qq=}} and represents the region's ancient burial practices. == Overview == At 82 meters in length, with a diameter of 67 meters and a height of 11 meters, Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is one of Japan's largest scallop-shaped kofun (hotategai-shiki kofun). An excavation conducted in 1897 (Meiji 30) revealed a stone coffin, and the burial goods were transferred to the Imperial Museum (now [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]). The mound is equi11ed with a stone covering (fukishi) and haniwa, and a horseshoe-shaped moat surrounds it.{{Cite book|和書|author1=世田谷区教育委員会|authorlink1=世田谷区教育委員会|author2=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「世田谷の三万年」-最近の発掘調査の成果から- |publisher=世田谷区教育委員会・世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1988|page=16}} == Burial Chambers == Four burial chambers are located in the posterior circular mound. The first chamber's split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin measures 8.2 meters in length and contains numerous grave goods, including: * Weapons and armor: short armor, helmets, gorgets, shoulder guards, 19 iron swords, and 25+ arrowheads * Mirrors and ornaments: {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uchimukemonkyō|qq=}}, bronze armlets, over 2,000 beads, and over 30 combs * Stone replicas: including axes and ceremonial tools burial goods from the box-shaped stone coffin include magatama, tubular beads, iron weapons, armor fragments, and a wide variety of stone replicas, such as bowls, plates, and knives made from steatite. == Cultural Significance == The kofun is recognized as an archaeological benchmark for understanding 5th-century burial practices. Detailed findings were compiled by {{ill|Morikazu Gotō|ja|後藤守一|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morikazu Gotō|qq=}} in 1936. == Designations == * Important Cultural Property of Japan (excavations): The burial goods are preserved at the {{ill|Setagaya Local History Museum|ja|世田谷区立郷土資料館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setagaya Local History Museum|qq=}} and the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]. * Tokyo Designated Historic Site: Designated as a Historic Site on February 6, 1975.{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.syougai.metro.tokyo.jp/iseki0/iseki/list/ruins/13112/112ruins.htm |title=世田谷区-遺跡一覧 東京都遺跡地図情報インターネット提供サービス |publisher=[[東京都教育委員会]] |accessdate=2016-07-03}} == Related Intypeation == * The landscape stamp of {{ill|Setagaya Noge Post Office|ja|世田谷野毛郵便局|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setagaya Noge Post Office|qq=}} features Noge Ōtsuka Kofun.[https://www.post.japanpost.jp/kitte_hagaki/stamp/fuke/detail.php?id=468 Landscape Stamp: Setagaya Noge Post Office] (Japan Post). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Noge-Otsuka Kofun}} * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/00011732 burial goods of Noge Ōtsuka Kofun (Setagaya City Ownership)] - Historic Site Online (Agency for Cultural Affairs) * [http://www.city.setagaya.lg.jp/kurashi/107/159/778/h26/d00135671.html Experience Ancient Life at Noge Ōtsuka Kofun Festival] - Setagaya City website {{Japanese-history-stub}} of Tokyo]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|野毛大塚古墳|version=104554573|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Tokyo]] [[Category:History of Setagaya]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:[[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Noge Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[ja:野毛大塚古墳]] [[Category:世田谷区の歴史]] [[Category:東京都の古墳]] [[Category:東京都区部の重要文化財]]
