Historic Site Online (Agency for Cultural Affairs) * [http://www.city.setagaya.lg.jp/kurashi/107/159/778/h26/d00135671.html Experience Ancient Life at Noge Ōtsuka Kofun Festival] - Setagaya City website {{Japanese-history-stub}} of Tokyo]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|野毛大塚古墳|version=104554573|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Tokyo]] [[Category:History of Setagaya]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:[[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Noge Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[ja:野毛大塚古墳]] [[Category:世田谷区の歴史]] [[Category:Kofun of Tokyo]] [[Category:東京都区部の重要文化財]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Tokyo]]

{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=}} | name=Noge Ōtsuka Kofun | alternate_name=Higashi Ōtsuka, Kaminoge Kofun, etc. | image=[[File:Noge Otsuka Kofun -01.jpg|260px]]
Noge Ōtsuka Kofun (April 10, 2010) | location=Noge 1-chome, [[:en:Setagaya|Setagaya, Tokyo]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo Metropolis]] | coordinates={{coord|35|36|18.1|N|139|38|35.1|E|}} | part_of={{ill|Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|ja|荏原台古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|qq=}} | type=Scallop-shaped kofun | built=Early 5th century | burial=Main chambers 1–3: split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffins in clay coffins
Chamber 4: box-shaped stone coffin | area=82 m in length; posterior circular mound diameter 67 m; height 11 m | Interred_individual=Unknown | burial goods=Short armor, helmet, gorgets, shoulder guards, iron swords (19), iron arrowheads (25+), iron knives, iron sickles, {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uchimukemonkyō|qq=}}, bronze armlets, 19 stone replicas, over 30 combs, over 2,000 beads, and a quiver or arrow case | Historic Site={{ill|Tokyo Designated Historic Site|ja|東京都指定史跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Designated Historic Site|qq=}}: "Noge Ōtsuka Kofun" | Historic Site=excavations (Important Cultural Property of Japan) | Specialnotes= | map={{location map|Japan Wards of Tokyo|map_dot_label=Noge
Ōtsuka Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=36|lat_sec=18.1|lon_deg=139|lon_min=38|lon_sec=35.1|mark=帆立貝形古墳-南西.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Tokyo}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|36|18.1|N|139|38|35.1|E}}}} }} '''Noge Ōtsuka Kofun''' (野毛大塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Setagaya|Setagaya, Tokyo]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo Metropolis]]. It is scallop-shaped and Historic Site1 as a {{ill|Tokyo Designated Historic Site|ja|東京都指定史跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Designated Historic Site|qq=}}. burial goods excavated from the tumulus are Historic Site1 as Important Cultural Property of Japan. == location == Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is situated in {{ill|Tamagawa Nogemachi Park|ja|玉川野毛町公園|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamagawa Nogemachi Park|qq=}}, within the [[:en:Musashino Plateau|Musashino Plateau]] at an elevation of approximately 30–33 meters.{{Cite book|和書|author=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「城南の遺跡」-世田谷周辺発掘調査最新報告- |publisher=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1993|pages=2-3}} Known since the late Edo period, it was historically referred to as "Higashi Ōtsuka." It has also been called "Kaminoge Kofun" and "Todoroki Golf Links Kofun." The tumulus is part of the {{ill|Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|ja|荏原台古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|qq=}} and represents the region's ancient burial practices. == Overview == At 82 meters in length, with a diameter of 67 meters and a height of 11 meters, Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is one of Japan's largest scallop-shaped kofun (hotategai-shiki kofun). An excavation conducted in 1897 (Meiji 30) revealed a stone coffin, and the burial goods were transferred to the Imperial Museum (now [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]). The mound is equi11ed with a stone covering (fukishi) and haniwa, and a horseshoe-shaped moat surrounds it.{{Cite book|和書|author1=世田谷区教育委員会|authorlink1=世田谷区教育委員会|author2=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「世田谷の三万年」-最近の発掘調査の成果から- |publisher=世田谷区教育委員会・世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1988|page=16}} == Burial Chambers == Four burial chambers are located in the posterior circular mound. The first chamber's split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin measures 8.2 meters in length and contains numerous grave goods, including: * Weapons and armor: short armor, helmets, gorgets, shoulder guards, 19 iron swords, and 25+ arrowheads * Mirrors and ornaments: {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uchimukemonkyō|qq=}}, bronze armlets, over 2,000 beads, and over 30 combs * Stone replicas: including axes and ceremonial tools burial goods from the box-shaped stone coffin include magatama, tubular beads, iron weapons, armor fragments, and a wide variety of stone replicas, such as bowls, plates, and knives made from steatite. == Cultural Significance == The kofun is recognized as an archaeological benchmark for understanding 5th-century burial practices. Detailed findings were compiled by {{ill|Morikazu Gotō|ja|後藤守一|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morikazu Gotō|qq=}} in 1936. == Designations == * Important Cultural Property of Japan (excavations): The burial goods are preserved at the {{ill|Setagaya Local History Museum|ja|世田谷区立郷土資料館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setagaya Local History Museum|qq=}} and the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]. * Tokyo Designated Historic Site: Designated as a Historic Site on February 6, 1975.{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.syougai.metro.tokyo.jp/iseki0/iseki/list/ruins/13112/112ruins.htm |title=世田谷区-遺跡一覧 東京都遺跡地図情報インターネット提供サービス |publisher=[[東京都教育委員会]] |accessdate=2016-07-03}} == Related Intypeation == * The landscape stamp of {{ill|Setagaya Noge Post Office|ja|世田谷野毛郵便局|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setagaya Noge Post Office|qq=}} features Noge Ōtsuka Kofun.[https://www.post.japanpost.jp/kitte_hagaki/stamp/fuke/detail.php?id=468 Landscape Stamp: Setagaya Noge Post Office] (Japan Post). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Noge-Otsuka Kofun}} * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/00011732 burial goods of Noge Ōtsuka Kofun (Setagaya City Ownership)] - Historic Site Online (Agency for Cultural Affairs) * [http://www.city.setagaya.lg.jp/kurashi/107/159/778/h26/d00135671.html Experience Ancient Life at Noge Ōtsuka Kofun Festival] - Setagaya City website {{Japanese-history-stub}} of Tokyo]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|野毛大塚古墳|version=104554573|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Tokyo]] [[Category:History of Setagaya]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:[[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] [[Category:Fixed Kofun templates]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Noge Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[ja:野毛大塚古墳]] [[Category:世田谷区の歴史]] [[Category:Kofun of Tokyo]] [[Category:東京都区部の重要文化財]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Tokyo]] {{Commons category|Noge-Otsuka Kofun}} [[zh:野毛大塚古墳]]

Chamber 4: box-shaped stone coffin | area=82 m in length; posterior circular mound diameter 67 m; height 11 m | Interred_individual=Unknown | burial goods=Short armor, helmet, gorgets, shoulder guards, iron swords (19), iron arrowheads (25+), iron knives, iron sickles, {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uchimukemonkyō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, bronze armlets, 19 stone replicas, over 30 combs, over 2,000 beads, and a quiver or arrow case | Historic Site={{ill|Tokyo Designated Historic Site|ja|東京都指定史跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Designated Historic Site|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: "Noge Ōtsuka Kofun" | Historic Site=excavations (Important Cultural Property of Japan) | Specialnotes= | map={{location map|Japan Wards of Tokyo|map_dot_label=Noge
Ōtsuka Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=36|lat_sec=18.1|lon_deg=139|lon_min=38|lon_sec=35.1|mark=帆立貝形古墳-南西.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Tokyo}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|36|18.1|N|139|38|35.1|E}}}} }} '''Noge Ōtsuka Kofun''' (野毛大塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Setagaya|Setagaya, Tokyo]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo Metropolis]]. It is scallop-shaped and Historic Site1 as a {{ill|Tokyo Designated Historic Site|ja|東京都指定史跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Designated Historic Site|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. burial goods excavated from the tumulus are Historic Site1 as Important Cultural Property of Japan. == location == Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is situated in {{ill|Tamagawa Nogemachi Park|ja|玉川野毛町公園|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamagawa Nogemachi Park|qq=}}, within the [[:en:Musashino Plateau|Musashino Plateau]] at an elevation of approximately 30–33 meters.{{Cite book|和書|author=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「城南の遺跡」-世田谷周辺発掘調査最新報告- |publisher=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1993|pages=2-3}} Known since the late Edo period, it was historically referred to as "Higashi Ōtsuka." It has also been called "Kaminoge Kofun" and "Todoroki Golf Links Kofun." The tumulus is part of the {{ill|Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|ja|荏原台古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|qq=}} and represents the region's ancient burial practices. == Overview == At 82 meters in length, with a diameter of 67 meters and a height of 11 meters, Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is one of Japan's largest scallop-shaped kofun (hotategai-shiki kofun). An excavation conducted in 1897 (Meiji 30) revealed a stone coffin, and the burial goods were transferred to the Imperial Museum (now [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]). The mound is equi11ed with a stone covering (fukishi) and haniwa, and a horseshoe-shaped moat surrounds it.{{Cite book|和書|author1=世田谷区教育委員会|authorlink1=世田谷区教育委員会|author2=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「世田谷の三万年」-最近の発掘調査の成果から- |publisher=世田谷区教育委員会・世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1988|page=16}} == Burial Chambers == Four burial chambers are located in the posterior circular mound. The first chamber's split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin measures 8.2 meters in length and contains numerous grave goods, including: * Weapons and armor: short armor, helmets, gorgets, shoulder guards, 19 iron swords, and 25+ arrowheads * Mirrors and ornaments: {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uchimukemonkyō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, bronze armlets, over 2,000 beads, and over 30 combs * Stone replicas: including axes and ceremonial tools burial goods from the box-shaped stone coffin include magatama, tubular beads, iron weapons, armor fragments, and a wide variety of stone replicas, such as bowls, plates, and knives made from steatite. == Cultural Significance == The kofun is recognized as an archaeological benchmark for understanding 5th-century burial practices. Detailed findings were compiled by {{ill|Morikazu Gotō|ja|後藤守一|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morikazu Gotō|qq=}} in 1936. == Designations == * Important Cultural Property of Japan (excavations): The burial goods are preserved at the {{ill|Setagaya Local History Museum|ja|世田谷区立郷土資料館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setagaya Local History Museum|qq=}} and the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]. * Tokyo Designated Historic Site: Designated as a Historic Site on February 6, 1975.{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.syougai.metro.tokyo.jp/iseki0/iseki/list/ruins/13112/112ruins.htm |title=世田谷区-遺跡一覧 東京都遺跡地図情報インターネット提供サービス |publisher=[[東京都教育委員会]] |accessdate=2016-07-03}} == Related Intypeation == * The landscape stamp of {{ill|Setagaya Noge Post Office|ja|世田谷野毛郵便局|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setagaya Noge Post Office|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} features Noge Ōtsuka Kofun.[https://www.post.japanpost.jp/kitte_hagaki/stamp/fuke/detail.php?id=468 Landscape Stamp: Setagaya Noge Post Office] (Japan Post). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Noge-Otsuka Kofun}} * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/00011732 burial goods of Noge Ōtsuka Kofun (Setagaya City Ownership)] - Historic Site Online (Agency for Cultural Affairs) * [http://www.city.setagaya.lg.jp/kurashi/107/159/778/h26/d00135671.html Experience Ancient Life at Noge Ōtsuka Kofun Festival] - Setagaya City website {{Japanese-history-stub}} of Tokyo]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|野毛大塚古墳|version=104554573|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun in Tokyo]] [[Category:History of Setagaya]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:[[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Noge Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[ja:野毛大塚古墳]] [[Category:世田谷区の歴史]] [[Category:Kofun of Tokyo]] [[Category:東京都区部の重要文化財]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Tokyo]] {{Commons category|Noge-Otsuka Kofun}} [[zh:野毛大塚古墳]]

Historic Site Online (Agency for Cultural Affairs) * [http://www.city.setagaya.lg.jp/kurashi/107/159/778/h26/d00135671.html Experience Ancient Life at Noge Ōtsuka Kofun Festival] - Setagaya City website {{Japanese-history-stub}} of Tokyo]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|野毛大塚古墳|version=104554573|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:[[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Noge Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[ja:野毛大塚古墳]] [[Category:世田谷区の歴史]] [[Category:Kofun of Tokyo]] [[Category:東京都区部の重要文化財]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Tokyo]] {{Commons category|Noge-Otsuka Kofun}} [[zh:野毛大塚古墳]]

revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name=Noge Ōtsuka Kofun | alternate_name=Higashi Ōtsuka, Kaminoge Kofun, etc. | image=[[File:Noge Otsuka Kofun -01.jpg|260px]]
Noge Ōtsuka Kofun (April 10, 2010) | location=Noge 1-chome, [[:en:Setagaya|Setagaya, Tokyo]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo Metropolis]] | coordinates={{coord|35|36|18.1|N|139|38|35.1|E|}} | part_of={{ill|Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|ja|荏原台古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|qq=}} | type=Scallop-shaped kofun | built=Early 5th century | burial=Main chambers 1–3: split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffins in clay coffins
Chamber 4: box-shaped stone coffin | area=82 m in length; posterior circular mound diameter 67 m; height 11 m | Interred_individual=Unknown | burial goods=Short armor, helmet, gorgets, shoulder guards, iron swords (19), iron arrowheads (25+), iron knives, iron sickles, {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uchimukemonkyō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, bronze armlets, 19 stone replicas, over 30 combs, over 2,000 beads, and a quiver or arrow case | Historic Site={{ill|Tokyo Designated Historic Site|ja|東京都指定史跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Designated Historic Site|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: "Noge Ōtsuka Kofun" | Historic Site=excavations (Important Cultural Property of Japan) | Specialnotes= | map={{location map|Japan Wards of Tokyo|map_dot_label=Noge
Ōtsuka Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=36|lat_sec=18.1|lon_deg=139|lon_min=38|lon_sec=35.1|mark=帆立貝形古墳-南西.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Tokyo}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|36|18.1|N|139|38|35.1|E}}}} }} '''Noge Ōtsuka Kofun''' (野毛大塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Setagaya|Setagaya, Tokyo]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo Metropolis]]. It is scallop-shaped and Historic Site1 as a {{ill|Tokyo Designated Historic Site|ja|東京都指定史跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Designated Historic Site|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. burial goods excavated from the tumulus are Historic Site1 as Important Cultural Property of Japan. == location == Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is situated in {{ill|Tamagawa Nogemachi Park|ja|玉川野毛町公園|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamagawa Nogemachi Park|qq=}}, within the [[:en:Musashino Plateau|Musashino Plateau]] at an elevation of approximately 30–33 meters.{{Cite book|和書|author=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「城南の遺跡」-世田谷周辺発掘調査最新報告- |publisher=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1993|pages=2-3}} Known since the late Edo period, it was historically referred to as "Higashi Ōtsuka." It has also been called "Kaminoge Kofun" and "Todoroki Golf Links Kofun." The tumulus is part of the {{ill|Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|ja|荏原台古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|qq=}} and represents the region's ancient burial practices. == Overview == At 82 meters in length, with a diameter of 67 meters and a height of 11 meters, Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is one of Japan's largest scallop-shaped kofun (hotategai-shiki kofun). An excavation conducted in 1897 (Meiji 30) revealed a stone coffin, and the burial goods were transferred to the Imperial Museum (now [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]). The mound is equi11ed with a stone covering (fukishi) and haniwa, and a horseshoe-shaped moat surrounds it.{{Cite book|和書|author1=世田谷区教育委員会|authorlink1=世田谷区教育委員会|author2=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「世田谷の三万年」-最近の発掘調査の成果から- |publisher=世田谷区教育委員会・世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1988|page=16}} == Burial Chambers == Four burial chambers are located in the posterior circular mound. The first chamber's split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin measures 8.2 meters in length and contains numerous grave goods, including: * Weapons and armor: short armor, helmets, gorgets, shoulder guards, 19 iron swords, and 25+ arrowheads * Mirrors and ornaments: {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uchimukemonkyō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, bronze armlets, over 2,000 beads, and over 30 combs * Stone replicas: including axes and ceremonial tools burial goods from the box-shaped stone coffin include magatama, tubular beads, iron weapons, armor fragments, and a wide variety of stone replicas, such as bowls, plates, and knives made from steatite. == Cultural Significance == The kofun is recognized as an archaeological benchmark for understanding 5th-century burial practices. Detailed findings were compiled by {{ill|Morikazu Gotō|ja|後藤守一|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morikazu Gotō|qq=}} in 1936. == Designations == * Important Cultural Property of Japan (excavations): The burial goods are preserved at the {{ill|Setagaya Local History Museum|ja|世田谷区立郷土資料館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setagaya Local History Museum|qq=}} and the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]. * Tokyo Designated Historic Site: Designated as a Historic Site on February 6, 1975.{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.syougai.metro.tokyo.jp/iseki0/iseki/list/ruins/13112/112ruins.htm |title=世田谷区-遺跡一覧 東京都遺跡地図情報インターネット提供サービス |publisher=[[東京都教育委員会]] |accessdate=2016-07-03}} == Related Intypeation == * The landscape stamp of {{ill|Setagaya Noge Post Office|ja|世田谷野毛郵便局|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setagaya Noge Post Office|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} features Noge Ōtsuka Kofun.[https://www.post.japanpost.jp/kitte_hagaki/stamp/fuke/detail.php?id=468 Landscape Stamp: Setagaya Noge Post Office] (Japan Post). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Noge-Otsuka Kofun}} * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/00011732 burial goods of Noge Ōtsuka Kofun (Setagaya City Ownership)] - Historic Site Online (Agency for Cultural Affairs) * [http://www.city.setagaya.lg.jp/kurashi/107/159/778/h26/d00135671.html Experience Ancient Life at Noge Ōtsuka Kofun Festival] - Setagaya City website {{Japanese-history-stub}} of Tokyo]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|野毛大塚古墳|version=104554573|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:[[Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] [[Category:Marked infobox kofun with unknown parameters]] }} [[sn:Noge Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[ja:野毛大塚古墳]] [[Category:世田谷区の歴史]] [[Category:Kofun of Tokyo]] [[Category:東京都区部の重要文化財]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Tokyo]] {{Commons category|Noge-Otsuka Kofun}} [[zh:野毛大塚古墳]]

Ōtsuka Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=36|lat_sec=18.1|lon_deg=139|lon_min=38|lon_sec=35.1|mark=帆立貝形古墳-南西.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Tokyo}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|36|18.1|N|139|38|35.1|E}}}} }} '''Noge Ōtsuka Kofun''' (野毛大塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Setagaya|Setagaya, Tokyo]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo Metropolis]]. It is scallop-shaped and Historic Site1 as a {{ill|Tokyo Designated Historic Site|ja|東京都指定史跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Designated Historic Site|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. burial goods excavated from the tumulus are Historic Site1 as Important Cultural Property of Japan. == location == Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is situated in {{ill|Tamagawa Nogemachi Park|ja|玉川野毛町公園|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamagawa Nogemachi Park|qq=}}, within the [[:en:Musashino Plateau|Musashino Plateau]] at an elevation of approximately 30–33 meters.{{Cite book|和書|author=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「城南の遺跡」-世田谷周辺発掘調査最新報告- |publisher=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1993|pages=2-3}} Known since the late Edo period, it was historically referred to as "Higashi Ōtsuka." It has also been called "Kaminoge Kofun" and "Todoroki Golf Links Kofun." The tumulus is part of the {{ill|Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|ja|荏原台古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|qq=}} and represents the region's ancient burial practices. == Overview == At 82 meters in length, with a diameter of 67 meters and a height of 11 meters, Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is one of Japan's largest scallop-shaped kofun (hotategai-shiki kofun). An excavation conducted in 1897 (Meiji 30) revealed a stone coffin, and the burial goods were transferred to the Imperial Museum (now [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]). The mound is equi11ed with a stone covering (fukishi) and haniwa, and a horseshoe-shaped moat surrounds it.{{Cite book|和書|author1=世田谷区教育委員会|authorlink1=世田谷区教育委員会|author2=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「世田谷の三万年」-最近の発掘調査の成果から- |publisher=世田谷区教育委員会・世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1988|page=16}} == Burial Chambers == Four burial chambers are located in the posterior circular mound. The first chamber's split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin measures 8.2 meters in length and contains numerous grave goods, including: * Weapons and armor: short armor, helmets, gorgets, shoulder guards, 19 iron swords, and 25+ arrowheads * Mirrors and ornaments: {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uchimukemonkyō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, bronze armlets, over 2,000 beads, and over 30 combs * Stone replicas: including axes and ceremonial tools burial goods from the box-shaped stone coffin include magatama, tubular beads, iron weapons, armor fragments, and a wide variety of stone replicas, such as bowls, plates, and knives made from steatite. == Cultural Significance == The kofun is recognized as an archaeological benchmark for understanding 5th-century burial practices. Detailed findings were compiled by {{ill|Morikazu Gotō|ja|後藤守一|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morikazu Gotō|qq=}} in 1936. == Designations == * Important Cultural Property of Japan (excavations): The burial goods are preserved at the {{ill|Setagaya Local History Museum|ja|世田谷区立郷土資料館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setagaya Local History Museum|qq=}} and the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]. * Tokyo Designated Historic Site: Designated as a Historic Site on February 6, 1975.{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.syougai.metro.tokyo.jp/iseki0/iseki/list/ruins/13112/112ruins.htm |title=世田谷区-遺跡一覧 東京都遺跡地図情報インターネット提供サービス |publisher=[[東京都教育委員会]] |accessdate=2016-07-03}} == Related Intypeation == * The landscape stamp of {{ill|Setagaya Noge Post Office|ja|世田谷野毛郵便局|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setagaya Noge Post Office|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} features Noge Ōtsuka Kofun.[https://www.post.japanpost.jp/kitte_hagaki/stamp/fuke/detail.php?id=468 Landscape Stamp: Setagaya Noge Post Office] (Japan Post). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Noge-Otsuka Kofun}} * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/00011732 burial goods of Noge Ōtsuka Kofun (Setagaya City Ownership)] - Historic Site Online (Agency for Cultural Affairs) * [http://www.city.setagaya.lg.jp/kurashi/107/159/778/h26/d00135671.html Experience Ancient Life at Noge Ōtsuka Kofun Festival] - Setagaya City website {{Japanese-history-stub}} of Tokyo]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|野毛大塚古墳|version=104554573|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:[[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Noge Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[ja:野毛大塚古墳]] [[Category:世田谷区の歴史]] [[Category:Kofun of Tokyo]] [[Category:東京都区部の重要文化財]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Tokyo]] {{Commons category|Noge-Otsuka Kofun}} [[zh:野毛大塚古墳]]

revision.comment: Merge [[Category:世田谷区の歴史]] into [[Category:History of Setagaya Ward]] via bot
revision.text:
{{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through [[Special:MassEditRegex]] to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|Scallop Kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Scallop Kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name=Noge Ōtsuka Kofun | alternate_name=Higashi Ōtsuka, Kaminoge Kofun, etc. | image=[[File:Noge Otsuka Kofun -01.jpg|260px]]
Noge Ōtsuka Kofun (April 10, 2010) | location=Noge 1-chome, [[:en:Setagaya|Setagaya, Tokyo]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo Metropolis]] | coordinates={{coord|35|36|18.1|N|139|38|35.1|E|}} | part_of={{ill|Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|ja|荏原台古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|qq=}} | type=Scallop-shaped kofun | built=Early 5th century | burial=Main chambers 1–3: split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffins in clay coffins
Chamber 4: box-shaped stone coffin | area=82 m in length; posterior circular mound diameter 67 m; height 11 m | Interred_individual=Unknown | burial goods=Short armor, helmet, gorgets, shoulder guards, iron swords (19), iron arrowheads (25+), iron knives, iron sickles, {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uchimukemonkyō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, bronze armlets, 19 stone replicas, over 30 combs, over 2,000 beads, and a quiver or arrow case | Historic Site={{ill|Tokyo Designated Historic Site|ja|東京都指定史跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Designated Historic Site|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: "Noge Ōtsuka Kofun" | Historic Site=excavations (Important Cultural Property of Japan) | Specialnotes= | map={{location map|Japan Wards of Tokyo|map_dot_label=Noge
Ōtsuka Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=36|lat_sec=18.1|lon_deg=139|lon_min=38|lon_sec=35.1|mark=帆立貝形古墳-南西.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Tokyo}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|36|18.1|N|139|38|35.1|E}}}} }} '''Noge Ōtsuka Kofun''' (野毛大塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Setagaya|Setagaya, Tokyo]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo Metropolis]]. It is scallop-shaped and Historic Site1 as a {{ill|Tokyo Designated Historic Site|ja|東京都指定史跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Designated Historic Site|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. burial goods excavated from the tumulus are Historic Site1 as Important Cultural Property of Japan. == location == Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is situated in {{ill|Tamagawa Nogemachi Park|ja|玉川野毛町公園|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamagawa Nogemachi Park|qq=}}, within the [[:en:Musashino Plateau|Musashino Plateau]] at an elevation of approximately 30–33 meters.{{Cite book|和書|author=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「城南の遺跡」-世田谷周辺発掘調査最新報告- |publisher=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1993|pages=2-3}} Known since the late Edo period, it was historically referred to as "Higashi Ōtsuka." It has also been called "Kaminoge Kofun" and "Todoroki Golf Links Kofun." The tumulus is part of the {{ill|Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|ja|荏原台古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|qq=}} and represents the region's ancient burial practices. == Overview == At 82 meters in length, with a diameter of 67 meters and a height of 11 meters, Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is one of Japan's largest scallop-shaped kofun (hotategai-shiki kofun). An excavation conducted in 1897 (Meiji 30) revealed a stone coffin, and the burial goods were transferred to the Imperial Museum (now [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]). The mound is equi11ed with a stone covering (fukishi) and haniwa, and a horseshoe-shaped moat surrounds it.{{Cite book|和書|author1=世田谷区教育委員会|authorlink1=世田谷区教育委員会|author2=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「世田谷の三万年」-最近の発掘調査の成果から- |publisher=世田谷区教育委員会・世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1988|page=16}} == Burial Chambers == Four burial chambers are located in the posterior circular mound. The first chamber's split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin measures 8.2 meters in length and contains numerous grave goods, including: * Weapons and armor: short armor, helmets, gorgets, shoulder guards, 19 iron swords, and 25+ arrowheads * Mirrors and ornaments: {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uchimukemonkyō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, bronze armlets, over 2,000 beads, and over 30 combs * Stone replicas: including axes and ceremonial tools burial goods from the box-shaped stone coffin include magatama, tubular beads, iron weapons, armor fragments, and a wide variety of stone replicas, such as bowls, plates, and knives made from steatite. == Cultural Significance == The kofun is recognized as an archaeological benchmark for understanding 5th-century burial practices. Detailed findings were compiled by {{ill|Morikazu Gotō|ja|後藤守一|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morikazu Gotō|qq=}} in 1936. == Designations == * Important Cultural Property of Japan (excavations): The burial goods are preserved at the {{ill|Setagaya Local History Museum|ja|世田谷区立郷土資料館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setagaya Local History Museum|qq=}} and the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]. * Tokyo Designated Historic Site: Designated as a Historic Site on February 6, 1975.{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.syougai.metro.tokyo.jp/iseki0/iseki/list/ruins/13112/112ruins.htm |title=世田谷区-遺跡一覧 東京都遺跡地図情報インターネット提供サービス |publisher=[[東京都教育委員会]] |accessdate=2016-07-03}} == Related Intypeation == * The landscape stamp of {{ill|Setagaya Noge Post Office|ja|世田谷野毛郵便局|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setagaya Noge Post Office|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} features Noge Ōtsuka Kofun.[https://www.post.japanpost.jp/kitte_hagaki/stamp/fuke/detail.php?id=468 Landscape Stamp: Setagaya Noge Post Office] (Japan Post). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Noge-Otsuka Kofun}} * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/00011732 burial goods of Noge Ōtsuka Kofun (Setagaya City Ownership)] - Historic Site Online (Agency for Cultural Affairs) * [http://www.city.setagaya.lg.jp/kurashi/107/159/778/h26/d00135671.html Experience Ancient Life at Noge Ōtsuka Kofun Festival] - Setagaya City website {{Japanese-history-stub}} of Tokyo]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|野毛大塚古墳|version=104554573|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:[[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Noge Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[ja:野毛大塚古墳]] [[Category:History of Setagaya Ward]] [[Category:Kofun of Tokyo]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Tokyo]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Tokyo]] {{Commons category|Noge-Otsuka Kofun}} [[zh:野毛大塚古墳]]

The mound is equi11ed with a stone covering (fukishi) and haniwa, and a horseshoe-shaped moat surrounds it.{{Cite book|和書|author1=世田谷区教育委員会|authorlink1=世田谷区教育委員会|author2=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「世田谷の三万年」-最近の発掘調査の成果から- |publisher=世田谷区教育委員会・世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1988|page=16}} == Burial Chambers == Four burial chambers are located in the posterior circular mound. The first chamber's split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin measures 8.2 meters in length and contains numerous grave goods, including: * Weapons and armor: short armor, helmets, gorgets, shoulder guards, 19 iron swords, and 25+ arrowheads * Mirrors and ornaments: {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uchimukemonkyō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, bronze armlets, over 2,000 beads, and over 30 combs * Stone replicas: including axes and ceremonial tools burial goods from the box-type stone coffin include magatama, tubular beads, iron weapons, armor fragments, and a wide variety of stone replicas, such as bowls, plates, and knives made from steatite. == Cultural Significance == The kofun is recognized as an archaeological benchmark for understanding 5th-century burial practices. Detailed findings were compiled by {{ill|Morikazu Gotō|ja|後藤守一|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morikazu Gotō|qq=}} in 1936. == Designations == * Important Cultural Property of Japan (excavations): The burial goods are preserved at the {{ill|Setagaya Local History Museum|ja|世田谷区立郷土資料館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setagaya Local History Museum|qq=}} and the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]. * Tokyo Designated Historic Site: Designated as a Historic Site on February 6, 1975.{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.syougai.metro.tokyo.jp/iseki0/iseki/list/ruins/13112/112ruins.htm |title=世田谷区-遺跡一覧 東京都遺跡地図情報インターネット提供サービス |publisher=[[東京都教育委員会]] |accessdate=2016-07-03}} == Related Intypeation == * The landscape stamp of {{ill|Setagaya Noge Post Office|ja|世田谷野毛郵便局|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setagaya Noge Post Office|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} features Noge Ōtsuka Kofun.[https://www.post.japanpost.jp/kitte_hagaki/stamp/fuke/detail.php?id=468 Landscape Stamp: Setagaya Noge Post Office] (Japan Post). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Noge-Otsuka Kofun}} * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/00011732 burial goods of Noge Ōtsuka Kofun (Setagaya City Ownership)] - Historic Site Online (Agency for Cultural Affairs) * [http://www.city.setagaya.lg.jp/kurashi/107/159/778/h26/d00135671.html Experience Ancient Life at Noge Ōtsuka Kofun Festival] - Setagaya City website {{Japanese-history-stub}} of Tokyo]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|野毛大塚古墳|version=104554573|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:[[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[sn:Noge Ōtsuka Kofun]] [[ja:野毛大塚古墳]] [[Category:History of Setagaya Ward]] [[Category:Kofun of Tokyo]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Tokyo]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Tokyo]] {{Commons category|Noge-Otsuka Kofun}} [[zh:野毛大塚古墳]]

[[commons:Category:Noge-Otsuka Kofun]][[ja:野毛大塚古墳]][[zh:野毛大塚古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q11645747]] Wikidata name: Noge Ōtsuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|qq=|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name=Noge Ōtsuka Kofun | alternate_name=Higashi Ōtsuka, Kaminoge Kofun, etc. | image=File:Noge-Otsuka Kofun.jpg | location={{ill|Noge|qid=Q11645744}} | coordinates={{Coord|35.605|N|139.643083|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | part_of={{ill|Ebaradai kofun-gun|qid=Q11618305}} | type=Scallop-shaped kofun | built=Early 5th century | burial=Main chambers 1–3: split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffins in clay coffins
Chamber 4: box-type Sarcophagus | area=82 m in length; posterior circular mound diameter 67 m; height 11 m | Interred_individual=Unknown | burial goods=Short armor, helmet, gorgets, shoulder guards, iron swords (19), iron arrowheads (25+), iron knives, iron sickles, {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uchimukemonkyō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, bronze armlets, 19 stone replicas, over 30 combs, over 2,000 beads, and a quiver or arrow case | Historic Site={{ill|Tokyo Designated Historic Site|ja|東京都指定史跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Designated Historic Site|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: "Noge Ōtsuka Kofun" | Historic Site=excavations (Important Cultural Property of Japan) | Specialnotes= | map={{location map|Japan Wards of Tokyo|map_dot_label=Noge
Ōtsuka Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=36|lat_sec=18.1|lon_deg=139|lon_min=38|lon_sec=35.1|mark=帆立貝形古墳-南西.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Tokyo}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|36|18.1|N|139|38|35.1|E}}}} | architectural_styles={{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|qid=Q11480883}} }} '''Noge Ōtsuka Kofun''' (野毛大塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Setagaya|Setagaya, Tokyo]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo Metropolis]]. It is scallop-shaped and Historic Site1 as a {{ill|Tokyo Designated Historic Site|ja|東京都指定史跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Designated Historic Site|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. burial goods excavated from the tumulus are Historic Site1 as Important Cultural Property of Japan. == location == Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is situated in {{ill|Tamagawa Nogemachi Park|ja|玉川野毛町公園|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamagawa Nogemachi Park|qq=|1=Tamagawa Nogemachi Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131912988}}, within the [[:en:Musashino Plateau|Musashino Plateau]] at an elevation of approximately 30–33 meters.{{Cite book|和書|author=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「城南の遺跡」-世田谷周辺発掘調査最新報告- |publisher=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1993|pages=2-3}} Known since the late Edo period, it was historically referred to as "Higashi Ōtsuka." It has also been called "Kaminoge Kofun" and "Todoroki Golf Links Kofun." The tumulus is part of the {{ill|Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|ja|荏原台古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|qq=|1=Ebaradai kofun-gun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11618305}} and represents the region's ancient burial practices. == Overview == At 82 meters in length, with a diameter of 67 meters and a height of 11 meters, Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is one of Japan's largest scallop-shaped kofun (hotategai-shiki kofun). An excavation conducted in 1897 (Meiji 30) revealed a Sarcophagus, and the burial goods were transferred to the Imperial Museum (now [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]). The mound is equi11ed with a stone covering (fukishi) and haniwa, and a horseshoe-shaped moat surrounds it.{{Cite book|和書|author1=世田谷区教育委員会|authorlink1=世田谷区教育委員会|author2=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「世田谷の三万年」-最近の発掘調査の成果から- |publisher=世田谷区教育委員会・世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1988|page=16}} == Burial Chambers == Four burial chambers are located in the posterior circular mound. The first chamber's split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin measures 8.2 meters in length and contains numerous grave goods, including: * Weapons and armor: short armor, helmets, gorgets, shoulder guards, 19 iron swords, and 25+ arrowheads * Mirrors and ornaments: {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uchimukemonkyō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, bronze armlets, over 2,000 beads, and over 30 combs * Stone replicas: including axes and ceremonial tools burial goods from the box-type Sarcophagus include magatama, tubular beads, iron weapons, armor fragments, and a wide variety of stone replicas, such as bowls, plates, and knives made from steatite. == Cultural Significance == The kofun is recognized as an archaeological benchmark for understanding 5th-century burial practices. Detailed findings were compiled by {{ill|Morikazu Gotō|ja|後藤守一|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morikazu Gotō|qq=|1=Shūichi Gotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11488456}} in 1936. == Designations == * Important Cultural Property of Japan (excavations): The burial goods are preserved at the {{ill|Setagaya Local History Museum|ja|世田谷区立郷土資料館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setagaya Local History Museum|qq=|1=Setagaya Local History Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q104708389}} and the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]. * Tokyo Designated Historic Site: Designated as a Historic Site on February 6, 1975.{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.syougai.metro.tokyo.jp/iseki0/iseki/list/ruins/13112/112ruins.htm |title=世田谷区-遺跡一覧 東京都遺跡地図情報インターネット提供サービス |publisher=[[東京都教育委員会]] |accessdate=2016-07-03}} == Related Intypeation == * The landscape stamp of {{ill|Setagaya Noge Post Office|ja|世田谷野毛郵便局|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setagaya Noge Post Office|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} features Noge Ōtsuka Kofun.[https://www.post.japanpost.jp/kitte_hagaki/stamp/fuke/detail.php?id=468 Landscape Stamp: Setagaya Noge Post Office] (Japan Post). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Noge-Otsuka Kofun}} * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/00011732 burial goods of Noge Ōtsuka Kofun (Setagaya City Ownership)] - Historic Site Online (Agency for Cultural Affairs) * [http://www.city.setagaya.lg.jp/kurashi/107/159/778/h26/d00135671.html Experience Ancient Life at Noge Ōtsuka Kofun Festival] - Setagaya City website {{Japanese-history-stub}} of Tokyo]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|野毛大塚古墳|version=104554573|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:[[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:野毛大塚古墳]] [[Category:History of Setagaya Ward]] [[Category:Kofun of Tokyo]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Tokyo]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Tokyo]] {{Commons category|Noge-Otsuka Kofun}} [[zh:野毛大塚古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q11645747}}

Chamber 4: box-type Sarcophagus | area=82 m in length; posterior circular mound diameter 67 m; height 11 m | Interred_individual=Unknown | burial goods=Short armor, helmet, gorgets, shoulder guards, iron swords (19), iron arrowheads (25+), iron knives, iron sickles, {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uchimukemonkyō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, bronze armlets, 19 stone replicas, over 30 combs, over 2,000 beads, and a quiver or arrow case | Historic Site={{ill|Tokyo Designated Historic Site|ja|東京都指定史跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Designated Historic Site|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: "Noge Ōtsuka Kofun" | Historic Site=excavations (Important Cultural Property of Japan) | Specialnotes= | map={{location map|Japan Wards of Tokyo|map_dot_label=Noge
Ōtsuka Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=36|lat_sec=18.1|lon_deg=139|lon_min=38|lon_sec=35.1|mark=帆立貝形古墳-南西.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Tokyo}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|36|18.1|N|139|38|35.1|E}}}} | architectural_styles={{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|WD=Q11480883}} }} '''Noge Ōtsuka Kofun''' (野毛大塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Setagaya|Setagaya, Tokyo]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo Metropolis]]. It is scallop-shaped and Historic Site1 as a {{ill|Tokyo Designated Historic Site|ja|東京都指定史跡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Designated Historic Site|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. burial goods excavated from the tumulus are Historic Site1 as Important Cultural Property of Japan. == location == Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is situated in {{ill|Tamagawa Nogemachi Park|ja|玉川野毛町公園|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamagawa Nogemachi Park|qq=|1=Tamagawa Nogemachi Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131912988}}, within the [[:en:Musashino Plateau|Musashino Plateau]] at an elevation of approximately 30–33 meters.{{Cite book|和書|author=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「城南の遺跡」-世田谷周辺発掘調査最新報告- |publisher=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1993|pages=2-3}} Known since the late Edo period, it was historically referred to as "Higashi Ōtsuka." It has also been called "Kaminoge Kofun" and "Todoroki Golf Links Kofun." The tumulus is part of the {{ill|Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|ja|荏原台古墳群|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|qq=|1=Ebaradai kofun-gun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11618305}} and represents the region's ancient burial practices. == Overview == At 82 meters in length, with a diameter of 67 meters and a height of 11 meters, Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is one of Japan's largest scallop-shaped kofun (hotategai-shiki kofun). An excavation conducted in 1897 (Meiji 30) revealed a Sarcophagus, and the burial goods were transferred to the Imperial Museum (now [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]). The mound is equi11ed with a stone covering (fukishi) and haniwa, and a horseshoe-shaped moat surrounds it.{{Cite book|和書|author1=世田谷区教育委員会|authorlink1=世田谷区教育委員会|author2=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「世田谷の三万年」-最近の発掘調査の成果から- |publisher=世田谷区教育委員会・世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1988|page=16}} == Burial Chambers == Four burial chambers are located in the posterior circular mound. The first chamber's split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin measures 8.2 meters in length and contains numerous grave goods, including: * Weapons and armor: short armor, helmets, gorgets, shoulder guards, 19 iron swords, and 25+ arrowheads * Mirrors and ornaments: {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uchimukemonkyō|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, bronze armlets, over 2,000 beads, and over 30 combs * Stone replicas: including axes and ceremonial tools burial goods from the box-type Sarcophagus include magatama, tubular beads, iron weapons, armor fragments, and a wide variety of stone replicas, such as bowls, plates, and knives made from steatite. == Cultural Significance == The kofun is recognized as an archaeological benchmark for understanding 5th-century burial practices. Detailed findings were compiled by {{ill|Morikazu Gotō|ja|後藤守一|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morikazu Gotō|qq=|1=Shūichi Gotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11488456}} in 1936. == Designations == * Important Cultural Property of Japan (excavations): The burial goods are preserved at the {{ill|Setagaya Local History Museum|ja|世田谷区立郷土資料館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setagaya Local History Museum|qq=|1=Setagaya Local History Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q104708389}} and the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]. * Tokyo Designated Historic Site: Designated as a Historic Site on February 6, 1975.{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.syougai.metro.tokyo.jp/iseki0/iseki/list/ruins/13112/112ruins.htm |title=世田谷区-遺跡一覧 東京都遺跡地図情報インターネット提供サービス |publisher=[[東京都教育委員会]] |accessdate=2016-07-03}} == Related Intypeation == * The landscape stamp of {{ill|Setagaya Noge Post Office|ja|世田谷野毛郵便局|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setagaya Noge Post Office|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} features Noge Ōtsuka Kofun.[https://www.post.japanpost.jp/kitte_hagaki/stamp/fuke/detail.php?id=468 Landscape Stamp: Setagaya Noge Post Office] (Japan Post). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Noge-Otsuka Kofun}} * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/00011732 burial goods of Noge Ōtsuka Kofun (Setagaya City Ownership)] - Historic Site Online (Agency for Cultural Affairs) * [http://www.city.setagaya.lg.jp/kurashi/107/159/778/h26/d00135671.html Experience Ancient Life at Noge Ōtsuka Kofun Festival] - Setagaya City website {{Japanese-history-stub}} of Tokyo]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|野毛大塚古墳|version=104554573|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:[[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:野毛大塚古墳]] [[Category:History of Setagaya Ward]] [[Category:Kofun of Tokyo]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Tokyo]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Tokyo]] {{Commons category|Noge-Otsuka Kofun}} [[zh:野毛大塚古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q11645747}}

Detailed findings were compiled by {{ill|Morikazu Gotō|ja|後藤守一|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morikazu Gotō|1=Shūichi Gotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11488456}} in 1936. == Designations == * Important Cultural Property of Japan (excavations): The burial goods are preserved at the {{ill|Setagaya Local History Museum|ja|世田谷区立郷土資料館|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setagaya Local History Museum|1=Setagaya Local History Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q104708389}} and the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]. * Tokyo Designated Historic Site: Designated as a Historic Site on February 6, 1975.{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.syougai.metro.tokyo.jp/iseki0/iseki/list/ruins/13112/112ruins.htm |title=世田谷区-遺跡一覧 東京都遺跡地図情報インターネット提供サービス |publisher=[[東京都教育委員会]] |accessdate=2016-07-03}} == Related Intypeation == * The landscape stamp of {{ill|Setagaya Noge Post Office|ja|世田谷野毛郵便局|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setagaya Noge Post Office|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} features Noge Ōtsuka Kofun.[https://www.post.japanpost.jp/kitte_hagaki/stamp/fuke/detail.php?id=468 Landscape Stamp: Setagaya Noge Post Office] (Japan Post). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Noge-Otsuka Kofun}} * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/00011732 burial goods of Noge Ōtsuka Kofun (Setagaya City Ownership)] - Historic Site Online (Agency for Cultural Affairs) * [http://www.city.setagaya.lg.jp/kurashi/107/159/778/h26/d00135671.html Experience Ancient Life at Noge Ōtsuka Kofun Festival] - Setagaya City website {{Japanese-history-stub}} of Tokyo]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|野毛大塚古墳|version=104554573|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:[[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:野毛大塚古墳]] [[Category:History of Setagaya Ward]] [[Category:Kofun of Tokyo]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Tokyo]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Tokyo]] {{Commons category|Noge-Otsuka Kofun}} [[zh:野毛大塚古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q11645747}}

[[sn:commons:Category:Noge-Otsuka Kofun]][[ja:野毛大塚古墳]][[zh:野毛大塚古墳]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q11645747]] Wikidata name: Noge Ōtsuka Kofun [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 08:34, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=trimming these off. the parameter works as "burial" so no need to use "Burial Facility"[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 03:35, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=added parameters Interred_individual and Mausoleum is planned to be added but I messed up in template editing[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:40, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=now we have a 'burial' and 'burial goods' parameter. So a lot of these pages might not be marked on our wiki as having unknown parameters. But specifically keeping this comment here in case of import, these parameters should be added to [[Template:infobox ancient site]] rather than deleted from this draft or article[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 02:02, 28 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|1=I hope this is not too annoying but this article is gonna be placed under and there is a decent chance these unknown parameters are ones I want added to the main template like burial chamber structure and burial goods. Please do not use any bots that just remove the unknown parameters without looking at them. This was added through to all pages with the category so apologies if it was just some kind of an error like the image parameter being in Japanese[[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 06:00, 27 March 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox ancient site|type = {{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|ja|帆立貝形古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/scallop-shaped kofun|comment=enwiki is a redirect}} | name=Noge Ōtsuka Kofun | alternate_name=Higashi Ōtsuka, Kaminoge Kofun, etc. | image=File:Noge-Otsuka Kofun.jpg | location={{ill|Noge|WD=Q11645744}} | coordinates={{Coord|35.605|N|139.643083|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | part_of={{ill|Ebaradai kofun-gun|WD=Q11618305}} | type=Scallop-shaped kofun | built=Early 5th century | burial=Main chambers 1–3: split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffins in clay coffins
Chamber 4: box-type Sarcophagus | area=82 m in length; posterior circular mound diameter 67 m; height 11 m | Interred_individual=Unknown | burial goods=Short armor, helmet, gorgets, shoulder guards, iron swords (19), iron arrowheads (25+), iron knives, iron sickles, {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uchimukemonkyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, bronze armlets, 19 stone replicas, over 30 combs, over 2,000 beads, and a quiver or arrow case | Historic Site={{ill|Tokyo Designated Historic Site|ja|東京都指定史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Designated Historic Site|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: "Noge Ōtsuka Kofun" | Historic Site=excavations (Important Cultural Property of Japan) | Specialnotes= | map={{location map|Japan Wards of Tokyo|map_dot_label=Noge
Ōtsuka Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=36|lat_sec=18.1|lon_deg=139|lon_min=38|lon_sec=35.1|mark=帆立貝形古墳-南西.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Tokyo}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|36|18.1|N|139|38|35.1|E}}}} | architectural_styles={{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|WD=Q11480883}} }} '''Noge Ōtsuka Kofun''' (野毛大塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Setagaya|Setagaya, Tokyo]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo Metropolis]]. It is scallop-shaped and Historic Site1 as a {{ill|Tokyo Designated Historic Site|ja|東京都指定史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Designated Historic Site|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. burial goods excavated from the tumulus are Historic Site1 as Important Cultural Property of Japan. == location == Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is situated in {{ill|Tamagawa Nogemachi Park|ja|玉川野毛町公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamagawa Nogemachi Park|1=Tamagawa Nogemachi Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131912988}}, within the [[:en:Musashino Plateau|Musashino Plateau]] at an elevation of approximately 30–33 meters.{{Cite book|和書|author=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「城南の遺跡」-世田谷周辺発掘調査最新報告- |publisher=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1993|pages=2-3}} Known since the late Edo period, it was historically referred to as "Higashi Ōtsuka." It has also been called "Kaminoge Kofun" and "Todoroki Golf Links Kofun." The tumulus is part of the {{ill|Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|ja|荏原台古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|1=Ebaradai kofun-gun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11618305}} and represents the region's ancient burial practices. == Overview == At 82 meters in length, with a diameter of 67 meters and a height of 11 meters, Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is one of Japan's largest scallop-shaped kofun (hotategai-shiki kofun). An excavation conducted in 1897 (Meiji 30) revealed a Sarcophagus, and the burial goods were transferred to the Imperial Museum (now [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]). The mound is equi11ed with a stone covering (fukishi) and haniwa, and a horseshoe-shaped moat surrounds it.{{Cite book|和書|author1=世田谷区教育委員会|authorlink1=世田谷区教育委員会|author2=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「世田谷の三万年」-最近の発掘調査の成果から- |publisher=世田谷区教育委員会・世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1988|page=16}} == Burial Chambers == Four burial chambers are located in the posterior circular mound. The first chamber's split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin measures 8.2 meters in length and contains numerous grave goods, including: * Weapons and armor: short armor, helmets, gorgets, shoulder guards, 19 iron swords, and 25+ arrowheads * Mirrors and ornaments: {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uchimukemonkyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, bronze armlets, over 2,000 beads, and over 30 combs * Stone replicas: including axes and ceremonial tools burial goods from the box-type Sarcophagus include magatama, tubular beads, iron weapons, armor fragments, and a wide variety of stone replicas, such as bowls, plates, and knives made from steatite. == Cultural Significance == The kofun is recognized as an archaeological benchmark for understanding 5th-century burial practices. Detailed findings were compiled by {{ill|Morikazu Gotō|ja|後藤守一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morikazu Gotō|1=Shūichi Gotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11488456}} in 1936. == Designations == * Important Cultural Property of Japan (excavations): The burial goods are preserved at the {{ill|Setagaya Local History Museum|ja|世田谷区立郷土資料館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setagaya Local History Museum|1=Setagaya Local History Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q104708389}} and the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]. * Tokyo Designated Historic Site: Designated as a Historic Site on February 6, 1975.{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.syougai.metro.tokyo.jp/iseki0/iseki/list/ruins/13112/112ruins.htm |title=世田谷区-遺跡一覧 東京都遺跡地図情報インターネット提供サービス |publisher=[[東京都教育委員会]] |accessdate=2016-07-03}} == Related Intypeation == * The landscape stamp of {{ill|Setagaya Noge Post Office|ja|世田谷野毛郵便局|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setagaya Noge Post Office|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} features Noge Ōtsuka Kofun.[https://www.post.japanpost.jp/kitte_hagaki/stamp/fuke/detail.php?id=468 Landscape Stamp: Setagaya Noge Post Office] (Japan Post). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Noge-Otsuka Kofun}} * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/00011732 burial goods of Noge Ōtsuka Kofun (Setagaya City Ownership)] - Historic Site Online (Agency for Cultural Affairs) * [http://www.city.setagaya.lg.jp/kurashi/107/159/778/h26/d00135671.html Experience Ancient Life at Noge Ōtsuka Kofun Festival] - Setagaya City website {{Japanese-history-stub}} of Tokyo]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|野毛大塚古墳|version=104554573|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:[[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:野毛大塚古墳]] [[Category:History of Setagaya Ward]] [[Category:Kofun of Tokyo]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Tokyo]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Tokyo]] {{Commons category|Noge-Otsuka Kofun}} [[zh:野毛大塚古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q11645747}}

Chamber 4: box-type Sarcophagus | area=82 m in length; posterior circular mound diameter 67 m; height 11 m | Interred_individual=Unknown | burial goods=Short armor, helmet, gorgets, shoulder guards, iron swords (19), iron arrowheads (25+), iron knives, iron sickles, {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uchimukemonkyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, bronze armlets, 19 stone replicas, over 30 combs, over 2,000 beads, and a quiver or arrow case | Historic Site={{ill|Tokyo Designated Historic Site|ja|東京都指定史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Designated Historic Site|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}: "Noge Ōtsuka Kofun" | Historic Site=excavations (Important Cultural Property of Japan) | Specialnotes= | map={{location map|Japan Wards of Tokyo|map_dot_label=Noge
Ōtsuka Kofun|lat_deg=35|lat_min=36|lat_sec=18.1|lon_deg=139|lon_min=38|lon_sec=35.1|mark=帆立貝形古墳-南西.svg|marksize=25|float=center|border=none|position=bottom|width=200|caption=location in Tokyo}} {{maplink2|type=point|frame=yes|plain=yes|zoom=12|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|36|18.1|N|139|38|35.1|E}}}} | architectural_styles={{ill|scallop-shaped kofun|WD=Q11480883}} }} '''Noge Ōtsuka Kofun''' (野毛大塚古墳) is a [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] located in [[:en:Setagaya|Setagaya, Tokyo]], [[:en:Tokyo|Tokyo Metropolis]]. It is scallop-shaped and Historic Site1 as a {{ill|Tokyo Designated Historic Site|ja|東京都指定史跡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tokyo Designated Historic Site|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}. burial goods excavated from the tumulus are Historic Site1 as Important Cultural Property of Japan. == location == Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is situated in {{ill|Tamagawa Nogemachi Park|ja|玉川野毛町公園|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tamagawa Nogemachi Park|1=Tamagawa Nogemachi Park|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q131912988}}, within the [[:en:Musashino Plateau|Musashino Plateau]] at an elevation of approximately 30–33 meters.{{Cite book|和書|author=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「城南の遺跡」-世田谷周辺発掘調査最新報告- |publisher=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1993|pages=2-3}} Known since the late Edo period, it was historically referred to as "Higashi Ōtsuka." It has also been called "Kaminoge Kofun" and "Todoroki Golf Links Kofun." The tumulus is part of the {{ill|Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|ja|荏原台古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ebara Plateau Kofun Group|1=Ebaradai kofun-gun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11618305}} and represents the region's ancient burial practices. == Overview == At 82 meters in length, with a diameter of 67 meters and a height of 11 meters, Noge Ōtsuka Kofun is one of Japan's largest scallop-shaped kofun (hotategai-shiki kofun). An excavation conducted in 1897 (Meiji 30) revealed a Sarcophagus, and the burial goods were transferred to the Imperial Museum (now [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]). The mound is equi11ed with a stone covering (fukishi) and haniwa, and a horseshoe-shaped moat surrounds it.{{Cite book|和書|author1=世田谷区教育委員会|authorlink1=世田谷区教育委員会|author2=世田谷区立郷土資料館 |title=企画展「世田谷の三万年」-最近の発掘調査の成果から- |publisher=世田谷区教育委員会・世田谷区立郷土資料館 |year=1988|page=16}} == Burial Chambers == Four burial chambers are located in the posterior circular mound. The first chamber's split-bamboo-shaped wooden coffin measures 8.2 meters in length and contains numerous grave goods, including: * Weapons and armor: short armor, helmets, gorgets, shoulder guards, 19 iron swords, and 25+ arrowheads * Mirrors and ornaments: {{ill|Uchimukemonkyō|ja|内向花文鏡|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Uchimukemonkyō|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, bronze armlets, over 2,000 beads, and over 30 combs * Stone replicas: including axes and ceremonial tools burial goods from the box-type Sarcophagus include magatama, tubular beads, iron weapons, armor fragments, and a wide variety of stone replicas, such as bowls, plates, and knives made from steatite. == Cultural Significance == The kofun is recognized as an archaeological benchmark for understanding 5th-century burial practices. Detailed findings were compiled by {{ill|Morikazu Gotō|ja|後藤守一|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Morikazu Gotō|1=Shūichi Gotō|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11488456}} in 1936. == Designations == * Important Cultural Property of Japan (excavations): The burial goods are preserved at the {{ill|Setagaya Local History Museum|ja|世田谷区立郷土資料館|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setagaya Local History Museum|1=Setagaya Local History Museum|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q104708389}} and the [[:en:Tokyo National Museum|Tokyo National Museum]]. * Tokyo Designated Historic Site: Designated as a Historic Site on February 6, 1975.{{Cite web|和書|date= |url=http://www.syougai.metro.tokyo.jp/iseki0/iseki/list/ruins/13112/112ruins.htm |title=世田谷区-遺跡一覧 東京都遺跡地図情報インターネット提供サービス |publisher=[[東京都教育委員会]] |accessdate=2016-07-03}} == Related Intypeation == * The landscape stamp of {{ill|Setagaya Noge Post Office|ja|世田谷野毛郵便局|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setagaya Noge Post Office|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} features Noge Ōtsuka Kofun.[https://www.post.japanpost.jp/kitte_hagaki/stamp/fuke/detail.php?id=468 Landscape Stamp: Setagaya Noge Post Office] (Japan Post). == References == {{Reflist}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Noge-Otsuka Kofun}} * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/201/00011732 burial goods of Noge Ōtsuka Kofun (Setagaya City Ownership)] - Historic Site Online (Agency for Cultural Affairs) * [http://www.city.setagaya.lg.jp/kurashi/107/159/778/h26/d00135671.html Experience Ancient Life at Noge Ōtsuka Kofun Festival] - Setagaya City website {{Japanese-history-stub}} of Tokyo]] {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|野毛大塚古墳|version=104554573|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Scallop Kofun]] [[Category:[[:en:Important Cultural Property (Japan)|Important Cultural Property]] [[Category:Completion tier 5]] }} [[ja:野毛大塚古墳]] [[Category:History of Setagaya Ward]] [[Category:Kofun of Tokyo]] [[Category:Important Cultural Properties in Tokyo]] [[Category:Designated historic sites of Tokyo]] {{Commons category|Noge-Otsuka Kofun}} [[zh:野毛大塚古墳]] {{wikidata link|Q11645747}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]
